ref: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
text: Dɩ-suomuŋ Wɩa ŋaa wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ tinteeŋ nɛ.

ref: The earth was barren, with no form of life; it was under a roaring ocean covered with darkness. But the Spirit of God was moving over the water. The First Day
text: Dʋnɩa fa bɩ kʋŋkala naga, ʋ fa pɩŋ hoi nɛ. Leriŋ kala fa bire nɛ, ka Wɩa Dɩma zɩzaarɛ lɩɩŋ nyuŋ.

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ pʋlʋmʋŋ chaanɛ,” ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ chaanɛ.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: Wɩa na pʋlʋmʋŋ zɔmɔ, ʋ kɛŋ pʋlʋmʋŋ arɩ birimiŋ kaa pɔrɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ.

ref: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa kɛŋ pʋlʋmʋŋ kaa yɩyɩrɛ wɩɩhɛyɛ aŋ kɛŋ birimiŋ ma yɩyɩrɛ tɩtaŋɩŋ. Wɩa jʋʋ dɩdaanɩŋ, taŋ pʋl chʋchʋɔŋ, chɛ-bumbuŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: God said, “I command a dome to separate the water above it from the water below it.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ lɩɩŋ kpaa balɩa aŋ ka wuoluŋ pɩŋ.”

ref: And that's what happened. God made the dome
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ kpaa, aŋ ka wuoluŋ, dɔŋɔ hɛ nyuŋ, dɔŋɔ ma hɛ bʋbʋɔŋ.

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Wɩa kɛŋ wuol-la sɩ hɛ ba pɛgɛ kaa yɩyɩrɛ wɩa-nyuŋ. Wɩa jʋʋ dɩdaanɩŋ, taŋ pʋl chʋchʋɔŋ, chɛ-lɩamɩŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: God said, “I command the water under the sky to come together in one place, so there will be dry ground.” And that's what happened.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ lɩɩŋ kala sɩ hɛ bʋbʋɔŋ kʋ hɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ aŋ ka tintee-hɩlɩŋ,” ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ ŋaa.

ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa kɛŋ dɩ-hɩlɩɩ-la kaa yɩyɩrɛ tinteeŋ aŋ kɛŋ lɩɩ-la sɩ hɛ bʋbʋɔŋ ma yɩyɩrɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ. Wɩa na dɩ ʋ zɔmɔ.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ kʋŋkala sɩɩ nɛŋ, yaŋ arɩ tɩɩsɩŋ, nyʋ.” Ŋɩɩ sɛnɛ ŋaa.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ yaŋ arɩ tɩɩsɩ-la nyʋ a kɛŋ doho. Ʋ na dɩ ba kala zɔmɔ.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Wɩa jʋʋ dɩdaanɩŋ, taŋ pʋl chʋchʋɔŋ, chɛ-torimuŋ chɛɛŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: God said, “I command lights to appear in the sky and to separate day from night and to show the time for seasons, special days, and years.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bɩra bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ pʋlʋmʋŋ chaanɛ wɩa-nyuŋ a kɛŋ wɩɩhɛyɛ arɩ tɩtaŋɩŋ pɔrɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ, a magɩlɛ tapʋlaa, chɛnsɩŋ arɩ jɩsɩŋ dɩ-suomuŋ,

ref: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
text: dɩ ba chaanɛ wɩa-nyuŋ dɩ tinteeŋ kɛŋ pʋlʋmʋŋ.” Ŋɩɩ sɛnɛ ŋaa.

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa ŋaa pʋlʋŋ balaa balɩa, ba tʋɔŋ kʋbalɩŋ pʋpɔsɛ wɩɩhɛyɛ, ka kʋbie ma pʋpɔsɛ tɩtaŋɩŋ, a ŋaa chɛnwʋlaa ma.

ref: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
text: Wɩa ŋaa pʋlʋŋ bala-la hɛ wɩa-nyuŋ nɛ dɩ ba chɩchaanɛ tinteeŋ,

ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: dɩ ba pʋpɔsɛ wɩɩhɛyɛ arɩ tɩtaŋɩŋ a kɛŋ pʋlʋmʋŋ arɩ birimiŋ pɔrɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa na dɩ ba zɔmɔ.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fourth day. The Fifth Day
text: Wɩa jʋʋ dɩdaanɩŋ, taŋ pʋl chʋchʋɔŋ, chɛ-nɛsɩmɩŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ kɩalɩŋ sɩ jaŋ hɛ lɩɩŋ tʋɔŋ a kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ kɔ, dɩ wɩa-nyuŋ fillee maa zaarɛ.”

ref: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa ŋaa lɩɩ-jʋʋraa arɩ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ tʋɔŋ kɩaa doho doho arɩ fillee kala sɩ hɛ wɩa-nyuŋ. Wɩa na dɩ ba zɔmɔ.

ref: Then he gave the living creatures his blessing—he told the ocean creatures to increase and live everywhere in the ocean and the birds to increase everywhere on earth.
text: Wɩa pɛ ba lɛ aŋ bʋl, “Má pʋŋa su lɩɩŋ kala aŋ ka fillee ma pʋŋa su tinteeŋ.”

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: Wɩa jʋʋ dɩdaanɩŋ, taŋ pʋl chʋchʋɔŋ, chɛ-nɔmʋŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ bagɩlaa arɩ pʋsʋŋ kala arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩɩ tuuri tinteeŋ kɛŋ doho.” Ba kala hɛ nɩmɛ a kɛŋ doho. Ŋɩɩ sɛnɛ ŋaa.

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Wɩa sɩ ŋaa ba kala, ʋ tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ ba nyuŋ.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bɩra bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ la ŋaa nuhuobiine dɩ ʋ nagɛla, a leŋ dɩ ʋ tɩɩ chenfile-la sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ tʋɔŋ arɩ fille-la kala sɩ hɛ wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ pʋsʋ-la kala sɩ hɛ tinteeŋ arɩ tintee-tuuroo kala.”

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa ŋaa nuhuobiinee ba nagʋ, baal arɩ haal.

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Wɩa pɛ ba lɛ aŋ bʋla pɩba, “Má kɛŋ doho a pʋŋa su tinteeŋ, má tɩɩ kʋŋ na-kala sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ arɩ fille-la kala sɩ hɛ wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩɩ tuuri tinteeŋ.”

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋl, “Mɩ joŋ kʋnɛnɛɛ arɩ tɩɩnɛnɛɛ kala pɩma nɛ, ba jaŋ ŋaa ma kʋdiilee.

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: Mɩ joŋ pɩpaarɩŋ arɩ yaŋ a pɩ bagɩlaa, pʋsʋŋ, tinteeŋ tuuroo arɩ fillee kala sɩ hɛ wɩa-nyuŋ. Kɩalɩŋ kala sɩɩ wiesu, kɩaa deemba nɛ jaŋ ŋaa ba kʋdiilee. Ŋɩɩ sɛnɛ ŋaa.”

ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bee na kɩalɩŋ kala ʋ sɩ ŋaa dɩ ba zɔmɔ woruŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ wɩa jʋʋ dɩdaanɩŋ, taŋ pʋl chʋchʋɔŋ, chɛ-dʋmʋŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ ŋaa tinteeŋ arɩ wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩ hɛ ba lɛ dɛrɛ.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa dɛrɛ ʋ tɩntɩŋŋaa aŋ wiesi chɛ-pɛmɩŋ.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Wɩa joŋ ʋ nɩsɩŋ daŋ tapʋl-pɛɩ-la lɛ a leŋ ʋ ŋaa tapʋl-zɔŋ, bɛɛ wɩaa tapʋlɩɩ-la nɛ ʋ tɩŋ ʋ tɩntɩŋŋaa dɛrɛ aŋ wiesi. Adam arɩ Hawa

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ tinteeŋ ŋaanɩŋ naasɩŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ. La TƖƖNA Wɩa fa sɩ ŋaa wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ tinteeŋ,

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: tɩɩsɩŋ, yaŋ arɩ kʋŋkala sɩɩ nyʋ tinteeŋ lɛ fa tuo, bɛɛ wɩaa La TƖƖNA Wɩa fa ha bɩ leŋe duonuŋ nɩɩ. Nʋʋ ma kala fa tuo dɩ ʋ pɛrɛ tintee-la lɛ,

ref: But streams came up from the ground and watered the earth.
text: ama lɩɩŋ bulo lɩɩ tinteeŋ nɛ a su leriŋ kala.

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
text: La TƖƖNA Wɩa lɔgɛ tɩɛrɩŋ nɛ a mɛ nuhuobiine aŋ wiese hɛ ʋ mɩɩ-boiŋ lɛ, ʋ kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ a wei.

ref: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
text: Bʋa-la lɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa keri ŋmaŋkaaŋ nɛ a joŋ baal-la ʋ sɩ mɛ a hɛ nɩmɛ. Lee-la yɩrɩŋ nɛ Ɛdɩŋ, a hɛ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ dɩɩrɩŋ.

ref: The Lord God filled the garden with all kinds of beautiful trees and fruit trees. Two other trees were in the middle of the garden. One of these gave life—the other gave the wisdom to know the difference between right and wrong.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa leŋ tɩɩ-doho kala nyʋ a nɛŋ nɛnɛɛ sɩ zɔmɔ sɩɩŋ lɛ, a zɔŋ arɩ diiniŋ ma. Ŋmaŋkaa-la tʋtʋɔbaanɩŋ nɛ tɩa-la nɛnɛɛ sɩɩ pɩ mɩɩsɩŋ arɩ tɩa-la nɛnɛɛ ma sɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ jɩŋ wʋzɔŋŋɔɔ arɩ wʋbɔŋŋɔɔ bʋa kala chɩŋ.

ref: From Eden a river flowed out to water the garden, then it divided into four rivers.
text: Fuoŋ nɛ fa hɛ Ɛdɩŋ lɛ a pɛllɛ jʋjʋʋ ŋmaŋkaa-la tʋɔŋ, a mʋ kpaa fuonaasɩŋ banɛsɛ.

ref: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
text: Fuo-naasɩ-la dɔŋɔ yɩrɩŋ nɛ Pɩsɔŋ. Ʋ pɛllɛ golli Havɩla tinteeŋ kala nɛ, lee-la sɛlɩmɛ sɩ hɛ.

ref: The second is the Gihon River that winds through Ethiopia.
text: Fuonaa-kel-lɩa-la yɩrɩŋ nɛ Gɩhɔŋ. Ʋ nɛ golli Kus tinteeŋ kala.

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: Fuonaa-kel torimuŋ nɛ Tɩgɩrɩs, ʋ nɛ pɛllɛ to Asiiria tinteeŋ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ. Fuonaa-kel nɛsɩmɩŋ nɛ ŋaa Yufireti.

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: La TƖƖNA Wɩa joŋ baal-la hɛ ŋmaŋkaa-la tʋɔŋ nɛ Ɛdɩŋ tinteeŋ lɛ dɩ ʋ pɛrɛ aŋ bibeŋ ʋ nyuŋ.

ref: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa bʋla pɩ baal-la, “Ŋ jaŋ wuo didii tɩɩ-nɛŋ kala sɩ hɛ ŋmaŋkaa-la tʋɔŋ,

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: ama ʋ bɩ maga dɩ ŋ dii tɩa-la nɛnɩŋ sɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ jɩŋ wʋzɔmʋŋ arɩ wʋbɔmʋŋ bʋa, dɩ ŋ nɛ die, ŋ jaŋ sʋʋ.”

ref: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
text: La TƖƖNA Wɩa sɩ, “Ʋ bɩ zɔŋ dɩ baala dʋndʋŋa hɛ nɩmɛ. Mɩ jaŋ ŋaa nʋʋ sɩ jaŋ wuo pɩpɛ ʋ lɛ.”

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa leŋ Adam pɩŋ doŋ a yuo naaŋ. Ʋ sɩ pɩŋ doŋ ŋɩɩ, Wɩa lɩɩsɛ ʋ sɛmpɛrɩ-biiŋ dɔŋɔ aŋ kɛŋ lee-la tɔ.

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa kɛŋ sɛmpɛrɩ-bii-la ʋ sɩ lɩɩsa Adam lɛ a ŋaa haala a kaa kʋ pʋ.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Adam sɩ, “Mɩ haŋgbeliŋ dɔŋɔ arɩ mɩ nammɩa sɛmɛ dɔŋɔ nɛ ŋ-la, mɩ jaŋ yɩrʋ haala, bɛɛ wɩaa ʋ lɩɩ mɩ lɛ nɛ.”

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ baala yie leŋ ʋ naaŋ arɩ ʋ nyɩmma aŋ ka ʋ dɩ ʋ haala mɛrɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ a ŋaa kʋbala.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Baal-la arɩ ʋ haal-la kala fa kɛŋ yarɩ-biree nɛ, ka hɩɩsɩŋ bɩ ba kɛnɛ.

ref: The snake was sneakier than any of the other wild animals that the Lord God had made. One day it came to the woman and asked, “Did God tell you not to eat fruit from any tree in the garden?”
text: Dɩmɩŋ nɛ fa kɛŋ wʋjɩmɩŋ a kɩɩ pʋsʋ-la kala La TƖƖNA Wɩa sɩ ŋaa. Ʋ pɩɛsɛ haal-la, “Wɩa sɛnɛ sɩ má sɩ́ tɩa-la sɩ hɛ ŋmaŋkaaŋ deeŋ tʋɔŋ nɛnɛɛ dii nɛɛ?”

ref: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Haal-la bʋla pʋ, “Laa dii tɩɩnɛnɛ-la kala nɛ,

ref: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
text: ama ŋmaŋkaa-la tʋtʋɔbaanɩŋ tɩa-la nɛnɛɛ Wɩa sɩ la sɩ́ dii nɛ, la sɩ́ digi ma, dɩ la nɛ dige, la jaŋ sʋʋ.”

ref: “No, you won't!” the snake replied.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ dɩŋ-la bʋla pɩ haal-la, “Ma sɛnɛ bɩ jaŋ sʋʋ.

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: Wɩa jɩma dɩ ma nɛ die, ma sɩaa jaŋ polli dɩ ma nagɛ ʋ-na Wɩa, a jɩŋ wʋzɔŋŋɔɔ arɩ wʋbɔŋŋɔɔ bʋa.”

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ haal-la beŋ a na tɩa-la nɛnɛɛ dɩ ba zɔmɔ sɩɩŋ lɛ, aŋ zɔŋ arɩ diiniŋ ma aŋ bɩra cho dɩ ʋ kɛŋ wʋjɩmɩŋ. Ʋ tɔrɔ dii, aŋ tɔrɔ pɩ ʋ bala ma ʋ dii.

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Ba dii tɩa-la nɛnɩŋ dɛrɛ, ba sɩaa polli, ba na arɩ ba kɛŋ yarɩ-biree. Ba kɛŋ kanchal-pɩpaarɩŋ pɛ dɔŋɔ a kaa tɔ ba yaraa.

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Ʋ dɩdaanɩŋ sɩ yie, ba nɩɩ La TƖƖNA Wɩa naagime ŋmaŋkaa-la tʋɔŋ. Ba mʋ faa tɩɩsɩŋ bʋbʋɔŋ.

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa yɩrɛ baal-la a bʋl, “Nɩɩ nɛ ŋ hɛ?”

ref: The man answered, “I was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Adam bʋl, “Mɩ nɩɩ ŋ naagime nɛ ŋmaŋkaaŋ tʋɔŋ, fawʋllʋŋ kɛmmɛ mɩ faa, bɛɛ wɩaa mɩ kɛŋ yarɩ-biree nɛ.”

ref: “How did you know you were naked?” God asked. “Did you eat any fruit from that tree in the middle of the garden?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa pɩɛsʋ, “Ŋ ŋɩɩ nɛ jɩŋ arɩ ŋ kɛŋ yarɩ-biree nɛ? Ŋ dii tɩa-la nɛnɩŋ mɩ sɩ sɩ ŋ sɩ́ dii nɛɛ?”

ref: “It was the woman you put here with me,” the man said. “She gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Adam bʋl, “Haal-la ŋ sɩ joŋo pɛ mɩ lɛ, ʋ nɛ tɔrɛ tɩɩnɛŋ-la pɩmɛ mɩ dii.”

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa pɩɛsɛ haal-la, “Bɛɛ nɛ ŋ ŋaa ŋ-la?” Ʋ sɩ, “Dɩmɩŋ nɛ nyɩsɛmɛ mɩ dii tɩɩnɛŋ-la.”

ref: So the Lord God said to the snake: “Because of what you have done, you will be the only animal to suffer this curse— For as long as you live, you will crawl on your stomach and eat dirt.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa ŋmɩɛsa hɛ dɩmɩŋ lɛ a bʋl, “A tɩŋ ŋ sɩ ŋaa wɩɩŋ deeŋ, pʋsʋŋ arɩ baga kɩaa kala tʋɔŋ, ŋ nɛ jaŋ tuuri ŋ luoruŋ nyuŋ a dii hagɩla ŋ mɩɩsɩŋ kala lɛ.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Mɩ jaŋ joŋ dʋndɔnnʋŋ hɛ ŋ dɩ haal-la pɛgɛ. Ŋ biiriŋ arɩ ʋ biiriŋ jaŋ ŋaa dʋndɔmɔ. Adam doho nʋʋ jaŋ ŋmoo ŋ nyuŋ yɛrɛ, ka dɩ ŋ ma ta ʋ naatilimiŋ lɛ.”

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ aŋ bɩra bʋla pɩ haal-la ma, “Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ na hɛɛŋ biiriŋ lʋlʋŋ lɛ. Dɩ ŋ nɩɩ lʋl, ŋ hɛɛŋ jaŋ yʋga pɛ. Ama ŋ ha jaŋ hɩhɩɛnɛ ŋ bala ʋ jaŋ kɛŋ doluŋ ŋ nyuŋ.”

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Wɩa bɩra bʋla pɩ Adam ma, “Ŋ sɩ jegile nɩɩ ŋ haala niiŋ a dii tɩɩnɛŋ-la mɩ sɩ sɩ ŋ sɩ́ dii, mɩ kalɩba hɛ tinteeŋ lɛ nɛ a tɩŋ ŋ wɩaa. Ŋ mɩɩsɩŋ kala lɛ ŋ jaŋ tɩma suoli aŋ-na dii.

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: Sɔsʋŋ arɩ yaŋ jaŋ nyʋ tinteeŋ, ŋ jaŋ tɩŋ a suoli aŋ-na wuo na kʋdiilee a dii.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: Ŋ jaŋ kpɩɩnɛ suoluŋ aŋ na kʋdiilee halɩɩ a mʋ sʋʋ. Tɩɛrɩŋ nɛ mɩ kaa mɛŋ, ŋ jaŋ bɩra bɩrɩmɛ tɩɛrɩŋ.”

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
text: Adam hɛ ʋ haala yɩrɩŋ Hawa, ʋ nɛ ŋaa nʋʋkala nahɩma.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: La TƖƖNA Wɩa kɛŋ teniŋ yɛrɛ gɛnnɩŋ a pɩ Adam arɩ ʋ haala Hawa ba kaa tɔ ba hɩɩsɩŋ.

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa bʋl, “Baala deeŋ lɛɛlɛ nagɛ la nɛ. Ʋ jɩŋ wʋzɔmʋŋ bʋa nɛ. Ʋ bɩ maga dɩ ʋ tɔrɛ tɩa-la sɩɩ pɩ mɩɩsɩŋ nɛnɩŋ dii a sɩ́ sʋʋ.”

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa kiri Adam arɩ ʋ haala ta ŋmaŋkaa-la sɩ hɛ Ɛdɩŋ tinteeŋ lɛ, dɩ Adam lɩɩ pɩpɛrɛ tintee-la Wɩa sɩ lɔgɔ mɛʋ lɛ.

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: Ʋ sɩ kiri Adam ta ʋ lɩɩ ŋɩɩ, ʋ leŋ Wɩa tɩndaara sɩ kɛŋ kɩŋkɛnsɩŋ a kori tokobiiŋ sɩɩ chol nyiniŋ a hɛ Ɛdɩŋ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ dɩɩrɩŋ a vivilli, a pɔ nʋʋkala sɩ́ tɩa-la sɩɩ pɩ mɩɩsɩŋ kpagʋ.

ref: Adam and Eve had a son. Then Eve said, “I'll name him Cain because I got him with the help of the Lord.”
text: Adam pɩŋ ʋ haala Hawa ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie, ba hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Keeŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Hawa bʋl, “Mɩ Tɩɩna Wɩa pɛ mɩ lɛ nɛ mɩ lʋl hambolibie.”

ref: Later she had another son and named him Abel. Abel became a sheep farmer, but Cain farmed the land.
text: Ʋ harɩŋ nɛ ʋ bɩra lʋl Keeŋ ŋaana Abɛl. Abɛl waa aa daa piesee aŋ ka Keeŋ ma waa a ŋaa paarʋ.

ref: One day, Cain gave part of his harvest to the Lord,
text: Tapʋlɩɩ kʋbala Keeŋ kɛŋ ʋ baga kʋdiilee dɔnsʋŋ kʋ ŋaa Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ pɩ La TƖƖNA.

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: Abɛl ma kɛŋ ʋ piesee dɔŋɔ sɩ lʋl pie-kee bumbuŋ a kpʋ a joŋ nammɩa-la lere-la sɩ zɔmɔ a ŋaa Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ. La TƖƖNA tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ Abɛl Wɩɩsʋlɩɩ-la nyuŋ,

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: ama ʋ tʋɔŋ bɩ Keeŋ arɩ ʋ kɩaa nyuŋ tɔrɔ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Keeŋ faasa na baanɩŋ a kɛŋ sɩaa chei.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: La TƖƖNA pɩɛsɛ Keeŋ, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ na baanɩŋ a kɛŋ ŋ sɩaa chei?

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
text: Dɩ ŋ nɛ fa ŋaa woruŋ, mɩ fa bɩŋ ka laa? Aŋ ka ŋ sɩ bɩ woruŋ ŋaa, wʋbɔmʋŋ chɩna gbɛrɩŋ nɛ a chɩchɛ dɩ ʋ kɛnɩŋ. Ʋ maga dɩ ŋ yuo diiu nɛ.”

ref: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let's go for a walk.” And when they were out in a field, Cain attacked and killed him.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Keeŋ bʋla pɩ ʋ ŋaana Abɛl, “Leŋ dɩ la mʋ gɩrɩŋ.” Ba sɩ mʋ gɩrɩŋ, Keeŋ gurigi ʋ ŋaana Abɛl a kpʋʋ.

ref: Afterwards the Lord asked Cain, “Where is Abel?” “How should I know?” he answered. “Am I supposed to look after my brother?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA pɩɛsɛ Keeŋ, “Nɩɩ nɛ ŋ ŋaana Abɛl hɛ?” Keeŋ sɩ, “Mɩ bɩ jɩŋ. Mɩ nɛ maga dɩ mɩ pɔ mɩ ŋaanaa?”

ref: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA pɩɛsɛ, “Bɛɛ nɛ ŋ ŋaa ŋ-la? Jegili, ŋ ŋaana chalɩŋ nɛ pɩŋ tinteeŋ a bunsoo pɩmɛ.

ref: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.
text: A lɩɩ jɩnɩŋ mɩ ŋmɩɛsa hɛ ŋ lɛ nɛ, ŋ kiri ŋ tɩtɩa nɛ tintee-la lɛ. A tɩŋ ŋ ŋaana chalɩŋ sɩ jaasa wɩaa, ŋ chei lee-la nɛ.

ref: When you try to farm the land, it won't produce anything for you. From now on, you'll be without a home, and you'll spend the rest of your life wandering from place to place.
text: Dɩ ŋ nɛ pɛrɛ kɩaa, ba bɩra bɩ wasɛ. Ŋ jaŋ ŋaa nɩgɔllɔ baarɩ tinteeŋ deeŋ lɛ.”

ref: “This punishment is too hard!” Cain said.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Keeŋ bʋla pɩ La TƖƖNA Wɩa, “Ŋ dɔgɩsɛmɛ kieli nɛ.

ref: “You're making me leave my home and live far from you. I will have to wander about without a home, and just anyone could kill me.”
text: Jɩnɩŋ ŋ kirimi tintee-la lɛ, a kirimi ŋ sɩpaaŋ mɩ gɔllɔɔ baarɛ, dɩ nʋʋkala nɛ namɛ, ʋ jaŋ wuo kpʋmɛ.”

ref: “No!” the Lord answered. “Anyone who kills you will be punished seven times worse than I am punishing you.” So the Lord put a mark on Cain to warn everyone not to kill him.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋl, “Ŋɩɩ dee, dɩ nʋʋ nɛ lɩɩsɛ ŋ mɩɩsɩŋ, tɩɩna nɩaa balɩpɛ jaŋ sʋʋ ŋɩɩ nyuŋ lɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa hɛ Keeŋ magɩlɩŋ, dɩ nʋʋ nɛ naʋ, dɩ ʋ sɩ́ ʋ kpʋ.

ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Keeŋ sʋʋsa lɩɩ Wɩa sɩpaaŋ a mʋ hɛ Nɔd tinteeŋ, Ɛdɩŋ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ dɩɩrɩŋ.

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Keeŋ pɩŋ ʋ haala ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl Ɛnɔk. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Keeŋ saa tabal a joŋ ʋ nanʋala Ɛnɔk yɩrɩŋ hɛ pɩ taŋ-la.

ref: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.
text: Ɛnɔk ma lʋl hambolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Irad, Irad ma lʋl Mɛhʋjal, Mehujal ma nɛ lʋl Mɛtʋsal, Mɛtʋsal ma lʋl Lamɛk.

ref: Lamech married Adah, then Zillah.
text: Lamɛk jaa haalaa balɩa, Ada arɩ Zila.

ref: Lamech and Adah had two sons, Jabal and Jubal. Their son Jabal was the first to live in tents and raise sheep and goats. Jubal was the first to play harps and flutes.
text: Ʋ naambie yɩrɩŋ nɛ Jubal, ba doho nɩaa nɛ ŋaa nɩalɩŋ sɩɩ ŋmʋʋ ŋmʋlaa aŋ pɛ dudu chenniŋ.

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Zila ma lʋl hambolibie, ʋ yɩrɩŋ nɛ Tubal-Keeŋ. Ʋ sii ŋaa libiru. Ʋ dɩhaala yɩrɩŋ nɛ Naama.

ref: One day, Lamech said to his two wives, “A young man wounded me, and I killed him.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Lamɛk bʋla pɩ ʋ haalaa Ada arɩ Zila, “Má jegili mɩ teeŋ woruŋ. Baal nɛ bolimimi, mɩ kpʋʋ. Nʋʋ ma pɩrɩmɛ, mɩ kpʋʋ.

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Dɩ nʋʋ nɛ kpʋ Keeŋ, dɩ ʋ nɩaa balɩpɛ sʋʋ ŋɩɩ nyuŋ lɛ, ŋɩɩ-na dɩ nʋʋ nɛ kpʋ mɩ-na Lamɛk, ʋ nɩaa mahɩŋ batori arɩ fii arɩ balɩpɛ jaŋ sʋʋ.”

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Adam bɩra pɩŋ ʋ haala Hawa ʋ lʋl hambolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Sɛf. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Hawa bʋl, “Wɩa bɩra joŋ baal nɛ hɛ Abɛl nyuŋ, Keeŋ sɩ kpʋ nɩɩ-la.”

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Sɛf ma sii lʋl hambolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Ɛnɔs. Bʋa-la lɛ nɛ nɩaa suomo yɩrɛ La TƖƖNA yɩrɩŋ.

ref: God created men and women to be like himself. He gave them his blessing and called them human beings. The following is a list of the descendants of Adam, the first man:
text: Ʋ ŋaa baal arɩ haal nɛ a pɛ ba lɛ. Bʋa-la lɛ ʋ sɩ ŋaaba nɛ ʋ yɩrɛba “nuhuobiinee.”

ref: and God took him away at the age of
text: Ɛnɔk to Wɩa wombiiŋ ŋɩɩ nɛ halɩɩ a mʋ tuo. Wɩa nɛ joŋu viiri.

ref: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text: Ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Nowa aŋ bʋl, “Wɩa fa ŋmɩɛsa hɛ tinteeŋ lɛ nɛ dɩ la tɩma suoli aŋ-na dii. Bie deeŋ nɛ jaŋ leŋ dɩ la tʋɔŋ pɩŋ dɩ-bala.”

ref: Then the Lord said, “I won't let my life-giving breath remain in anyone forever. No one will live for more than
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋl, “Mɩ bɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ mɩ Dɩma-la hɛ nuhuobiine lɛ deeŋ deeŋ. Ʋ yaraa wʋŋaalaa nɛ ʋ tuto. Ʋ jɩsɩŋ jaŋ ŋaa zɔlɔ arɩ mara.”

ref: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.
text: Bʋa-la lɛ nɩwaayiilee fa hɛ dʋnɩa lɛ arɩ ŋɩɩ harɩŋ. Wɩa bii-baalaa kɛŋ nuhuobiinee toloo lʋl biiriŋ. Ba nɛ fa ŋaa nyuŋ tɩŋŋaa a kɛŋ yɩrɩŋ.

ref: The Lord saw how bad the people on earth were and that everything they thought and planned was evil.
text: La TƖƖNA na ŋɩɩ nuhuobiine tʋɔŋ sɩ hɛyɛ dʋnɩa tʋɔŋ, ʋ tʋɔbɩɩnaa ma kala lɔrɛ bʋa na-kala.

ref: and he said, “I'm going to destroy every person on earth! I'll even wipe out animals, birds, and reptiles. I'm sorry I ever made them.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋl, “Mɩ jaŋ chei kʋŋkala sɩ hɛ dʋnɩa lɛ a kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ, nuhuobiinee, pʋsʋŋ arɩ baga kɩaa, kɩalɩŋ sɩɩ tuuri tinteeŋ arɩ zaaraa, mɩ tʋɔŋ cheye nɛ mɩ sɩ ŋaaba.”

ref: But the Lord was pleased with Noah,
text: Ama Wɩa tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ Nowa-na nyuŋ nɛ.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Ŋ-la nɛ Nowa fa nagɛ: Ʋ fa ŋaa tʋɔpʋl tɩɩna nɛ Wɩa teeŋ a bɩ wɩɩkala ma cheye ʋ nɩaa tʋɔŋ. Ʋ to Wɩa wombiiŋ nɛ woruŋ.

ref: He had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text: Nowa fa lʋl hambolibiisiŋ batori nɛ, ba yɩraa nɛ Sɛm, Ham arɩ Jafɛf.

ref: God knew that everyone was terribly cruel and violent.
text: Ʋ na dɩ dʋnɩa cheye nɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa nʋʋkala ka ŋaa wʋbɔŋŋɔɔ nɛ.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Ŋɩɩ wɩaa kɛŋ kanchal daaŋ kpaasɛ lɩɩŋ daboro dɩ ʋ ŋaa dɩa a kɛŋ tʋɔŋ kpaa dɩɩsɩŋ a kɛŋ keliŋ taarɛ ʋ tʋɔŋ arɩ harɩŋ kala dɩ lɩɩŋ sɩ́ jʋʋ.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa ŋaa wɩalɩŋ kala Wɩa sɩ sɩ ʋ ŋaa.

ref: I'm going to send a flood that will destroy everything that breathes! Nothing will be left alive.
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ lɩɩ-balɩŋ kʋ tɔ tinteeŋ kala, a kpʋ kʋŋkala sɩ kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ. Kʋŋ na-kala sɩ hɛ dʋnɩa lɛ jaŋ sʋʋ.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ama mɩ jaŋ bil niiŋ mɩ dɩŋ pɛgɛ. Kɛŋ ŋ haala, ŋ biiriŋ, arɩ ŋ bii-haalaa, dɩ ma kala jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Kɛŋ filli na-kala doho balɩa a kɛŋ pʋsʋŋ arɩ baga kɩaa doho-na kala balɩa arɩ tinteeŋ tuuroo ma doho kala balɩa jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la dɩ ba kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Kɛŋ kʋdiil na-kala doho kaa jʋʋ dɩ ba ŋaa ma kala kʋdiilee.”

ref: The Lord told Noah: Take your whole family with you into the boat, because you are the only one on this earth who pleases me.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋla pɩ Nowa, “Ŋ dɩ ŋ dɩa nɩaa kala jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la, bɛɛ wɩaa ma lɛɛlɛ nɩaa kala tʋɔŋ ŋ dʋndʋŋa nɛ mɩ na dɩ ŋ ŋaa tʋɔpʋl tɩɩna.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: To kɛŋ pʋsʋ-la ma sɩ bɩ kɩsɛ belliŋ arɩ nɩɩnɩŋ balɩpɛ balɩpɛ aŋ kɛŋ pʋsʋ-la ma sɩɩ kɩsɛ balɩa-balɩa dɩ ba doho sɩ́ dɛrʋ

ref: Also take seven pairs of every kind of bird with you. Do this so there will always be animals and birds on the earth.
text: aŋ bɩra kɛŋ fillee arɩ zaaraa, belliŋ arɩ nɩɩnɩŋ balɩpɛ balɩpɛ dɩ ba doho sɩ́ dɛrʋ.

ref: He obeyed God and took a male and a female of each kind of animal and bird into the boat with him.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ kʋŋ na-kala doho, bele arɩ nɩɩŋ to Nowa harɩŋ a jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la arɩ ŋɩɩ Wɩa sɩ bʋla pɩ Nowa.

ref: Seven days later a flood began to cover the earth.
text: Tapʋlaa balɩpɛ harɩŋ, lɩɩŋ kʋ su dʋnɩa.

ref: The water under the earth started gushing out everywhere, the sky opened like windows, and rain poured down for
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ duonuŋ nɩɩ ŋɩɩ halɩɩ tapʋlaa mahɩŋ balɩa, wɩɩhɛyɛ arɩ tɩtaŋɩŋ kala.

ref: On that day Noah and his wife went into the boat with their three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and their wives.
text: Tapʋlɩɩ-la tɩtɩa, Nowa arɩ ʋ biiriŋ Sɛm, Ham arɩ Jafɛf arɩ ʋ haala arɩ ʋ bii-haalaa batori kala jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la.

ref: They took along every kind of animal, tame and wild, including the birds.
text: Ba to kɛŋ bagɩlaa arɩ pʋŋ-doho na-kala arɩ tinteeŋ tuuroo arɩ fillee arɩ zaaraa kala.

ref: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ kʋŋ na-kala doho sɩ kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ, ba balɩa balɩa to Nowa harɩŋ a jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la.

ref: just as God had told him to do. And when they were all in the boat, the Lord closed the door.
text: Pʋsʋ-la kala sɩ to jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la ŋaa belliŋ arɩ nɩɩnɩŋ nɛ, arɩ ŋɩɩ Wɩa sɩ bʋla pɩ Nowa. Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA kɛŋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la boiŋ tɔ.

ref: Finally, the mighty flood was so deep that even the highest mountain peaks were about seven meters below the surface of the water.
text: Lɩɩ-la sɩ su tinteeŋ ŋɩɩ a tɔ peelee nyuŋ, ʋ ha kieli peele-la nyuŋ a yi naakieliŋ bʋlɩpɛ nɛ.

ref: Not a bird, animal, reptile, or human was left alive anywhere on earth.
text: Kʋŋ na-kala sɩ kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ a hɛ dʋnɩa lɛ sʋʋ: Bagɩlaa, pʋsʋŋ, diibiisiŋ arɩ fillee arɩ nuhuobiine kala.

ref: The Lord destroyed everything that breathed. Nothing was left alive except Noah and the others in the boat.
text: Lɩɩŋ kpʋ nuhuobiinee, bagɩlaa, pʋsʋŋ, arɩ tinteeŋ tuuroo arɩ fillee kala, see Nowa, ʋ dɩa nɩaa arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala fa sɩ jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la dʋndʋŋa nɛ fa ka.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Lɩɩ-la fa tɔ tinteeŋ a yi tapʋlaa zɔlɔ arɩ mahɩŋ balɩa arɩ fii nɛ.

ref: God did not forget about Noah and the animals with him in the boat. So God made a wind blow, and the water started going down.
text: Ama Wɩa fa bɩ Nowa arɩ bagɩlaa arɩ pʋsʋ-la sɩ to Nowa harɩŋ jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la wɩaa yʋa. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ leŋ peliŋ lu dʋnɩa lɛ. Lɩɩ-la suomo hʋhʋ.

ref: God stopped up the places where the water had been gushing out from under the earth. He also closed up the sky, and the rain stopped.
text: Lɩɩ-bul-sɩaa sɩ hɛ tinteeŋ lɛ kala tɔ, lɩɩ-la bɩra bɩ pʋŋaa pɛ, wɩa-nyuŋ viisiŋ kala tɔ.

ref: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
text: Ʋ chɛŋ-pɛmɩŋ tapʋlaa fii arɩ balɩpɛ sɩ kiele, lɩɩŋ daboro-la tuu sagɛ peel-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Ararat nyuŋ.

ref: The water kept going down, and the mountain tops could be seen on the first day of the tenth month.
text: Lɩɩ-la tuu mʋa mʋa a mʋ yi chɛŋ-fii tʋɔŋ tapʋl-bumbuŋ dɩ peelee nyusuŋ nyɩɩsa.

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
text: aŋ lɩɩsɛ zaarɩ-kokobie ta ʋ fil a mʋ aŋ kʋkɔ ŋɩɩ halɩɩ lɩɩ-la kala hʋ dɛrɛ.

ref: Noah wanted to find out if the water had gone down, so he sent out a dove.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa bɩra chɩchɛ dɩ ʋ na lɩɩ-la hʋa nɛɛ, ʋ ta kokomo dɩ ʋ lɩɩ na.

ref: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.
text: Kokomo-la sɩ lɩa, ʋ bɩ wuo tuu tinteeŋ, bɛɛ wɩaa lɩɩŋ ha hɛ tinteeŋ kala lɛ nɛ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ kokomo-la mɩɩra kɔ lɩɩŋ daboro-la leriŋ. Nowa yɩa kɛŋ kokomo-la arɩ nɩsɩŋ a kaa jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la.

ref: Seven days later Noah sent the dove out again.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa gbɛrɛ tapʋlaa balɩpɛ aŋ bɩra lɩɩsɛ kokomo-la ta ʋ lɩɩ.

ref: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
text: Kokomo-la mɩɩra chɛbɛ olif pɩpaa-laaŋ ʋ niiŋ lɛ a kɔ dɩdaanɩŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa jɩŋ arɩ lɩɩ-la ka mʋa nɛ.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa bɩra gbɛrɛ tapʋlaa balɩpɛ, aŋ bɩra ta kokomo-la ʋ lɩɩ. Bʋa deeŋ-na lɛ kokomo-la bɩra bɩ mɩɩra jʋʋ ʋ teeŋ.

ref: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
text: Chɛŋ-lɩamɩŋ tapʋlaa mara arɩ balɩpɛ chɛɛŋ dɩ tinteeŋ hɩla dɛrɛ.

ref: God said to Noah,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋla pɩ Nowa,

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: “Ŋ dɩ ŋ haala, arɩ ŋ biiriŋ arɩ ba haalaa kala, má kʋ lɩɩ daboro-la tʋɔŋ.

ref: Let out the birds, animals, and reptiles, so they can mate and live all over the earth.”
text: Kɛŋ kʋŋkala sɩ kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ a hɛ ŋ teeŋ kʋ lɩɩ: Fillee, bagɩlaa, pʋsʋŋ arɩ tinteeŋ tuuroo a kʋ lɩɩ dɩ ba pʋŋa yʋgɛ.”

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa arɩ ʋ haala arɩ ʋ biiriŋ arɩ ba haalaa kʋ lɩɩ.

ref: the living creatures left in groups of their own kind. The Lord's Promise for the Earth
text: Bagɩlaa, pʋsʋŋ, zaaraa, arɩ tinteeŋ tuuroo kala ma kʋ lɩɩ aa vɛŋ doho doho.

ref: Noah built an altar where he could offer sacrifices to the Lord. Then he offered on the altar one of each kind of animal and bird that could be used for a sacrifice.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a pɩ La TƖƖNA a kɛsɛ kɩalɩŋ ba sɩ bɩ kɩsɛ: Pʋsʋŋ doho kala dɔnsʋŋ arɩ zaaraa doho kala dɔnsʋŋ, a kpʋ a ŋaa kʋnyɩgaa Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ a pɩ Wɩa Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ koru-la nyuŋ.

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa nɩɩ kɩalɩŋ Nowa sɩ nyɩga sʋɔra dɩ ba sɩma. Ʋ tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ, ʋ bʋl ʋ tʋɔŋ lɛ, “Mɩ bɩra bɩ jaŋ tɩŋ nuhuobiine wɩaa a ŋmɩɛsa hɛ tinteeŋ lɛ. Nuhuobiine tʋɔbɩɩnaa kala ŋaa kʋbɔmɔ nɛ a lɩɩ ʋ hambiiriŋ kala lɛ. Mɩ bɩra bɩ jaŋ kɔ dɩ mɩ kpʋ kʋŋkala sɩ weye arɩ mɩ sɩ ŋaa ŋɩɩ bʋa-la lɛ.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: Dʋnɩa sɩ hɛ nɩmɛ a kaa mʋmʋ, kɩaa duunuŋ arɩ kʋmʋŋ, waarɩŋ arɩ wulumuŋ, yibiiniŋ arɩ lɩmbaŋa, wɩɩhɛyɛ arɩ tɩtaŋɩŋ kala jaŋ hɛ nɩmɛ a kaa mʋ wuu.”

ref: God said to Noah and his sons: I am giving you my blessing. Have a lot of children and grandchildren, so people will live everywhere on this earth.
text: Wɩa pɛ Nowa arɩ ʋ biiriŋ lɛ aŋ bʋl, “Má lʋl biiriŋ yʋgɛ dɩ ba kɛŋ doho a pʋŋa su tinteeŋ kala.

ref: All animals, birds, reptiles, and fish will be afraid of you. I have placed them under your control,
text: Dʋnɩa kɩaa kala bagɩlaa, zaaraa, tinteeŋ tuuroo kala arɩ chenfilee sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ tʋɔŋ jaŋ fɩfáma. Ma jaŋ kɛŋ ba kala doluŋ.

ref: and I have given them to you for food. From now on, you may eat them, as well as the green plants that you have always eaten.
text: Kʋŋ na-kala sɩɩ wiesu jaŋ ŋaa ma kʋdiilii arɩ mɩ sɩ joŋ pɩpaa na-kala pɩma ŋɩɩ. Mɩ lɛɛlɛ joŋ kʋŋkala pɩma nɛ.

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Ama má sɩ́ nammɩa ha sɩ kɛŋ chalɩŋ chaŋ, chalɩŋ nɛ ŋaa ʋ mɩɩsɩŋ.

ref: I want you and your descendants to have many children, so people will live everywhere on earth.
text: Má lʋl biiriŋ yʋgɛ dɩ ba kɛŋ doho a pʋŋa su tinteeŋ kala.”

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋla pɩ Nowa arɩ ʋ biiriŋ,

ref: I am going to make a solemn promise to you and to everyone who will live after you.
text: “Lɛɛlɛ mɩ bil niiŋ nɛ mɩ dɩŋ arɩ ŋ doho nɩaa kala pɛgɛ,

ref: This includes the birds and the animals that came out of the boat.
text: a pɛ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩɩ wiesu lɛ: Zaaraa, bagɩlaa, pʋsʋŋ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩ to ŋ harɩŋ lɩɩ lɩɩŋ daboro-la tʋɔŋ.

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Niiŋ deeŋ nɛ mɩ bil mɩ dɩ ma pɛgɛ: Mɩ bɩra bɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ lɩɩ-balɩŋ chei dʋnɩa kɩaa kala sɩ kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ a hɛ dɔŋɔ. Lɩɩ-balɩŋ bɩra bɩ jaŋ dii dʋnɩa kala.

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Duoŋ-sɩa nɛ mɩ leŋ ʋ hɛ duoŋ-bɩlɩmbaanaa tʋɔŋ. Ʋ nɛ jaŋ ŋaa nii-la mɩ sɩ bile mɩ dɩ dʋnɩa nɩaa pɛgɛ magɩlɩŋ.

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Bʋa na-kala dɩ mɩ nɛ leŋe duoŋ-bɩlɩmbaanaa kʋ hɛ dɔŋɔ dɩ duoŋ-sɩa lɩɩ chɩŋ wɩa-nyuŋ,

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: mɩ jaŋ liisi nii-la mɩ sɩ bile mɩ dɩŋ pɛgɛ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩ weye a ŋaa doho doho a hɛ dʋnɩa lɛ. Mɩ bɩra bɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ lɩɩ-balɩŋ kʋ chei kɩalɩŋ mɩɩsɩŋ kala.

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Bʋa na-kala dɩ duoŋ-sɩa nɛ lɩɩ chɩŋ duoŋ-bɩlɩmbaanaa tʋɔŋ, mɩ jaŋ naʋ a liise yi nii-la sɩ bɩ dɛrɛ mɩ sɩ bile, mɩ dɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩ weye tinteeŋ lɛ pɛgɛ.”

ref: Noah and his sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, left the boat. Ham later had a son named Canaan.
text: Nowa hambolibiisi-la sɩ to ʋ harɩŋ kʋ lɩɩ lɩɩŋ daboro-la lɛ nɛ ŋaa Sɛm, Ham, arɩ Jafɛf .

ref: All people on earth are descendants of Noah's three sons.
text: Nowa hambolibiisiŋ deemba batori nɛ kʋ ŋaa dʋnɩa nɩaa kala doho.

ref: Noah farmed the land and was the first to plant a vineyard.
text: Nowa fa ŋaa paarʋ nɛ a bil baga a chɔnsɛ tɩɩnɛnɛɛ.

ref: One day he got drunk and was lying naked in his tent.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ tapʋlɩɩ kʋbala ʋ nyʋa tɩɩnɛnɛ-la sɩnɩŋ a bugi, a kɛŋ timbogulo pɩŋ ʋ lɛbɩŋ tʋɔŋ.

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Ham sɩ ŋaa Kɛnaŋ nyɩmma na ʋ nyɩmma timbogulo a lɩɩ bʋla pɩ ʋ naambiiri-la balɩamɩŋ sɩ hɛ dɩ-jalɩŋ.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Sɛm arɩ Jafɛf joŋ gɛrɩŋ daŋ ba vaasɩŋ lɛ a vɛŋ tulo a joŋ sɩaa ta dɩ-wogo dɩ ba sɩ́ ba nyɩmma timbogulo na, aŋ kɛŋ gɛrɩ-la kʋ tɔ ʋ yarɩ-bire-la.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Sɩŋ-la sɩ lɩɩ Nowa sɩaa lɛ, ka ʋ nɩɩ ŋɩɩ ʋ biiriŋ tʋɔŋ kʋbie-la sɩ ŋaa ʋ lɛ,

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: ʋ sɩ, “Mɩ ŋmɩɛsa hɛ Kɛnaŋ lɛ nɛ, ʋ jaŋ ŋaa yosuŋ tʋɔŋ yomo ʋ naambiiriŋ tʋɔŋ.”

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bɩra bʋl, “Mɩɩ dɛnnɛ mɩ Tɩɩna, Wɩa-la sɩ ŋaa Sɛm Wɩa. Kɛnaŋ jaŋ ŋaa Sɛm yomo.

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Wɩa jaŋ leŋ dɩ Jafɛf leriŋ jallɛ, dɩ Jafɛf jʋʋ Sɛm daanyʋɔsʋŋ lɛ aŋ ka dɩ Kɛnaŋ ŋaa Sɛm yoŋ.”

ref: After the flood Shem, Ham, and Japheth had many descendants. The Descendants of Japheth
text: Nowa hambolibiisiŋ Sɛm, Ham arɩ Jafɛf doho nɩaa nɛ ŋ-la. Lɩɩ-bal-la sɩ dii dʋnɩa harɩŋ lɛ, ba ma lʋl biiriŋ nɛ.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Lɩɩ-bal-la sɩ bala, Nowa biiriŋ doho nɛ gɔllɔ hɔŋ hɔŋ tinteyee, a pɩsa su dʋnɩa kala.

ref: At first everyone spoke the same language,
text: Bumbuŋ-la dʋnɩa nɩaa kala faa bʋl yikoru-bala nɛ a kɛŋ nii-bala.

ref: but after some of them moved from the east and settled in Babylonia,
text: Ba sii gɔllɔ lɩɩ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ dɩɩrɩŋ a kʋ na tintee-taala kʋbala sɩ hɛ Babɩlɔŋ tinteeŋ lɛ, a hɔŋ nɩmɛ.

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba bʋla pɩ dɔŋɔ, “Má leŋ dɩ la saa tabal arɩ dɩɩ-leŋiŋ sɩ jaŋ jɩla yi wɩa-nyuŋ a pɩ la tɩtɩa. Dɩ la nɛ ŋaa ŋɩɩ, la jaŋ laa yɩrɩŋ a pɩ la tɩtɩa a sɩ́ dɔŋɔ lɛ pɩsʋ, tinteeŋ lɛ.”

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA kʋ tuu dɩ ʋ na taŋ-la arɩ dɩɩ-leŋii-la nuhuobiinee sɩɩ saa.

ref: he said: These people are working together because they all speak the same language. This is just the beginning. Soon they will be able to do anything they want.
text: La TƖƖNA bʋl, “Dɩ nuhuobiine nɩɩ bʋl yikoru-bala, ba jaŋ wuo ŋaa wɩɩkala ba sɩɩ chɛ dɩ ba ŋaa.

ref: Let's go down and confuse them! We'll make them speak different languages, and they won't be able to understand each other.
text: Leŋ dɩ La tuu kɛŋ ba yikoro vʋgʋmɛ dɩ ba bɩra sɩ́ dɔŋɔ wʋbʋlʋŋ nɩɩ.”

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ ba fa yɩyɩrɛ taŋ-la Babɛl, bɛɛ wɩaa La TƖƖNA kɛŋ yikoro kala vʋgʋmɛ tinteeŋ lɛ nɛ. A lɩɩ nɩmɛ La TƖƖNA kɛmba pɩsɛ dʋnɩa kala lɛ. A lɩɩ Sɛm doho a kaa mʋ Abɩram doho

ref: Arpachshad had more children and died at the age of
text: Afaza sɩ lʋl Sɛla, ʋ hɛ ʋ mɩɩsɩŋ lɛ jɩsɩŋ zɔɔ-banɛsɛ arɩ batori nɛ aŋ lʋl hambolibiisiŋ arɩ hantolibiisiŋ dɔnsʋŋ.

ref: Serug had more children and died at the age of
text: Sɛrʋg sɩ lʋl Nahɔ, ʋ hɛ ʋ mɩɩsɩŋ lɛ jɩsɩŋ zɔɔ-balɩa nɛ, aŋ bɩra lʋl hambolibiisiŋ arɩ hantolibiisiŋ dɔnsʋŋ.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Tɛra kɛŋ ʋ nanʋala Abɩram arɩ ʋ nihiŋ Lɔt sɩ ŋaa Haraŋ nanʋala, arɩ ʋ bii-haala Saraɩ sɩ ŋaa Abɩram haala. Ba kala lɩɩ Uu sɩ hɛ Babɩlɔŋ tɩŋŋaa tinteeŋ a chaasɛ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ dɩɩrɩŋ a mʋmʋ. Ba sɩ yi taŋ-la ba sɩ yɩrɛ Haraŋ, ba hɔŋ nɩmɛ.

ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: La TƖƖNA fa bʋla pɩ Abɩram: “Lɩɩ ŋ tinteeŋ lɛ, a leŋ ŋ doho nɩaa arɩ ŋ dɩa nɩaa kala aŋ mʋ tintee-la mɩ sɩ jaŋ dagɩŋ.

ref: I will bless you and make your descendants into a great nation. You will become famous and be a blessing to others.
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ, a ŋaa tintee-bal. Mɩ jaŋ pɛ ŋ lɛ, a leŋ dɩ ŋ yɩrɩŋ yɩrɛ, a to ŋ lɛ a pɛ nɩaa lɛ.

ref: I will bless those who bless you, but I will put a curse on anyone who puts a curse on you. Everyone on earth will be blessed because of you.
text: Mɩ jaŋ pɛ nɩalɩŋ kala sɩ pɛ ŋ lɛ, aŋ ŋmɩɛsa hɛ nɩalɩŋ ma sɩ ŋmɩɛsa hɛ ŋ lɛ, aŋ to ŋ lɛ a pɛ dʋnɩa nɩaa kala lɛ.”

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: Abɩram vɛŋ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ lɛ ŋɩɩ nɛ a mʋ yi tɩɩ-bal kʋbala baa yɩrɛ More a hɛ lee-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Sɛkɛm. Bʋa-la lɛ dɩ Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa nɛ fa hɛ tintee-la lɛ.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa dagɛ Abɩram, aŋ bʋla pʋ, “Mɩ jaŋ joŋ tinteeŋ deeŋ a pɩ ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a pɩ La TƖƖNA, lee-la ʋ sɩ kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa dagʋ.

ref: Abram traveled to the hill country east of Bethel and camped between Bethel and Ai, where he built another altar and worshiped the Lord.
text: Ʋ lɩɩ nɩmɛ a mʋ peel-la sɩ hɛ Bɛtɛl wɩɩpɔsʋŋ, a hɛ ʋ lɛbɩŋ Bɛtɛl wɩɩjʋʋlʋŋ dɩɩrɩŋ, ka Aɩ ma hɛ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ dɩɩrɩŋ. Nɩmɛ nɛ ʋ saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a pɩ La TƖƖNA a yɩrɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ.

ref: Later, Abram started out toward the Southern Desert. Abram in Egypt
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram sii nɩmɛ a mʋmʋ Nɛgɛf dɩɩrɩŋ. Nɛgɛf hɛ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ nagʋa dɩɩrɩŋ nɛ. Abɩram arɩ Saraɩ hɛ Ijipiti tinteeŋ lɛ

ref: The crops failed, and there was no food anywhere in Canaan. So Abram and his wife Sarai went to live in Egypt for a while. But just before they got there, Abram said, “Sarai, you are really beautiful!
text: Ʋ sɩ mʋ kpagɛ Ijipiti tinteeŋ, ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram bʋla pɩ ʋ haala Saraɩ, “Mɩ jɩma ŋ ŋaa hazɔŋ nɛ woruŋ.

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: Dɩ Ijipiti tɩŋŋaa nɛ naŋ, ba jaŋ bʋl, ‘Na ʋ haala nɛ ŋ-la.’ Ba jaŋ kpʋmɛ aŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ dɩ ba hɔŋ.

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Ba sɩ kʋ jʋʋ Ijipiti tinteeŋ, Ijipiti tɩŋŋaa na dɩ Saraɩ ŋaa hazɔŋ nɛ.

ref: The king's officials told him about her, and she was taken to his house.
text: Ijipiti kuoro nanzɔɔba sɩ na Saraɩ, ba bʋl ʋ zɔmʋŋ wɩaa a pɩ ba kuoro aŋ kɛnʋ kaa jʋʋ ʋ dɩa.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ijipiti kuoro kɛŋ Abɩram woruŋ a tɩŋ Saraɩ wɩaa a pʋ piesee, nɛsɩŋ, kaku-belliŋ arɩ nɩɩnɩŋ, aŋ pʋ tɩntɩnnaa arɩ bʋntaŋŋaba.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Ama a tɩŋ Saraɩ wɩaa La TƖƖNA joŋ nyanyal bɔŋŋɔɔ hɛ Ijipiti kuoro arɩ ʋ dɩa nɩaa lɛ.

ref: Finally, the king sent for Abram and said to him, “What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me Sarai was your wife?
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ijipiti kuoro yɩrɛ Abɩram a pɩɛsʋ: “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ ŋaa ŋ-la? Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ bɩ bʋla pɩmɛ arɩ ʋ ŋaa ŋ haala nɛ?

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ bʋl arɩ ʋ ŋaa ŋ dɩhaal nɛ, a leŋ mɩ kɛnʋ mʋ ŋaa mɩ haal? Laa ŋ haala kaa viiri.”

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ijipiti kuoro leŋ ʋ nɩaa kɛŋ Abɩram arɩ ʋ haala arɩ ba kɩaa kala a kaa lɩɩ hɛ wombiiŋ lɛ dɩ ba viiri.

ref: Abram and Sarai took everything they owned and went to the Southern Desert. Lot went with them.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram arɩ ʋ haala arɩ kʋŋkala ʋ sɩ kɛnɛ lɩɩ Ijipiti tinteeŋ lɛ a mʋ Nɛgɛf tinteeŋ. Lɔt to ba harɩŋ.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ lɩɩ Nɛgɛf a dʋʋrɛ ŋɩɩ a kʋ yi Bɛtɛl, a kiele mʋ Bɛtɛl arɩ Aɩ pɛgɛ, lee-la ʋ lɛbɩŋ fa sɩ hɛ.

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: Lee-la nɛ ʋ laa sɩpaaŋ saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a yɩrɛ La TƖƖNA yɩrɩŋ.

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Lɔt fa sɩɩ to Abɩram harɩŋ, ʋ ma fa kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa piesee, nɛsɩŋ, arɩ lɛŋŋɛɛ nɛ.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram bʋla pɩ Lɔt, “Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, la ŋaa naambiiriŋ nɛ, sɩ́ leŋe la yuyuo dɔŋɔ, dɩ la kʋdaaraa ma sɩ́ dɔŋɔ yuyuo.

ref: There is plenty of land for you to choose from. Let's separate. If you go north, I'll go south; if you go south, I'll go north.”
text: Tinteeŋ dee bɩ ŋɩɩ pɩnaa? Leŋ la pɔrɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ, dɩ ŋ nɛ jaŋ mʋ nadiiŋ, mɩ jaŋ mʋ nagʋa. Dɩ ŋ nɛ bɩra jaŋ mʋ nagʋa, mɩ jaŋ mʋ nadiiŋ.”

ref: This happened before the Lord had destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. And when Lot looked around, he saw there was plenty of water in the Jordan Valley. All the way to Zoar the valley was as green as the garden of the Lord or the land of Egypt.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Lɔt bee na Jɔdaŋ fuonaaŋ kala dɩ ʋ pɩna a fɩɛlɛ woruŋ. Fuonaa-la pɩŋ ŋɩɩ nɛ a mʋ yi taŋ-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Zʋaa. Ʋ fa kɛŋ lɩɩŋ kɩŋkɛŋ a nagɛ Ɛdɩŋ ŋmaŋkaaŋ a bɩra nagɛ Ijipiti tinteeŋ ma.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Lɔt bagɩsɛ joŋ Jɔdaŋ fuonaa-la kala a mʋ hɔŋ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ dɩɩrɩŋ, ʋ dɩ Abɩram pɔrɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram hɔŋ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ dundolo nyuŋ, ka Lɔt ma mʋ hɔŋ tabala-la sɩ hɛ fuonaaŋ lɛ a hɛ ʋ lɛbɩŋ kpagɛ tabal-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Sodom.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Ama Sodom nɩaa fa ŋaa nɩbɔmɔ nɛ a chaasa ŋaa wʋbɔŋŋɔɔ La TƖƖNA teeŋ.

ref: After Abram and Lot had gone their separate ways, the Lord said to Abram: Look around to the north, south, east, and west.
text: Abɩram arɩ Lɔt sɩ kɛrɛ dɔgɔ lɛ, Wɩa bʋla pɩ Abɩram, “Lee-la ŋ sɩ chɩna, kɛŋ ŋ nyuŋ je a beŋ taŋ-sɛsɩŋ, taŋ-bʋbʋɔŋ arɩ taŋ-nyuŋ.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: Lee-la kala ŋ sɩ na, mɩ jaŋ joŋo pɩŋ, ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa a kaa mʋ wuu.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa nagɛ hagɩlɩ-biee. Dɩ nʋʋ nɛ jaŋ wuo dɩɩsɛ hagɩlɩ-bie, ʋ jaŋ wuo dɩɩsɛ ŋ doho nɩaa.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Sii a vɛnɛ golli tintee-la, ʋ duomuŋ arɩ ʋ jalɩŋ. Ʋ nɛ mɩɩ chɛ mɩ joŋo pɩŋ.”

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram churi ʋ lɛŋŋɛɛ a kaa mʋ hɔŋ taŋ-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Hɛbʋrɔŋ lɛ a kpagɛ Mamɩrɛ tɩɩ-bala-la. Nɩmɛ nɛ ʋ saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a pɩ La TƖƖNA.

ref: About this time, King Amraphel of Babylonia, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim
text: Kuoroo banɛsɛ nɛ fa hɛ nɩmɛ: Babɩlɔŋ kuoro Amɩrafɛl, Ɛlasa kuoro Arɔk, Ɛlam kuoro Kedolooma arɩ Goim kuoro Tɩdal.

ref: attacked King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, a city also known as Zoar.
text: Ba nɛ sii a mʋ dɩ ba yuo kuoroo deemba banɔŋ: Sodom kuoro Bɛra, Gomora kuoro Bɛɛsa, Adɩma kuoro Sɩnab, Zeboyim kuoro Sɛmɛba arɩ Bɛla kuoro. Bɛla nɛ ha ŋaa Zʋaa.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Jɩ-fii arɩ banɛsɩmɩŋ tʋɔŋ Kedolooma arɩ ʋ nɩaa yuo dii nɩaa deemba: Refem tɩŋŋaa sɩ hɔŋ Asɩtɛrɔf Kanam lɛ. Zuzi tɩŋŋaa sɩ hɛ Ham lɛ, Emi tɩŋŋaa sɩ hɔŋ Kɩrɩatam lɛ

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: arɩ Hori tɩŋŋaa sɩ hɛ tintee-la peelee sɩ yʋga lɛ baa yɩrɛ Sɛɛ a kaa mʋ yi Ɛl Paraŋ sɩ kpagɛ tintee-hɩlɩŋ.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba mɩɩrɛ harɩŋ a mʋ Ɛŋ-Mɩsɩpat, Kadɛs nɛ ŋɩɩ, a yuo dii Amalɛk tɩŋŋaa tinteeŋ kala arɩ Amɔrɩ tɩŋŋaa fa sɩ hɔŋ Hazazɔŋ Tama lɛ.

ref: At Siddim Valley, the armies of the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bela fought the armies of King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Babylonia, and King Arioch of Ellasar. The valley
text: dɩ ba yuo Ɛlam kuoro Kedolooma, Goim kuoro Tɩdal, Sɩna kuoro Amɩrafɛl arɩ Ɛlasa kuoro Arɔk, kuoroo banɛsɛ a yuyuo kuoroo banɔŋ.

ref: was full of tar pits, and when the troops from Sodom and Gomorrah started running away, some of them fell into the pits. Others escaped to the hill country.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ dɩ Sɩdɩm fuonaa-la fa kɛŋ bʋa-tuonuŋ a kɛŋ keliŋ yʋgɛ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Sodom arɩ Gomora kuoroo arɩ ba laalɩyuoroo fɩfa dɩ ba laa ba tɩtɩa. Ba sɩɩ fa ŋɩɩ, dɔnsʋŋ tel Sɩdɩm fuonaa-la tʋɔŋ, ka dɔnsʋŋ ma fá jʋʋ peele-la tʋɔŋ.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ kuorusu-la banɛsɛ yuo dii Sodom arɩ Gomora nɩaa a laa ba kɩaa arɩ ba kʋdiilee kala a kaa viiri.

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Ba pɛ kɛŋ Abɩram ŋaana bie Lɔt arɩ ʋ dʋa kala ma pɛ mʋ, bɛɛ wɩaa bʋa-la lɛ dɩ ʋ ma fa hɛ Sodom lɛ.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Ba tʋɔŋ dɔŋɔ sɩ foro fa nɛ mʋ bʋla pɩ Abɩram sɩ ŋaa Hiburu tɩɩna. Bʋa-la lɛ Abɩram fa sɩ jʋʋ lee-la kpagɛ Mamɩrɛ tɩɩ-bala-la. Mamɩrɛ fa ŋaa Amɔrɩ tɩɩna nɛ. Ʋ naambiiriŋ nɛ fa Ɛsɩkɔl arɩ Ana, ba dɩ Abɩram fa ŋaa sɩma nɛ.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Abɩram sɩ nɩa dɩ ba yuo a kɛŋ ʋ ŋaana bie Lɔt, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ yɩrɛ ʋ laalɩyuoroo zɔɔ-batori arɩ fii arɩ chori ba sɩ lʋla ʋ dɩa lɛ ʋ dagɛ ba laalɩŋ-yuonuŋ a ŋaa ba lɩɩ kiri nɩalɩŋ harɩŋ a mʋ yi taŋ-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Daŋ.

ref: That night, Abram divided up his troops, attacked from all sides, and won a great victory. But some of the enemy escaped to the town of Hobah north of Damascus,
text: Ʋ tɩtaŋɩŋ Abɩram kɛŋ ʋ laalɩyuoroo kaa kpaa ba yuo dii nɩalɩŋ a kiriba a mʋ yi Hɔba. Hɔba hɛ Damasɩkʋs wɩɩpɔsʋŋ nagʋa dɩɩrɩŋ nɛ.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ laa kɩalɩŋ kala a laa Lɔt ʋ ŋaana bie arɩ ʋ dʋa arɩ haala-la arɩ nɩalɩŋ dɔnsʋŋ Kedolooma sɩɩ yuo kɛsɛ.

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Abɩram sɩ mʋ yuo dii kuoro Kedolooma arɩ ʋ kuoru dɔnsʋ-la aŋ mɩɩra kɔ, ŋɩɩ nɛ Sodom kuoro kʋ lɩɩ chemu Save fuonaa-la lɛ, ʋ nɛ ŋaa kuoroo fuonaaŋ.

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Mɛlɩkɩzɛdɛk kɛŋ kʋdiilee arɩ sɩnɩŋ kʋ pɩ Abɩram. Mɛlɩkɩzɛdɛk ŋaa Salɛm kuoro nɛ aŋ bɩra ŋaa nɩɩ-la sɩ laa nɩaa nyuŋ a tɩma pɩpɩ Wɩa sɩ kɩɩ kʋŋ kala.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ sʋl Wɩa pɩ Abɩram aŋ bʋl, “Wɩa-la sɩ kɩɩ kʋŋkala a ŋaa wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ tinteeŋ nɛ jaŋ pɛ Abɩram lɛ.

ref: All praise belongs to God Most High for helping you defeat your enemies.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
text: Mɩɩ dɛnnɛ Wɩa sɩ kɩɩ kʋŋkala sɩ joŋ ŋ dʋndɔŋŋɔɔ hɛ ŋ nɩsɩŋ lɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram kɛŋ kɩalɩŋ kala ka kpaa puguloo fii a joŋ dɔŋɔ pɩ Mɛlɩkɩzɛdɛk.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Sodom kuoro bʋla pɩ Abɩram, “Joŋ nɩalɩŋ pɩmɛ aŋ kɛŋ kɩalɩŋ.”

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Ama Abɩram bʋla pɩ Sodom kuoro, “Mɩ je mɩ nɩsɩŋ dagɛ Wɩa sɩ kɩɩ kʋŋkala a ŋaa wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ tinteeŋ, a ŋmɩɛsɛ

ref: that I won't keep anything of yours, not even a sandal strap or a piece of thread. Then you can never say that you are the one who made me rich.
text: arɩ mɩ bɩ jaŋ laa kʋŋkala sɩ ŋaa ŋ kʋŋ, dɩ ʋ ŋaa ŋmɛŋ koo nɛntɛbɩŋ jolo, dɩ ŋ sɩ́ wuo bʋl ŋ nɛ ŋaa mɩ dʋa.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: Mɩ bɩ ma kʋŋkala ka chɛ see kɩalɩŋ mɩ nɩaa sɩ die arɩ kpaal-la sɩ maga dɩ nɩalɩŋ sɩ to mɩ harɩŋ mʋ, Ana, Ɛsɩkɔl arɩ Mamɩrɛ, leŋ ba laa ba kpaalɩŋ.”

ref: Later the Lord spoke to Abram in a vision, “Abram, don't be afraid! I will protect you and reward you greatly.”
text: Ʋ ŋɩɩ harɩŋ nɛ Wɩa bʋl wɩaa a pɩ Abɩram arɩ duosoo tʋɔŋ. Ʋ sɩ, “Abɩram, sɩ́ leŋe dɩ fawʋllʋŋ kɛnɩŋ. Mɩ nɛ jaŋ chu tɔ ŋ lɛ, ŋ time jaŋ yʋgɛ.”

ref: But Abram answered, “Lord All-Powerful, you have given me everything I could ask for, except children. And when I die, Eliezer of Damascus will get all I own.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram pɩɛsɛ, “Mɩ Tɩɩna Wɩɩjarɩbakɔ, bɛɛ nɛ ŋ jaŋ joŋo pɩmɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa mɩ bɩ bii kala kɛnɛ sɩ jaŋ dii mɩ dɩa? Nɩɩ-la sɩɩ beŋ mɩ dɩa nɛ Elieza a lɩɩ taŋ-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Damasɩkʋs.”

ref: You have not given me any children, and this servant of mine will inherit everything.”
text: Abɩram bɩra bʋl, “Ŋ sɩ bɩ mɩ biiriŋ pa, ŋɩɩ-na mɩ dɩa tɩntɩnnʋ-la Elieza nɛ jaŋ dii mɩ dɩa.”

ref: The Lord replied, “No, he won't! You will have a son of your own, and everything you have will be his.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa bʋla pʋ, “Baal-la dee bɩ jaŋ dii ŋ dɩa, ŋ tɩtɩa bii-lʋlʋŋ nɛ jaŋ dii ŋ dɩa.”

ref: Then the Lord took Abram outside and said, “Look at the sky and see if you can count the stars. That's how many descendants you will have.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA kɛŋ Abɩram lɩɩ dɩ-jalɩŋ aŋ bʋl, “Beŋ wɩa-nyuŋ a na chɛnwʋlaa sɩ magɛ ŋɩɩ, ŋ bɩ jaŋ wuo dɩɩsɛba. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ŋ doho nɩaa jaŋ magɛ.”

ref: Abram believed the Lord, so the Lord was pleased with him and accepted him. The Lord Makes Another Promise to Abram
text: Abɩram laa wɩalɩŋ kala La TƖƖNA sɩ bʋla pʋ dii nɛ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ Wɩa joŋu arɩ ʋ ŋaa tʋɔpʋl tɩɩna nɛ.

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋla pʋ, “Mɩ nɛ kɛnɩŋ lɩɩ Uu sɩ hɛ Babɩlɔŋ tinteeŋ kɔ dɩ mɩ joŋ tinteeŋ deeŋ pɩŋ nɛ. Ʋ jaŋ ŋaa ŋ kʋŋ.”

ref: Abram asked, “Lord God, how can I know the land will be mine?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram pɩɛsɛ, “La TƖƖNA Wɩɩjarɩbakɔ, ɛɛ nɛ mɩ jaŋ wuo jɩŋ arɩ mɩ nɛ jaŋ tɩɩ tinteeŋ deeŋ?”

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋla pɩ Abɩram, “Kɛŋ jɩsɩŋ batori napaha, jɩsɩŋ batori bʋ-nɩɩŋ, jɩsɩŋ batori pie-bele, koko-pʋla arɩ koko-fɩaŋ a kaa kɔ.”

ref: Abram obeyed the Lord. Then he cut the animals in half and laid the two halves of each animal opposite each other on the ground. But he did not cut the doves and pigeons in half.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram kɛŋ kɩalɩŋ Wɩa sɩ daga a kaa keri balɩa balɩa a kaa yelli yelli dɔŋɔ, ama ʋ bɩ kokosu-la kaa keri.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ zaaraa kʋ tuu dɩ ba dii kɩalɩŋ, Abɩram kiri.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Wɩɩ-muluŋ sɩ tuu jʋʋ, doŋ faasa kɛŋ Abɩram. Ʋ nyuŋ ŋmɛrɛ, fawʋllʋŋ kɛnʋ woruŋ.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Ama, ŋ doho nɩaa nihi-lɩɛrɛ naansɩɩŋ bʋnɛsɛ ha jaŋ mɩɩra kɔ daha, dɩ Amɔrɩ tɩŋŋaa wʋbɔŋŋɔɔ ŋaanɩŋ ha bɩ dɛrɛ.”

ref: Sometime after sunset, when it was very dark, a smoking cooking pot and a flaming fire passed between the two halves of each animal.
text: Wɩɩ-muluŋ sɩɩŋ sɩ jʋa, leriŋ biri lim, ŋɩɩ nɛ nyʋɔsʋŋ arɩ nyiŋgeliŋ to nammɩɩsɩ-la pɛgɛ a lɩɩ chaanɛ.

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: Tapʋlɩɩ-la tɩtɩa nɛ La TƖƖNA bil niiŋ ʋ dɩ Abɩram pɛgɛ a bʋl, “Mɩ jaŋ joŋ tinteeŋ deeŋ a pɩ ŋ doho nɩaa a chɩŋ Ijipiti fuo-la a kaa mʋ Yufireti fuo-bal-la, nɩaa deemba tinteyee:

ref: They will possess the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,
text: Keni tɩŋŋaa, Kenas tɩŋŋaa, Kadomo tɩŋŋaa,

ref: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites,
text: Hiti tɩŋŋaa, Pɛrɛs tɩŋŋaa, Refem tɩŋŋaa,

ref: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
text: Amɔrɩ tɩŋŋaa, Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa, Gɩgasɩ tɩŋŋaa arɩ Jebus tɩŋŋaa.”

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Abɩram haala Saraɩ bɩ bii kala lʋla. Ama ʋ fa kɛŋ ketiiŋ nɛ, baa yɩrɛ Haga a lɩɩ Ijipiti tinteeŋ.

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Saraɩ bʋla pɩ Abɩram, “La TƖƖNA bɩ leŋe mɩ lʋl bii, mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, kɛŋ mɩ ketiiŋ Haga dɩ ʋ lʋl biiriŋ pɩmɛ.” Abɩram sɛɩ wɩɩ-la Saraɩ sɩ bʋla.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Abɩram hɛ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ lɛ jɩsɩŋ fii nɛ ka ʋ haala Saraɩ joŋ ʋ ketiiŋ Haga a pʋ, ʋ ŋaa ʋ haal.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Abɩram arɩ Haga pɛ bɔsɔ ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ. Haga sɩ na dɩ ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ, ʋ sɩɩŋ bɩra bɩ Saraɩ ka su.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Saraɩ bʋla pɩ Abɩram, “Wɩɩŋ deeŋ sɩɩ ŋaa mɩ lɛ ŋ nɛ tɩɩ. Mɩ nɛ joŋ mɩ ketiiŋ pɩŋ, ʋ sɩ na dɩ ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ, ʋ sɩɩŋ bɩra bɩ mɩɩ su. La TƖƖNA nɛ jaŋ laa wɩɩ-la bʋla pɩ la kala.”

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram bʋla pɩ Saraɩ, “Ŋ ketiiŋ hɛ ŋ nɩsɩŋ lɛ nɛ, ŋaaʋ wɩɩ-la kala ŋ sɩɩ chɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Saraɩ dɔgɩsʋ woruŋ, ʋ lɩɩ fá.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Wɩa tɩndaara na Haga dɩ ʋ hɛ tintee-hɩlɩŋ lɛ a kpagɛ lɩɩ-bulo. Lɩɩ-bulo-la hɛ lee-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Suu wombiiŋ nɛ.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaarʋ-la pɩɛsʋ, “Saraɩ ketiiŋ Haga, nɩɩ nɛ ŋ lɩɩ a mʋmʋ nɩɩ?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Haga bʋl, “Mɩ fáa kagɛ mɩ dɩa-naaŋ lɛ nɛ.”

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: Wɩa tɩndaarʋ-la bʋla pʋ, “Mɩɩra mʋ ŋ dɩa-naaŋ teeŋ a sɛɩ pʋ.

ref: I will give you a son, who will be called Ishmael, because I have heard your cry for help. And someday I will give you so many descendants that no one will be able to count them all.
text: Wɩa tɩndaarʋ-la bɩra bʋla pʋ, “Ŋ kɛŋ luoruŋ nɛ, ŋ jaŋ lʋl hambolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Ɩsɩmɛl, bɛɛ wɩaa Wɩa na ŋ nenige nɛ.

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Bile jaŋ waa a nagɛ kaku-werigiŋ, ʋ dɩ nʋʋkala bɩ jaŋ sɩŋ. Ʋ jaŋ tɛrɛ ʋ naambiiriŋ teeŋ.”

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Ʋ sɩ bʋl ŋɩɩ dɛrɛ, Haga bʋl: “Lɛɛlɛ mɩ na Wɩa arɩ mɩ sɩɩŋ aŋ bɩ sʋba. Mɩ jaŋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ ‘Wɩa-la sɩ namɛ’.”

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba kɛŋ lɩɩ-bul-sɩɩ-la sɩ pɩŋ Kadɛs arɩ Bɛrɛd pɛgɛ yɩyɩrɛ Bɛɛ-Lahaɩ-Roi. Bɛrɛd bʋbʋɔŋ nɛ: ‘Wɩa-la sɩ weye a namɛ viliŋ lɛ’.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ nii-la mɩ sɩ bile mɩ dɩŋ pɛgɛ chɩŋ a leŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa pʋŋa yʋgɛ kɩŋkɛŋ.”

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩram tele chu, Wɩa bʋla pʋ,

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: Ba bɩra bɩ jaŋ yɩrɩŋ Abɩram, ŋ yɩrɩŋ jaŋ ŋaa Abɩraham, bɛɛ wɩaa mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ ŋaa tintee daŋ nyɩmma.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ pʋŋ. Ŋ doho nɩaa jaŋ ŋaa tintee daŋ, dɩ kuoroo lɩɩ ba tʋɔŋ.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ nii-la mɩ sɩ bile mɩ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa arɩ nɩalɩŋ sɩ jaŋ to ŋ harɩŋ kɔ pɛgɛ, dɩ ʋ chɩŋ kpaŋkpaŋ a kaa mʋ wuu. Mɩ jaŋ ŋaa ŋ dɩ nɩalɩŋ sɩ jaŋ to ŋ harɩŋ kɔ Wɩa.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ deeŋ lɛ ŋ sɩ hɛ a ŋaa nʋhʋɔrʋ ŋ-la, mɩ jaŋ joŋu pɩ ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa dɩ ʋ ŋaa ma kʋŋ a kaa mʋ wuu dɩ mɩ ŋaa ba Wɩa.”

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bɩra bʋla pɩ Abɩraham, “Ŋ-na Abɩraham arɩ ŋ doho nɩaa, má to nii-la mɩ sɩ bile mɩ dɩ ma pɛgɛ a kaa mʋ wuu.

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Peniŋ keriŋ nɛ jaŋ ŋaa mɩ dɩ ma pɛgɛ nii-bilii-la magɩlɩŋ.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Dɩ baal-na kala nɛ bɩ ʋ peniŋ kere, ʋ bɩ jaŋ ŋaa mɩ nʋʋ, bɛɛ wɩaa ʋ bɩ nii-la mɩ sɩ bile ka to.”

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋla pɩ Abɩraham, “Lɛɛlɛ bɩra sɩ́ ŋ haala Saraɩ yɩrʋ, yɩyɩrʋ Sara.

ref: I will bless her, and you will have a son by her. She will become the mother of nations, and some of her descendants will even be kings.
text: Mɩ jaŋ pɛ ʋ lɛ dɩ ma lʋl hambolibie, a bɩra pɛ ʋ lɛ dɩ ʋ ŋaa tintee daŋ naaŋ, dɩ kuoroo lɩɩ ba tʋɔŋ.”

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋla pɩ Wɩa, “Dɩ ŋ nɛ jaŋ pɛ Ɩsɩmɛl lɛ.”

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋl, “Mɩ sɛyɛ nɛ, ama ŋ haala Sara jaŋ lʋl hambolibie, dɩ ŋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Aɩzɩk. Mɩ jaŋ bil nii-la sɩ bɩ dɛrɛ a pɩ Aɩzɩk arɩ ʋ doho nɩaa kala.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: Ɩsɩmɛl ma wɩaa, mɩ nɩa nɛ, mɩ jaŋ pɛ ʋ lɛ a leŋ dɩ ʋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ woruŋ. Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ʋ ŋaa kuori-gentisiŋ fii arɩ balɩa nyɩmma, dɩ ʋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ woruŋ.

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Ama mɩ ha jaŋ bil niiŋ mɩ dɩ Aɩzɩk, pɛgɛ, bile Sara sɩ jaŋ lʋl harɩŋ jɩna bʋa deeŋ lɛ a leŋ dɩ ʋ chɩŋ.”

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Wɩa sɩ bʋl wɩaa pɩ Abɩraham dɛrɛ, ʋ leŋu aŋ mʋ.

ref: On that same day Abraham obeyed God by circumcising Ishmael. Abraham was also circumcised, and so were all the other men and boys in his household, including his servants and slaves. He was
text: a keri baala-la kala ba sɩ pɛ lʋl Abɩraham dɩa lɛ, arɩ yosu-la kala ba sɩ yɔbɔ nʋhʋɔraa teeŋ.

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa kʋ dagɛ Abɩraham a kpagɛ Mamɩrɛ tɩɩ-bala-la, bʋa-la lɛ dɩ Abɩraham hɔŋ ʋ lɛbɩŋ boiŋ niiŋ wɩɩ-hɛɛ-siesee.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: Ʋ kɛŋ ʋ nyuŋ je a na baalaa batori dɩ ba mʋ chɩŋ sɩpaaŋ. Ʋ sɩ naba, ʋ sii ʋ lɛbɩŋ boiŋ niiŋ a mʋ chemba lɩma lɩma. Ʋ sɩ chemba, ʋ tuu kpirimi ba sɩpaaŋ,

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: aŋ bʋla pɩ ba dɔŋɔ, “Mɩ kʋhɩaŋ, dɩ ma tʋɔŋ nɛ tɔrɛ mɩ nyuŋ, má sɩ́ mɩ-na ma tɩntɩnna kiele bal.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: Má leŋ dɩ mɩ kɛŋ lɩɩŋ mʋa kɔ dɩ ma sɔŋ ma naasɩŋ aŋ wiesi tɩa deeŋ bʋbʋɔŋ.

ref: Let me get you some food to give you strength before you leave. I would be honored to serve you.” “Thank you very much,” they answered. “We accept your offer.”
text: Ma sɩ zilmi ŋɩɩ a kɔ mɩ-na ma tɩntɩnna dɩa, má leŋ dɩ mɩ ŋaa kʋdiilee pɩma dɩ ma dii aŋ mʋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba bʋl, “Ŋ lɔlʋŋ nɛ hɛ nɩmɛ, la sɛyɛ nɛ.”

ref: Abraham went quickly to his tent and said to Sarah, “Hurry! Get a large sack of flour and make some bread.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham fá jʋʋ lɛbɩŋ tʋɔŋ Sara teeŋ, a bʋla pʋ, “Ŋaa munuŋ gbansɩŋ batori a ŋaa kʋdiilee lɩma.”

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abɩraham jʋʋ na-dɩa a kɛŋ na-bie sɩ kɛŋ nammɩa a pɩ ʋ tɩntɩnna ʋ kpʋ a bɩrɩmɛ lɩma lɩma.

ref: He then served his guests some yogurt and milk together with the meat. While they were eating, he stood near them under the tree,
text: Ʋ kɛŋ nayɩl-nʋʋŋ, nayɩlɩŋ arɩ nammɩa-la ba sɩ chɔ kaa kʋ lɩɩ tɩa-la bʋbʋɔŋ pɩba ba didii, ka ʋ gbɛrɛ.

ref: and they asked, “Where's your wife Sarah?” “She is right there in the tent,” Abraham answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba pɩɛsɛ Abɩraham, “Nɩɩ nɛ ŋ haala Sara hɛ?” Ʋ sɩ ʋ hɛ lɛbɩŋ tʋɔŋ.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Bʋa deeŋ lɛ dɩ Abɩraham arɩ Sara hɩɛsa, Sara paala kieli biiriŋ lʋlʋŋ.

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”
text: Sara fɩɛla mɔŋ a hɛ ʋ tʋɔŋ aŋ bʋl, “Mɩ sɩ hɩɛsa ŋ-la! Mɩ bala ma hɩɛsɛ, mɩ bɩra jaŋ bɩbɩɩnɛ baalaa wɩaa?”

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA pɩɛsɛ Abɩraham, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ Sara mɔŋ ʋ tʋɔŋ lɛ, ʋ bɩɩna ʋ sɩ hɩɛsa wɩaa ʋ bɩra bɩ jaŋ wuo lʋl bie?

ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: Wɩɩ nɛ hɛ nɩmɛ La TƖƖNA bɩ ʋ doluŋ kɛnɛɛ? Bʋa deeŋ tɩtɩa mɩ sɩ daga lɛ, mɩ jaŋ mɩɩra kɔ dɩ Sara lʋl hambolibie.”

ref: Sarah was so frightened that she lied and said, “I didn't laugh.” “Yes, you did!” he answered. Abraham Prays for Sodom
text: La TƖƖNA sɩ pɩɛsɛ ŋɩɩ, fawʋllʋŋ kɛŋ Sara, ʋ nyɩɛ wɩaa arɩ ʋ bɩ mɔmɔ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋl, “Ŋ mɔmɔ nɛ.” Abɩraham sʋla laa Sodom tɩŋŋaa

ref: When the three men got ready to leave, they looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham walked part of the way with them.
text: Baala-la batori sɩ sie dɩ ba viiri, ba to Sodom dɩɩrɩŋ nɛ. Abɩraham kɛmba mʋ hɛ wombiiŋ lɛ.

ref: The Lord said to himself, “I should tell Abraham what I am going to do,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋl, “Wɩɩ-la mɩ sɩɩ chɛ dɩ mɩ ŋaa, ʋ bɩ maga dɩ mɩ kaa faa Abɩraham.

ref: since his family will become a great and powerful nation that will be a blessing to all the other nations on earth.
text: Abɩraham doho nɩaa jaŋ pʋŋ a yʋgɛ a kɛŋ doluŋ ma. Ba lɛ nɛ mɩ jaŋ to a pɛ dʋnɩa nɩaa kala lɛ.

ref: I have chosen him to teach his family to obey me forever and to do what is right and fair. If they do, I will give Abraham many descendants, just as I promised.”
text: Mɩ nɛ lɩɩsʋ dɩ ʋ dagɛ ʋ biiriŋ arɩ ʋ doho nɩaa sɩ jaŋ to harɩŋ kɔ mɩ wombiiŋ dɩ ba kɛŋ wʋtɩtɩɩ, a ŋɩŋaa wɩaa ba bʋa lɛ, dɩ ŋɩɩ leŋ dɩ mɩ ŋaa wɩɩ-la mɩ sɩ bʋla pɩ Abɩraham arɩ mɩ jaŋ ŋaa pʋ.”

ref: ?” “Because of them, I won't destroy the city,” was the Lord's answer.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋl, “Mɩ nɩa arɩ Sodom arɩ Gomora nɩaa ŋaa nɩbɔmɔ nɛ a faasaa ŋaa wʋbɔŋŋɔɔ.

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: Mɩ jaŋ tuu na wɩalɩŋ ba sɩ ŋaa sɩ nagɛ ŋɩɩ nɩaa bubunsi ba bɔmʋŋ lɛ, dɩ ʋ ŋaa wʋtɩtɩɩ dɩ mɩ jɩŋ.”

ref: The men turned and started toward Sodom. But the Lord stayed with Abraham.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baala-la batori tʋɔŋ balɩa mɩɩra chaasɛ Sodom dɩɩrɩŋ a mʋmʋ aŋ ka Abɩraham ha chɩŋ La TƖƖNA sɩpaaŋ.

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Abɩraham sʋʋsa yi La TƖƖNA a pɩɛsʋ, “Ŋ jaŋ kpʋ tʋɔpʋl tɩŋŋaa arɩ nɩbɔŋŋɔɔ kala hɛ dɔŋɔɔ?

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Ʋ bɩ kɩsɛ dɩ ŋ kpʋ tʋɔpʋl tɩŋŋaa arɩ nɩbɔŋŋɔɔ kala hɛ dɔŋɔ lɛɛ? Ŋɩɩ bɩ kɩsɛɛ, Nɩɩ-la sɩ jaŋ dii dʋnɩa kala sarɩya sɩ jaŋ ŋaa ŋɩɩ?”

ref: Abraham answered, “I am nothing more than the dust of the earth. Please forgive me, Lord, for daring to speak to you like this.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bɩra bʋl, “Lɛɛlɛ mɩ nyuŋ duoro nɛ dɩ mɩ wuo bʋl wɩaa La TƖƖNA teeŋ, mɩ paala bɩ kʋŋkala ŋaa ŋ sɩpaaŋ.

ref: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
text: La TƖƖNA Wɩa arɩ Abɩraham sɩ bʋl wɩaa dɛrɛ, ʋ ta Abɩraham, ʋ mʋ dɩa.

ref: That evening, while Lot was sitting near the city gate, the two angels arrived in Sodom. When Lot saw them, he got up, bowed down low,
text: Wɩa tɩndaara-la balɩa mʋ Sodom dɩdaanɩŋ, dɩ Lɔt hɔŋ tabal-la boi-baalɩŋ. Ʋ sɩ naba, ʋ sii mʋ chemba a tuu kpirimi ba sɩpaaŋ a kɛŋ ʋ nyuŋ chuguli tinteeŋ,

ref: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
text: aŋ bʋla pɩba, “Mɩ kʋhɩasɩŋ, má kʋ jʋʋ mɩ-na ma tɩntɩnna dɩa a sɔŋ ma naasɩŋ aŋ chʋa, dɩ taŋ nɛ pʋla chʋchʋɔ-fɩɛla, ma sii viiri.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaara-la bʋl, “Aɩ, la jaŋ lɩɩ chʋa lee-la taŋ nɩaa sɩɩ hilimu.”

ref: But Lot kept insisting, until they finally agreed and went home with him. He quickly baked some bread, cooked a meal, and they ate.
text: Lɔt vɩa aŋ pɛsɛ ba lɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ba to ʋ harɩŋ jʋʋ ʋ dɩa. Ʋ ŋaa kʋdiilee a wasɛ boroboro sɩ bɩ sɩbʋrʋŋ kɛnɛ a pɩba ba dii.

ref: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
text: Ka dɩ ba-na pɩŋ doŋ, baalaa lɩɩ Sodom sɛmɛ kala kɔ, nɩhɩasɩŋ arɩ bapʋɔsaa kala a golli Lɔt dɩa.

ref: and started shouting, “Where are your visitors? Send them out, so we can have sex with them!”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baala-la yɩrɛ Lɔt a pɩɛsʋ, “Nɩɩ nɛ nʋhʋɔra-la sɩ kɔ ŋ teeŋ tɩtaŋɩŋ deeŋ hɛ? Kɛmba kaa kʋ lɩɩ pɩla dɩ la tʋʋ ba bʋbʋɔ-niiree.”

ref: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Lɔt kʋ lɩɩ chemba boiŋ niiŋ aŋ kɛŋ boro-la tɔ

ref: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
text: aŋ bʋl, “Nandɔmba, aɩ, má sɩ́ wʋbɔmʋŋ deeŋ ŋaa.

ref: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
text: Mɩ kɛŋ toloo balɩa nɛ, ba ha bɩ baal jɩŋ. Mɩ jaŋ kɛmba kʋ lɩɩ pɩma, dɩ ma ŋaa ŋɩɩ kala ma sɩɩ chɛ ba lɛ. Ama má sɩ́ wɩɩkala ŋaa nʋhʋɔraa deemba lɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa ba ŋaa mɩ nʋhʋɔraa nɛ, ʋ maga mɩ pɔba.”

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ nɩalɩŋ bʋl dɩ Lɔt sii lee-la aŋ bɩra bʋl, “Ŋ ŋaa nʋhʋɔrʋ nɛ la jaŋ lɛ, sɩ́ la wɩaa laa jɩŋ. La jaŋ dɔgʋsʋŋ a kɩɩ nʋhʋɔra-la.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba yigi Lɔt ta aŋ mʋ dɩ ba ŋmoo ʋ boro chei.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaara-la kɛŋ Lɔt lɛrɛ jʋʋ dɩa aŋ kɛŋ boiŋ niiŋ tɔ,

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: aŋ ŋaa nɩalɩŋ fa sɩ chɩŋ boiŋ niiŋ, banɩhɩasɩŋ arɩ bapʋɔsɩa-la kala nyʋlɩmɛ, ba chɛ dɩa-la boiŋ niiŋ ka.

ref: The two angels said to Lot, “The Lord has heard many terrible things about the people of Sodom, and he has sent us here to destroy the city. Take your family and leave. Take every relative you have in the city, as well as the men your daughters are going to marry.”
text: Laa chɛ dɩ la chei taŋ deeŋ nɛ. Wɩa nɩa dɩ nɩaa faasa bubunsi a tɩgɛ nɩaa deemba lɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ Wɩa tɩŋ la dɩ la kʋ chei taŋ deeŋ.”

ref: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Lɔt lɩɩ bʋla pɩ nɩalɩŋ fa sɩɩ chɛ dɩ ba jaa ʋ toloo, “Má sii a ŋaa dee dee a lɩɩ taŋ deeŋ lɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa La TƖƖNA ka chɛ ʋ chei tabalɩŋ deeŋ nɛ.” Ama ba fa bɩɩna Lɔt faa gbieli nɛ.

ref: Early the next morning the two angels tried to make Lot hurry and leave. They said, “Take your wife and your two daughters and get away from here as fast as you can! If you don't, every one of you will be killed when the Lord destroys the city.”
text: Taŋ sɩ pʋlaa kɔ, Wɩa tɩndaara-la bʋla pɩ Lɔt, “Ŋaa lɩma a kɛŋ ŋ haala arɩ ŋ toloo balɩa-la sɩ hɛ daha a lɩɩ, dɩ ŋɩɩ dee, ŋ ma jaŋ sʋʋ dɩ tabalɩŋ deeŋ nɩɩ chei.”

ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Lɔt kɛŋ dɩ ʋ lɛrɛ, Wɩa tɩndaara-la kɛŋ ʋ nɩsɩŋ lɛ a kɛŋ ʋ haala ma nɩsɩŋ lɛ arɩ ʋ toloo balɩa-la kala nɩsaa lɛ a kaa lɛrɛ lɩɩ tabal-la lɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa La TƖƖNA fá ba nenige nɛ.

ref: When they were outside, one of the angels said, “Run for your lives! Don't even look back. And don't stop in the valley. Run to the hills, where you'll be safe.”
text: Ba sɩ kɛmba lɩɩ dɛrɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaara-la dɔŋɔ bʋla pɩba, “Má fá a laa ma mɩɩsɩŋ! Má sɩ́ harɩŋ daasʋ, a sɩ́ lee-kala ma chɩŋ! Má fá jɩl peele-la nyuŋ, dɩ ŋɩɩ dee ma jaŋ sʋʋ.”

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: Mɩ-na ma tɩntɩnna na ŋɩɩ ma sɩ chomi nɛ. Má kɛmmɛ woruŋ a chɩchɛ dɩ ma laa mɩ mɩɩsɩŋ, ama mɩ bɩ jaŋ wuo fá jɩl peele-la nyuŋ, wɩɩŋ deeŋ jaŋ puumi dɩ mɩ sʋʋ.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Bee-na tabie nɛ kpagɛ daha, ʋ faasa muuri nɛ. Mɩ jaŋ wuo fá mʋ tabie-laa? Má leŋ dɩ mɩ fá mʋ lee-la a laa mɩ mɩɩsɩŋ.”

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Fá lɩma mʋ nɩmɛ, mɩ bɩ jaŋ wuo ŋaa wɩɩkala see dɩ ŋ yi Zʋaa .”

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Wɩa kʋʋ haasɛ dɩ Lɔt jʋʋ Zʋaa.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Wɩa leŋ jiribiiŋ chol nyiniŋ, a lɩɩ wɩa-nyuŋ a kʋ tuu dii Sodom arɩ Gomora kala,

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
text: a dii nuhuobiinee arɩ kʋŋ na-kala sɩ hɛ tabala-la balɩamɩŋ tinteeŋ lɛ.

ref: On the way, Lot's wife looked back and was turned into a block of salt.
text: Lɔt haala daasɛ harɩŋ a bɩrɩmɛ yɩsɩŋ a ŋaa jebiiŋ a chɩŋ.

ref: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
text: Chʋchʋɔ-fɩɛla Abɩraham sii folo mʋ lee-la ʋ fa sɩ chɩŋ La TƖƖNA sɩpaaŋ.

ref: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
text: Ʋ sɩ beŋ Sodom arɩ Gomora tinteeŋ dɩɩrɩŋ, dɩ nyiŋgeliŋ arɩ nyʋɔsʋŋ sii jɩjɩl wɩa-nyuŋ a nagɛ ŋɩɩ hololo a dii fuonaa-la kala.

ref: When God destroyed the cities of the valley where Lot lived, he remembered his promise to Abraham and saved Lot from the terrible destruction. Moab and Ammon
text: A tɩŋ Wɩa sɩ bil nii-la pɩ Abɩraham wɩaa, nyiniŋ dii tasɩ-la kala lɛ Lɔt fa sɩ hɔnɔ aŋ lɩɩsɛ Lɔt ta. Lɔt arɩ ʋ toloo wɩaa

ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Lɔt arɩ ʋ toloo balɩa-la lɩɩ Zʋaa lɛ a mʋ hɛ peele-la tʋɔŋ a jʋjʋʋ bʋ-bʋa a tɩŋ fawʋllʋŋ sɩ kɛmba wɩaa.

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: Tapʋlɩɩ kʋbala ʋ tol-hɩaŋ bʋla pɩ ʋ ŋaana, “La nyɩmma hɩɛsa nɛ, ka baal ma kala tuo sɩ jaŋ jaala dɩ la lʋl biiriŋ. Biiriŋ lʋlʋŋ ŋaa dʋnɩa kala wɩɩ nɛ.

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: Leŋ dɩ la pɩ la nyɩmma sɩnɩŋ dɩ ʋ nyʋa bugi, a kɛnla pɩŋ dɩ la to ʋ lɛ kɛŋ biiriŋ dɩ la doho sɩ́ dɛrʋ.”

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
