text: Ʋ tɩtaŋɩŋ ba pɩ ba nyɩmma sɩnɩŋ ʋ nyʋa bugi, tol-hɩaŋ jʋʋ ʋ dɩ ʋ nyɩmma pɛ bɔsɔ, ka ʋ nyɩmma bɩ jɩŋ ŋɩɩ ʋ tolo sɩ ŋaa kʋ jʋʋ a bɩra bɩ jɩŋ ŋɩɩ ʋ sɩ ŋaa lɩɩ.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Ʋ taŋ sɩ pʋla, ŋɩɩ nɛ tol-hɩaŋ-la bʋla pɩ ʋ ŋaana arɩ ʋ dɩ ʋ nyɩmma dɩa pɛ bɔsɔ nɛ. Ʋ sɩ, “Leŋ dɩ la bɩra pʋ sɩnɩŋ jɩnɩŋ tɩtaŋɩŋ dɩ ʋ nyʋa bugi dɩ ŋ ma jʋʋ dɩ ŋ dɩ ʋ pɩŋ. Ŋɩɩ lɛ nɛ la jaŋ to ʋ lɛ a lʋl biiriŋ dɩ la doho sɩ́ dɛrʋ.”

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba bɩra leŋ ba nyɩmma nyʋa sɩnɩŋ bugi tɩtaŋɩŋ, tol-bie-la ma bɩra jʋʋ ʋ dɩ ʋ pɛ bɔsɔ, ka ʋ nyɩmma bɩ jɩŋ ŋɩɩ ʋ sɩ ŋaa jʋʋ arɩ ŋɩɩ ʋ sɩ ŋaa lɩɩ.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Lɔt tolie-la balɩa kala ŋaa ba nyɩmma lɛ a kɛŋ luoruŋ.

ref: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
text: Ʋ tol-hɩaŋ lʋl hambolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Mʋab, ʋ nɛ jɩnɩŋ ŋaa Mʋab tɩŋŋaa naabalɩma.

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: Ʋ tol-bie ma lʋl hambolibie, a ma hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Bɛŋ-Ami, ʋ ma nɛ ŋaa Amɔŋ tɩŋŋaa naabalɩma jɩnɩmba ŋ-la.

ref: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.
text: Ʋ sɩ yi Gɛra, ʋ bʋl arɩ Sara ŋaa ʋ dɩhaal nɛ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Gɛra kuoro Abɩmɛlɛk tɩŋ ba mʋ kɛŋ Sara kʋ pʋ.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Ama Abɩmɛlɛk pɩŋ tɩtaŋɩŋ a duoso na Wɩa, ʋ bʋla pʋ, “Ŋ jaŋ sʋʋ, bɛɛ wɩaa haal-la ŋ sɩ leŋe ba kaa kʋ pɩŋ ŋaa nʋʋ haal nɛ.”

ref: Abimelech said to the Lord, “Don't kill me! I haven't slept with Sarah. Didn't they say they were brother and sister? I am completely innocent.”
text: Abɩraham tɩtɩa nɛ bʋla arɩ Sara ŋaa ʋ dɩhaal nɛ. Sara ma sɛɩ arɩ ʋ ŋaa ʋ dɩbaal nɛ. Mɩ ŋaa wɩɩŋ deeŋ arɩ tʋɔpʋla nɛ, mɩ bɩ wʋbɔŋ kala bɩɩna.”

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bɩra bʋla pʋ duoso-la tʋɔŋ, “Mɩ jɩma ŋ bɩ wɩɩkala cheye, ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ mɩ bɩ leŋe ŋ digi tolo-la dɩ ŋɩɩ ŋaa wʋbɔŋ.

ref: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
text: Ŋɩɩ wɩaa joŋ haal-la pɩ ʋ bala, ʋ ŋaa mɩ wʋlaabʋllʋ nɛ. Ʋ jaŋ sʋlmɛ pɩŋ dɩ ŋ sɩ́ sʋʋ. Ama dɩ ŋ nɛ vɩa, jɩŋ ma dɩ ŋ nɩaa kala jaŋ sʋʋ.”

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
text: Ʋ taŋ sɩ pʋla chʋchʋɔ-fɩɛla Abɩmɛlɛk sii yɩrɛ ʋ nɩhɩasɩŋ kala kʋ hilimi a bʋl wɩalɩŋ kala sɩ ŋaa a pɩba. Ba sɩ nɩɩ ŋɩɩ, fawʋllʋŋ kɛmba woruŋ.

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk yɩrɛ Abɩraham a pɩɛsʋ, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ ŋaa wɩɩŋ deeŋ la lɛ? Bɛɛ wɩɩ nɛ mɩ cheyiŋ ŋ lɛrɛ wʋbɔŋ-balɩŋ deeŋ kʋ hɛ mɩ dɩ mɩ nɩaa lɛ? Ŋ ŋaa wɩɩ fa sɩ bɩ maga dɩ ŋ ŋaa nɛ mɩ lɛ.”

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk bɩra pɩɛsɛ Abɩraham, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ ŋaa ŋ-la?”

ref: Abraham answered: I did it because I didn't think any of you respected God, and I was sure that someone would kill me to get my wife.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋl, “Mɩ bɩɩna ma bɩ Wɩa ka fá daha, ma jaŋ tɩŋ haal-la wɩaa a kpʋmɛ.

ref: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
text: Wɩa sɩ ŋaa mɩ lɩɩ mɩ nyɩmma dɩa lɛ, mɩ bʋla pɩ mɩ haala Sara, ‘Ŋ-la nɛ ŋ jaŋ dagɛ arɩ ŋ chomi nɛ: Dɩ la nɛ mʋ lee-kala, bʋl mɩ ŋaa ŋ dɩbaal nɛ.’ ”

ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk kɛsɛ piesee, nɛsɩŋ, baalaa arɩ haalaa sɩ ŋaa yosuŋ a pɩ Abɩraham a pɛ joŋ ʋ haala Sara bɩra pʋ.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Abɩmɛlɛk bʋla pɩ Abɩraham, “Mɩ tinteeŋ nɛ ŋ-la, hɔŋ lee-la kala ŋ sɩ cho.”

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: Wɩa fa leŋe Abɩmɛlɛk dɩa haalaa kala lʋla tɔ nɛ a tɩŋ Abɩraham haala Sara wɩaa.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: La TƖƖNA fá Sara nenige a ŋaa wɩɩ-la ʋ fa sɩ sɩ ʋ jaŋ ŋaa pʋ.

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: Ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie a pɩ Abɩraham bʋa-la tɩtɩa lɛ Wɩa fa sɩ daga arɩ ʋ jaŋ lʋl, dɩ Abɩraham hɩɛsa.

ref: Abraham named his son Isaac,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham hɛ bile yɩrɩŋ Aɩzɩk.

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: Aɩzɩk sɩ yi tapʋlaa chori, Abɩraham keri ʋ peniŋ arɩ Wɩa fa sɩ dagʋ ŋɩɩ.

ref: and Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. And now everyone will laugh with me.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Sara bʋl, “Wɩa leŋe mɩ tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ nɛ mɩ mʋmɔŋ. Dɩ nʋʋkala nɛ nɩɩ wɩɩŋ deeŋ, ʋ jaŋ pɛ mɩ lɛ dɩ la mɔŋ.”

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
text: Ʋ bɩra bʋl, “Kʋbɛɛ nɛ fa jaŋ wuo bʋla pɩ Abɩraham arɩ mɩ-na Sara jaŋ lʋl bie a kɛŋ yɩlɩŋ pʋ dɩ ʋ wasɛ? Ama ʋ hɩɛsa ŋɩɩ nɛ aŋ ka mɩ lʋl bie pʋ.” Ba kiri Haga arɩ Ɩsɩmɛl ta

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Aɩzɩk waa, ba kɛrʋ yɩlɩŋ lɛ. Tapʋlɩɩ-la ba sɩ kɛrʋ yɩlɩŋ lɛ, Abɩraham ŋaa kʋdiilee ba gbieli gbieli-bal.

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Sara bʋla pɩ Abɩraham, “Kiri yomo-la arɩ ʋ bie ta, mɩ bɩ chɛ dɩ yomo-la bie pɛ mɩ bie Aɩzɩk lɛ dii dɩa.”

ref: But God said, “Abraham, don't worry about your slave woman and the boy. Just do what Sarah tells you. Isaac will inherit your family name,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋla pʋ, “Sɩ́ leŋe dɩ wɩɩŋ deeŋ walɩmɩŋ a tɩŋ bile arɩ ʋ naaŋ wɩaa. Jegile nɩɩ wɩɩ-la kala Sara sɩ bʋla, bɛɛ wɩaa Aɩzɩk lɛ nɛ mɩ jaŋ to pɛ ŋ doho nɩaa lɛ.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ yomo bie Ɩsɩmɛl ma pʋŋ a ŋaa nɩ-daŋ, bɛɛ wɩaa ʋ ma ŋaa ŋ doho nʋʋ nɛ.”

ref: Early the next morning Abraham gave Hagar an animal skin full of water and some bread. Then he put the boy on her shoulder and sent them away. They wandered around in the desert near Beersheba,
text: Taŋ sɩ pʋla chʋchʋɔ-fɩɛla Abɩraham ŋaa kʋdiilee a ŋaa lɩɩŋ ma hɛ puruŋ tʋɔŋ a joŋo laalɛ Haga aŋ kiri ʋ dɩ ʋ bie ta. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba lɩɩ jaŋ-la lɛ a mʋ bɩbaarɛ Bɛɛsɩba tintee-hɩlɩɩ-la lɛ.

ref: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.
text: Lɩɩ-la fa sɩ dɛrɛ puru-la tʋɔŋ, ʋ joŋ bile bil tɩɩ-puguŋ bʋbʋɔŋ.

ref: Then she sat down a long way off, because she could not bear to watch him die. And she cried bitterly.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ aŋ kiele mʋ hɔŋ a yi naakieliŋ zɔlɔ aŋ bʋl, “Mɩ bɩ jaŋ wuo bibeŋ ka dɩ bile sʋʋ.” Ʋ sɩ hɔnɔ ŋɩɩ, ʋ yiyel.

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
text: Wɩa nɩɩ bile sɩɩ yel. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaara lɩɩ wɩa-nyuŋ a yɩrɛ Haga a bʋl, “Bɛɛ nɛ, Haga? Sɩ́ fawʋllʋŋ fá, Wɩa nɩɩ bile sɩ pɩŋ nɩmɛ aa yel nɛ.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Sii mʋ a kɛŋ bile dɩ ʋ sɩ́ yel, bɛɛ wɩaa, mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ʋ pʋŋ a kʋ ŋaa tintee-bal.”

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa suri Haga sɩaa ʋ na viliŋ sɩ kɛŋ lɩɩŋ. Ʋ mʋ ŋaa lɩɩŋ a su puru-la a kaa kʋ pɩ bile ʋ nyʋa.

ref: God blessed Ishmael, and as the boy grew older, he became an expert at hunting with his bow and arrows. He lived in the Paran Desert, and his mother chose an Egyptian woman for him to marry. A Peace Treaty
text: Bile sɩ hɛ Paraŋ tintee-hɩlɩɩ-la lɛ, ʋ naaŋ jaa Ijipiti tɩŋŋaa tolo pʋ. Nii-la Abɩraham arɩ Abɩmɛlɛk sɩ bile pɩ dɔŋɔ

ref: About this time Abimelech and his army commander Phicol said to Abraham, “God blesses everything you do!
text: Bʋa-la lɛ Abɩmɛlɛk arɩ ʋ laalɩyuori-hɩaŋ Fiko mʋ Abɩraham teeŋ a bʋla pʋ, “Wɩa chɩŋ ŋ harɩŋ nɛ, wɩɩ na-kala ŋ sɩɩ ŋaa lɛ.

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Ŋmɩɛsa pɩla Wɩa sɩpaaŋ arɩ ŋ bɩ jaŋ nyɩsɛla, a bɩra bɩ jaŋ nyɩsɛ la biiriŋ ma, koo la doho nɩaa. La ziliŋ nɛ. La sɩ ziliŋ ŋɩɩ, ŋ ma zilla arɩ la nɩaa, tintee-la ŋ sɩ́ hɔnɔ lɛɛlɛ a ŋaa nʋhʋɔrʋ lɛ.”

ref: And so, Abraham promised he would.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋl, “Tɔɔ, mɩ jaŋ ŋmɩɛsɛ.”

ref: One day, Abraham told Abimelech, “Some of your servants have taken over one of my wells.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋla pɩ Abɩmɛlɛk, “Ŋ tɩntɩnnaa sɩ kɛŋ vil-la laa mɩ teeŋ wɩɩmɛ nɛ woruŋ.”

ref: “This is the first I've heard about it,” Abimelech replied. “Why haven't you said something before? I don't have any idea who did it.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk bʋl, “Mɩ bɩ jɩŋ nɩɩ-la sɩ ŋaa wɩɩŋ deeŋ. Ŋ ma bɩ bʋla pɩmɛ, jɩnɩŋ nɛ mɩ nɩɩʋ ŋ-la.”

ref: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham kɛŋ piesee arɩ nɛsɩŋ a kaa kɔ pɩ Abɩmɛlɛk, ba balɩa-la bil niiŋ pɩ dɔŋɔ.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham lɩɩsɛ pie-keyee balɩpɛ, ʋ piesee tʋɔŋ.

ref: and Abimelech asked, “Why have you done this?”
text: Abɩmɛlɛk pɩɛsɛ Abɩraham, “Wɩɩ-la ŋ sɩ ŋaa bʋbʋɔŋ nɛ bɛɛ?”

ref: Abraham replied, “I want you to accept these seven lambs as proof that I dug this well.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋl, “Laa pie-keyee deemba balɩpɛ mɩ nɩsɩŋ lɛ dɩ ŋɩɩ dagɛ arɩ mɩ nɛ lu viliŋ deeŋ.”

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: Baa yɩrɛ lee-la Bɛɛsɩba, bɛɛ wɩaa nɩmɛ nɛ ba ŋmɩɛsa pɩ dɔŋɔ.

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Ba sɩ bil nii-la Bɛɛsɩba lɛ dɛrɛ, Abɩmɛlɛk arɩ ʋ laalɩyuori-hɩaŋ Fiko mɩɩra mʋ Fɩlɩsɩtɩa tɩŋŋaa tinteeŋ.

ref: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham chɔnsɛ tɩa kʋbala baa yɩrɛ tamarɛs Bɛɛsɩba lɛ. Nɩmɛ nɛ ʋ yɩrɛ La TƖƖNA yɩrɩŋ, Wɩa-la sɩ hɛ nɩmɛ a kaa mʋ wuu.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Abɩraham hɔŋ Fɩlɩsɩtɩa tɩŋŋaa tinteeŋ lɛ a dieni.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Ʋ sɩ yi bʋa kʋbala Wɩa chɩchɛ dɩ ʋ na Abɩraham laa ʋ wɩaa dii nɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ yɩrʋ, “Abɩraham!” Ʋ sɛɩ a bʋl, “Namɛ nɛ ŋ-la!”

ref: The Lord said, “Go get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋl, “Kɛŋ Aɩzɩk ŋ bii-bala-la ŋ sɩ cho, a mʋ lee-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Moria. Kpʋʋ peel-la nyuŋ mɩ sɩ jaŋ dagɩŋ a ŋaa kʋnyɩgaa Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ pɩmɛ.”

ref: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.
text: Ʋ taŋ sɩ pʋla chʋchʋɔ-fɩɛla, Abɩraham sii joŋ kaku-kpasa a daŋ ʋ kakumo nyuŋ a vɔɔ daasɩŋ daŋ a kɛŋ ʋ nanʋala Aɩzɩk arɩ ʋ tɩntɩnnaa balɩa a mʋmʋ lee-la Wɩa sɩ dagʋ.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Tapʋl torimuŋ chɛɛŋ Abɩraham beŋ sɩpaaŋ a mʋ na lee-la, dɩ ʋ hɛ dɩ-bolii.

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋla pɩ ʋ tɩntɩnna-la balɩa, “Má kɛŋ kakumo-la chɩŋ daha, ka mɩ dɩ bile kiele mʋ a chʋɔlɛ Wɩa aŋ kɔ.”

ref: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
text: Abɩraham puri daasɩ-la chʋŋ Aɩzɩk aŋ tɩtɩa kori sɩa arɩ nyiniŋ. Ba to dɔŋɔ mʋmʋ lee-la.

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋl, “Mɩ bii, Wɩa tɩtɩa nɛ jaŋ pɩla piesu-la la sɩ jaŋ kpʋ a nyɩga pʋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba to dɔŋɔ a mʋmʋ lee-la.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Ba sɩ mʋ yi lee-la Wɩa sɩ daga, ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a paa daasɩ-la daŋ ʋ nyuŋ aŋ vɔɔ ʋ bie Aɩzɩk a joŋo daŋ Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ koru-la arɩ daasɩ-la nyuŋ.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ lɩɩsɛ sɩa dɩ ʋ kpʋ Aɩzɩk.

ref: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaara yɩrɛ, “Abɩraham, Abɩraham.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ sɛɩ, “Namɛ nɛ ŋ-la.”

ref: “Don't hurt the boy or harm him in any way!” the angel said. “Now I know that you truly obey God, because you were willing to offer him your only son.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaarʋ-la bʋla pʋ, “Sɩ́ bile wɩɩkala ŋaa. Lɛɛlɛ mɩ jɩma ŋ fá Wɩa nɛ a laaʋ dii, bɛɛ wɩaa ŋ bɩ ʋ nii-la vɩa ʋ sɩ sɩ ŋ kɛŋ ŋ bii-bala-la kpʋ a nyɩga pʋ.”

ref: Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in the bushes. So he took the ram and sacrificed it instead of his son.
text: Abɩraham kɛŋ nyuŋ je, a beŋ dɩ ʋ na pie-bele dɩ ʋ nyilliŋ gɩrɩgɛ tiiŋ tʋɔŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham mʋ lɩɩsɛ piesu-la a kaa kʋ kpʋ a nyɩga pɩ Wɩa aŋ leŋ ʋ bile.

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Abɩraham hɛ lee-la yɩrɩŋ ‘Wɩa nɛ jaŋ chɛ pɩmɛ ’. Arɩ jɩnɩŋ kala lee-la yɩrɩŋ nɛ ha ŋɩɩ: “Peel-la nyuŋ Wɩa jaŋ chɛ pɩŋ.”

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaarʋ-la bɩra yɩrʋ lɩamɩŋ lɛ

ref: You were willing to offer your only son to the Lord, and so he makes you this solemn promise,
text: a bʋl, “Mɩ ŋmɩɛsɛ mɩ tɩtɩa a tɩŋ ŋ sɩ bɩ ŋ bii-bala-la vɩa dɩ ŋ kpʋ pɩmɛ,

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: mɩ jaŋ pɛ ŋ lɛ a leŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ a nagɛ chɛnwʋlaa, koo hagɩlɩ-bumbugulo sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ niiŋ. Ŋ doho nɩaa jaŋ yuo laa ba dʋndɔŋŋɔɔ tinteeŋ.

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: A tɩŋ ŋ sɩ sɛɩ mɩ niiŋ wɩaa, mɩ jaŋ to ŋ doho nɩaa lɛ a pɛ dʋnɩa nɩaa kala lɛ.”

ref: Abraham and Isaac went back to the servants who had come with him, and they returned to Abraham's home in Beersheba. The Children of Nahor
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham mɩɩra kɔ ʋ tɩntɩnna-la balɩamɩŋ teeŋ. Ba to dɔŋɔ mʋ Bɛɛsɩba. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham hɔŋ nɩmɛ. Nahɔ hambolibiisiŋ

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Ruuma fa ŋaa Nahɔ haala dɔŋɔ ʋ sɩ bile nɛ aŋ ha bɩ jaa ka ba lʋl biiriŋ deemba: Teba, Gaham, Tahas arɩ Maaka.

ref: he went to the Hittites and said,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham sii sʋʋ-la bʋbʋɔŋ a mʋ Hiti tɩŋŋaa teeŋ a bʋl,

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: “Mɩ ŋaa nʋhʋɔrʋ nɛ ma tʋɔŋ, mɩɩ sʋlma, má yallɛ leriŋ pɩmɛ dɩ mɩ joŋ mɩ haala hugi.”

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: “Nɩhɩaŋ, jegile nɩɩ la teeŋ, ŋ ŋaa kuori-bii-duo nɛ a hɛ la tʋɔŋ. Lɩɩsɛ la bʋɔsʋ-la tʋɔŋ kʋzɔmʋŋ a hugi ŋ haala. La kʋŋkala bɩ jaŋ vɔmʋŋ leriŋ.”

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: aŋ bʋl, “Dɩ ma nɛ jaŋ sɛɩ dɩ mɩ hugi mɩ haala daha, má sʋl Zoha nanʋala Ɛfʋrɔŋ pɩmɛ,

ref: to sell me Machpelah Cave at the end of his field. I'll pay what it's worth, and all of you can be witnesses.”
text: dɩ ʋ joŋ Makɩpɛla bʋ-bʋa-la sɩ hɛ ʋ ŋmaŋkaaŋ niiŋ, a yalla pɩmɛ ma sɩpaaŋ. Ʋ yalɩŋ sɩ maga ŋɩɩ kala, mɩ jaŋ tiŋ ka dɩ ʋ ŋaa mɩ sʋʋnʋŋ dɩ-huguŋ.”

ref: Ephron was sitting there near the city gate, when Abraham made this request, and he answered,
text: Dɩ Ɛfʋrɔŋ Hiti tɩɩna-la arɩ ʋ nɩaa kala hilime ba jaŋ boiŋ niiŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋla pɩ Abɩraham ʋ nɩalɩŋ kala sɩ kʋ hilimi ʋ jaŋ boiŋ niiŋ, ba kala nɩɩ.

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: “Aɩ, mɩ kʋhɩaŋ, jegile nɩɩ mɩ teeŋ, mɩ joŋ ŋmaŋkaa-la arɩ bʋa-la kala aa pɩŋ nɛ mɩ nɩaa sɩpaaŋ dɩ ŋ hugi ŋ haala.”

ref: Once again, Abraham bowed down
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bɩra tuu kpirimi tintee-la tɩŋŋaa sɩpaaŋ,

ref: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
text: aŋ bʋla pɩ Ɛfʋrɔŋ, ʋ nɩaa kala sɩpaaŋ, “Ɛfʋrɔŋ, jegile nɩɩ mɩ teeŋ, dɩ ŋ nɛ jaŋ sɛɩ, mɩ jaŋ tiŋ ŋmaŋkaa-la chɩmɩŋ pɩŋ aŋ joŋ mɩ haala hugi nɩmɛ.”

ref: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
text: Ʋ ŋɩɩ harɩŋ nɛ Abɩraham joŋ ʋ haala Sara hugi bʋ-bʋa-la lɛ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ.

ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
text: Abɩraham fa hɩɛsa nɛ woruŋ, Wɩa pɛ ʋ lɛ wɩɩkala lɛ.

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: Abɩraham bʋla pɩ ʋ dɩa tɩntɩnnɩ-hɩaŋ sɩɩ beŋ ʋ dɩa kʋŋkala, “Joŋ ŋ nɩsɩŋ hɛ mɩ naapuiŋ bʋbʋɔŋ.

ref: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
text: Mɩɩ chɛ dɩ ŋ ŋmɩɛsɛ La TƖƖNA Wɩa sɩ ŋaa wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ tinteeŋ kala arɩ ŋ bɩ jaŋ jaa tolo sɩ lɩɩ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ pɩ mɩ bie Aɩzɩk, nɩalɩŋ tʋɔŋ mɩ sɩ hɛ.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
text: Mʋ mɩ tinteeŋ, mɩ naambiiriŋ tɩtɩa tʋɔŋ a jaa haala kaa kʋ pɩ mɩ nanʋala Aɩzɩk.”

ref: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ tɩntɩnnʋ-la pɩɛsʋ, “Ka dɩ haal-la nɛ bɩ sɛyɛ dɩ ʋ to mɩ harɩŋ kɔ daha, mɩ kɛŋ ŋ bile kaa mʋ tintee-la ŋ sɩ lɩaa?”

ref: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋl, “Pɔ ŋ sɩ́ mɩ bie kaa mʋ nɩmɛ.

ref: The Lord who rules heaven brought me here from the land where I was born and promised that he would give this land to my descendants forever. When you go back there, the Lord will send his angel ahead of you to help you find a wife for my son.
text: La TƖƖNA, Wɩa-la sɩ ŋaa wɩa-nyuŋ a kɛmmɛ kaa lɩɩ mɩ nyɩmma arɩ mɩ naambiiriŋ tʋɔŋ kɔ daha, ʋ bil niiŋ pɩmɛ arɩ ʋ jaŋ joŋ tinteeŋ deeŋ pɩ mɩ doho nɩaa. Ʋ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ʋ tɩndaara laa ŋ sɩpaaŋ dɩ ŋ wuo na haala lee-la a pɩ mɩ bie.

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Dɩ haal-la nɛ bɩ jaŋ to ŋ harɩŋ kɔ, ŋɩɩ-na mɩ ŋmɩaŋ-la bɩra bɩŋ dii, ama pɔ dɩ ŋ sɩ́ mɩ bie kaa mʋ nɩmɛ.”

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ tɩntɩnnɩ-hɩaŋ-la joŋ ʋ nɩsɩŋ hɛ ʋ kʋhɩaŋ Abɩraham naapuiŋ bʋbʋɔŋ a ŋmɩɛsɛ arɩ Abɩraham sɩ dagʋ ŋɩɩ.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩntɩnnɩ-hɩaŋ-la kɛsɛ Abɩraham bʋntaŋŋaba fii a pɛ paa Abɩraham kʋzɔŋ na-kala doho a lɩɩ mʋmʋ Aram Naharem tinteeŋ, taŋ-la lɛ Nahɔ sɩ hɛ.

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: Ʋ sɩ mʋ yi taŋ-la dɩdaanɩŋ dɩɩrɩŋ, bʋa-la lɛ haalaa sɩ yie lɩɩ a ŋɩŋaa lɩɩŋ, ʋ leŋ bʋntaŋŋaba-la tuu pɩŋ viliŋ sɛmɛ.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ sʋl Wɩa a bʋl, “Ou, mɩ TƖƖNA, mɩ kʋhɩaŋ Abɩraham Wɩa, pɛ mɩ lɛ dɩ mɩ na wɩɩ-la mɩ sɩɩ chɛ jɩnɩŋ, a dagɛ ŋ zile pɩ mɩ kʋhɩaŋ.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Na, mɩ sɩ chɩŋ lɩɩ-bulo deeŋ sɛmɛ ŋ-la, taŋ deeŋ nɩaa toloo jaŋ kʋ lɩɩ dɩ ba ŋaa lɩɩŋ.

ref: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
text: Leŋ dɩ mɩ nɛ bʋla pɩ hantolibie, ‘Jaaŋ jaaŋ, mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, ŋaa lɩɩŋ pɩmɛ dɩ mɩ nyʋa,’ dɩ ʋ bʋl, ‘mɩ jaŋ ŋaa pɩŋ dɩ ŋ nyʋa aŋ pɛ ŋaa pɩ ŋ bʋntaŋŋaba maa,’ leŋ dɩ ʋ nɛ ŋaa nɩɩ-la ŋ sɩ lɩɩsa dɩ ŋ pɩ ŋ tɩntɩnna Aɩzɩk. Dɩ ŋ nɛ ŋaa ŋ-la, mɩ jaŋ jɩŋ arɩ ŋ dagɛ ŋ zile a pɩ mɩ kʋhɩaŋ nɛ.”

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham tɩntɩnnʋ-la mʋ chemu lɩma a bʋl, “Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, ŋaa lɩɩŋ mʋa ŋ nyuvie tʋɔŋ pɩmɛ.”

ref: “I'll be glad to,” she answered. Then she quickly took the jar from her shoulder and held it while he drank.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋl, “Mɩ nɩhɩaŋ, ŋ jaŋ wuo nyʋa,” aŋ chʋgɛ nyuvie-la lɩma a hɛ ʋ nɩsɩŋ lɛ a wɛnsɛ lɩɩ-la pʋ.

ref: After he had finished, she said, “Now I'll give your camels all the water they want.” She quickly poured out water for them, and she kept going back for more, until his camels had drunk all they wanted.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bɩsɛ ʋ nyuvie lɩɩŋ kala lɩma lɩma a hɛ pʋsʋŋ dɩ-nyʋɔlʋŋ, aŋ fá mʋ viliŋ a bɩra ŋaa lɩɩŋ a kaa kʋ pɩ bʋntaŋŋaba-la ba nyʋa a vɔgɛ.

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Ka baal-la bɩ wɩɩkala bʋla aŋ chɩna bibeŋ fii dɩ ʋ na dɩ La Tɩɩna jaŋ ŋaa dɩ ʋ wombii-la ʋ sɩ vɛnɛ ŋaa wombii-zɔŋ.

ref: The servant had brought along an expensive gold ring and two large gold bracelets. When Rebekah had finished bringing the water, he gave her the ring for her nose and the bracelets for her arms.
text: Bʋntaŋŋaba-la sɩ nyʋa lɩɩŋ dɛrɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la joŋ sɛlɩmɛ nenii-pɩrɩŋ ba sɩ yie joŋo hɛ mɩɩsɩŋ arɩ sɛlɩmɛ kagunsuŋ balɩa a pɩ tolo-la. Ba yallɩŋ hɛyɛ.

ref: Then he said, “Please tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ aŋ pɩɛsɛ tolo-la, “Kʋbɛɛ tolo nɛ ŋ ŋaa, mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ bʋla pɩmɛ. Dɩpɩnɩŋ jaŋ hɛ ŋ nyɩmma dɩa lɛ dɩ la chʋa tɩtaŋɩŋ?”

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ tolo-la bʋl, “Mɩ ŋaa Bɛtʋɛl tolo nɛ, Nahɔ arɩ ʋ haala Mɩlɩka nɛ lʋl Bɛtʋɛl.”

ref: We have a place where you and your men can stay, and we also have enough straw and feed for your camels.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ aŋ bɩra bʋl, “La kɛŋ pʋsʋŋ yadiilee yʋgɛ nɛ arɩ dɩpɩnɩŋ ma, ma sɩ jaŋ wuo pɩŋ tɩtaŋɩŋ.”

ref: Then the servant bowed his head and prayed,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la tuu kpirimi a chʋɔlɛ La TƖƖNA

ref: “I thank you, Lord God of my master Abraham! You have led me to his relatives and kept your promise to him.”
text: aŋ bʋl, “Mɩɩ dɛnnɛ mɩ Tɩɩna, mɩ nɩhɩaŋ Abɩraham Wɩa, ʋ sɩ bɩ mɩ nɩhɩaŋ vɩa ta aŋ dagɛ ʋ zile arɩ ʋ wʋtɩtɩɩ, mɩ na dɩ La TƖƖNA nɛ kɛmmɛ vɛŋ wombiiŋ a kɔ mɩ nɩhɩaŋ nɩaa teeŋ.”

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ tolo-la fá mʋ bʋla pɩ ʋ naaŋ dɩa nɩaa wɩalɩŋ kala sɩ ŋaa.

ref: Her brother Laban heard her tell what the servant had said, and he saw the ring and the bracelets she was wearing. So Laban ran out to Abraham's servant, who was standing by his camels at the well.
text: Labaŋ sɩ na nenii-pɩrɩ-la ba sɩ yie joŋo hɛ mɩɩsɩŋ arɩ kagunsu-la dɩ ba hɛ ʋ dɩhaala nɩsɩŋ arɩ wɩɩ-la Rɩbɛka sɩ bʋla arɩ baal-la bʋla pʋ, ʋ sii mʋ Abɩraham tɩntɩnnʋ-la teeŋ dɩ ʋ chɩŋ ʋ bʋntaŋŋaba bʋbʋɔŋ, lɩɩ-bulo-la sɛmɛ.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ bʋla pʋ, “Kɔ, ŋ ŋaa nʋʋ La TƖƖNA sɩ pɛ ŋ lɛ nɛ. Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ chɩŋ jeeŋ harɩŋ? Mɩ wasɛ ŋ dɩpɩnɩŋ arɩ ŋ bʋntaŋŋaba ma leriŋ nɛ.”

ref: Abraham's servant went home with Laban, where Laban's servants unloaded his camels and gave them straw and feed. Then they brought water into the house, so Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la jʋʋ dɩa, Labaŋ puri chʋgʋsʋ-la sɩ saga bʋntaŋŋaba-la nyuŋ a kɛŋ pʋsʋŋ yadiilee kʋ pɩ bʋntaŋŋaba-la, aŋ kɛŋ lɩɩŋ kʋ pɩba, ʋ dɩ baala-la kala sɩ to ʋ harɩŋ sɔŋ ba naasɩŋ.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba ŋaa kʋdiilee kʋ pɩ baal-la, ʋ bʋl, “Mɩ ha bɩ jaŋ dii see dɩ mɩ bʋl wɩɩ-la sɩ kɛmmɛ kɔ daha.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ sɩ, “Bʋla pɩla.”

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋl, “Mɩ ŋaa Abɩraham tɩntɩnnʋ nɛ.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: La TƖƖNA pɛ mɩ nɩhɩaŋ lɛ nɛ kɩŋkɛŋ, ʋ kɛŋ kɩaa woruŋ. Ʋ pʋ piesee, nɛsɩŋ, warɩpʋla, sɛlɩmɛ, tɩntɩnnaa, baalaa arɩ haalaa bʋntaŋŋaba arɩ kakusuŋ.

ref: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
text: Mɩ nɩhɩaŋ haala Sara hɩɛsa nɛ aŋ lʋl hambolibie a pʋ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ joŋ kʋŋkala ʋ sɩ kɛnɛ a pɩ bile.

ref: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ nɩhɩaŋ leŋ mɩ ŋmɩɛsɛ arɩ ʋ bɩ maga dɩ mɩ jaa Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa tolo a pɩ ʋ bie, nɩalɩŋ tinteeŋ lɛ mɩ sɩ hɔnɔ,

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: dɩ mɩ mʋ ʋ nyɩmma dɩa, ʋ doho nɩaa tʋɔŋ, a jaa haala nɩmɛ a kaa kʋ pɩ ʋ bile.

ref: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ pɩɛsɛ mɩ nɩhɩaŋ, ‘Ka dɩ haal-la nɛ vɩa dɩ ʋ to mɩ harɩŋ a kɔ daha?’

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋl, ‘La TƖƖNA mɩ sɩɩ to arɩ mɩ wʋtɩtɩɩ, ʋ jaŋ tɩŋ ʋ tɩndaara dɩ ʋ pɛ ŋ lɛ a leŋ dɩ ŋ wombiiŋ zɔŋ, dɩ ŋ wuo na haala mɩ nyɩmma dɩa lɛ, mɩ doho nɩaa tʋɔŋ a kaa kʋ pɩ mɩ nanʋala.

ref: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
text: Dɩ ŋ nɛ mʋ mɩ doho nɩaa teeŋ, a kɛŋ tolo kɔ koo dɩ ba nɛ vɩa dɩ ba joŋ haala pɩŋ dɩ ŋ kaa kɔ, ŋɩɩ-na ŋ laa ŋ tɩtɩa nɛ ŋmɩaŋ-la lɛ.’

ref: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
text: Mɩ sɩ kɔ lɩɩ-bulo deeŋ bʋbʋɔŋ jɩnɩŋ, mɩ bʋla, ‘Mɩ TƖƖNA, mɩ nɩhɩaŋ Abɩraham Wɩa, dɩ ŋ nɛ jaŋ ŋaa dɩ mɩ wombiiŋ deeŋ mɩ sɩ vɛnɛ ŋaa wombii-zɔŋ, wɩɩ-la wɩaa mɩ sɩ kɔ.

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: Na, mɩ chɩŋ lɩɩ-bulo deeŋ sɛmɛ nɛ, dɩ haal-la sɩ bɩ baal jɩŋ nɛ kɔ dɩ ʋ ŋaa lɩɩŋ dɩ mɩ bʋla pʋ: Jaaŋ jaaŋ, ŋaa lɩɩŋ pɩmɛ dɩ mɩ nyʋa,’

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: dɩ ʋ nɛ bʋla pɩmɛ, ‘Nyʋa lɩɩŋ ka dɩ mɩ ŋaa pɩ ŋ bʋntaŋŋaba ma, leŋ dɩ ʋ nɛ ŋaa tolo-la ŋ-na mɩ TƖƖNA sɩ lɩɩsa dɩ ŋ pɩ mɩ kʋhɩaŋ nanʋala.’

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Mɩ sɩ chʋɔlɛ Wɩa mɩ tʋɔŋ lɛ ŋ-la a dɛrɛ, dɩ Rɩbɛka kʋ lɩɩ a baasɛ ʋ nyuvie ʋ vaaŋ lɛ. Ʋ tuu lɩɩ-bulo a ŋaa lɩɩŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ bʋla pʋ, ‘Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, ŋaa lɩɩŋ pɩmɛ mɩ nyʋa.’

ref: She quickly lowered the jar from her shoulder and said, “Have a drink. Then I'll get water for your camels.” So I drank, and after that she got some water for my camels.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ chʋgɛ ʋ nyuvie ʋ vaaŋ lɛ lɩma lɩma aŋ bʋl, ‘Nyʋa ka dɩ mɩ ŋaa pɩ ŋ bʋntaŋŋaba ma.’ Ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ nyʋa aŋ ka ʋ ŋaa pɩ mɩ bʋntaŋŋaba ma.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Mɩ sɩ nyʋa dɛrɛ, mɩ pɩɛsʋ, ‘Ŋ ŋaa kʋbɛɛ tolo nɛ?’ Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋl, ‘Mɩ ŋaa Bɛtʋɛl tolo nɛ, Nahɔ arɩ ʋ haala Mɩlɩka nɛ lʋl Bɛtʋɛl.’ Ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ joŋ nenii-pɩrɩŋ a hɛ ʋ mɩɩsɩŋ aŋ joŋ kagunsuŋ a hɛ ʋ nɩsaa.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ tuu kpirimi, a chʋɔlɛ La TƖƖNA. Mɩ dɛnnɛ La TƖƖNA, mɩ nɩhɩaŋ Abɩraham Wɩa, Wɩa-la sɩ kɛmmɛ kaa vɛŋ wombii-zɔmʋŋ deeŋ mɩ na mɩ nɩhɩaŋ naambie nihiŋ dɩ mɩ pɩ mɩ nɩhɩaŋ nanʋala.

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: Dɩ ma nɛ jaŋ dagɛ zile arɩ wʋtɩtɩɩ a pɩ mɩ nɩhɩaŋ, má bʋla pɩmɛ, dɩ ŋɩɩ ma dee, má bʋla pɩmɛ dɩ mɩ jɩŋ wombii-la mɩ sɩ jaŋ to.”

ref: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ arɩ Bɛtʋɛl bʋl, “Wɩɩŋ deeŋ lɩɩ La TƖƖNA teeŋ nɛ, la bɩ jaŋ wuo kaa lɛrɛ.

ref: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”
text: Na Rɩbɛka nɛ ŋ-la, kɛnʋ kaa mʋ dɩ ʋ ŋaa ŋ nɩhɩaŋ nanʋala haala, arɩ La TƖƖNA sɩ dagɛ ŋɩɩ.”

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: aŋ lɩɩsɛ sɛlɩmɛ arɩ hɔpʋla kpɩɛraa arɩ gɛnnɩŋ a joŋo pɩ Rɩbɛka, a bɩra joŋ kpɩɛraa dɔnsʋŋ ma sɩ yuŋŋie a pɩ Rɩbɛka naaŋ arɩ ʋ naambie Labaŋ.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ka Abɩraham tɩntɩnnʋ-la arɩ ʋ harɩ-tooroo kala dii kɩaa aŋ nyʋa a chʋa nɩmɛ. Ba sɩ sie chʋchʋɔŋ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋl, “Má leŋ dɩ mɩ mɩɩra mʋ mɩ nɩhɩaŋ teeŋ.”

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rɩbɛka dɩbaala arɩ ʋ naaŋ bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ tolo-la hɛ la teeŋ tapʋlaa fii aŋ ka dɩ ma-na mʋ.”

ref: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
text: Ama ʋ bʋla pɩba, “Má sɩ́ leŋe dɩ mɩ dieni, La TƖƖNA sɩ leŋe mɩ wombiiŋ zɔŋ. Ma hɛla wombiiŋ lɛ dɩ mɩ wuo mʋ mɩ nɩhɩaŋ teeŋ.”

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ la yɩrɛ tolo-la a pɩɛsʋ na.”

ref: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
text: Ba yɩrɛ Rɩbɛka a pɩɛsʋ, “Ŋ jaŋ to baala deeŋ harɩŋ mʋ?” Ʋ bʋl, “Mɩ jaŋ mʋ.”

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba hɛ Rɩbɛka arɩ nɩɩ-la sɩɩ benu wombiiŋ lɛ, a pɛ Abɩraham tɩntɩnna arɩ ʋ nɩaa lɛ.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba sʋl Wɩa pɩ Rɩbɛka a bʋl, “La dɩhaala, Wɩa jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ pʋŋ a yi bʋɩba bʋɩ, a leŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa wuo ba dʋndɔŋŋɔɔ.”

ref: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rɩbɛka arɩ ʋ ketiee kɛŋ ba tɩtɩa hɛ dɔŋɔ a jɩl ba bʋntaŋŋaba a to Abɩraham tɩntɩnna arɩ ʋ nɩaa harɩŋ ba mʋ.

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: aŋ pɩɛsɛ Abɩraham tɩntɩnnʋ-la, “Kʋbɛɛ nɛ baala deeŋ sɩɩ chenla pɔkɔŋ deeŋ nyuŋ ŋɩɩ?” Abɩraham tɩntɩnnʋ-la bʋl, “Mɩ nɩhɩaŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rɩbɛka joŋ ʋ nyu-kpala a tɔ ʋ sɩaa.

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk kɛŋ Rɩbɛka ba jʋʋ ʋ naaŋ Sara lɛbɩŋ. Ʋ jaaʋ, ba cho dɔŋɔ woruŋ. Aɩzɩk naaŋ sʋʋŋ harɩŋ ʋ ha kɛŋ kenyiri a tɩŋ ʋ sɩ jaa Rɩbɛka wɩaa.

ref: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text: Ʋ dɩ Abɩraham nɛ lʋl biiriŋ deemba: Ziraŋ, Jokisaŋ, Medaŋ, Midiaŋ, Isibak arɩ Sʋa.

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Jokisaŋ ma lʋl Sɛba arɩ Dɛdaŋ, Dɛdaŋ doho nɩaa nɛ Asu tɩŋŋaa, Letusi tɩŋŋaa arɩ Lumi tɩŋŋaa.

ref: Midian also had five sons: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.
text: Midiaŋ ma hambolibiisiŋ nɛ: Ɛfa, Ɛfɛ, Hanɔk, Abɩda arɩ Ɛlɩdaa. Nɩaa deemba kala ŋaa Ketura doho nɩaa nɛ.

ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: Abɩraham fa ha sɩ hɛ ʋ mɩɩsɩŋ lɛ, ʋ joŋ kɩaa a pɩ ʋ haala-la dɔnsʋŋ ʋ sɩ bile aŋ bɩ jaa biiriŋ ma nɛ, aŋ leŋ ba lɩɩ a mʋ hɛ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ dɩɩrɩŋ, aŋ ka Aɩzɩk.

ref: Abraham died at the ripe old age of
text: Ʋ fa hɩɛsa nɛ woruŋ aŋ-na sʋʋ.

ref: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
text: Abɩraham sɩ sʋba, Wɩa pɛ ʋ bie Aɩzɩk lɛ woruŋ. Bʋa-la lɛ dɩ ʋ hɔnɔ kpagɛ lɩɩ-bulo-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Bɛɛ-Lahaɩ-Roi lɛ. Ɩsɩmɛl hambolibiisiŋ

ref: Ishmael was the son of Abraham and Hagar, the slave woman of Sarah.
text: Ijipiti tɩŋŋaa tolo Haga arɩ Abɩraham nɛ lʋl Ɩsɩmɛl. Haga ŋaa Sara ketiiŋ nɛ ʋ joŋo pɩ ʋ bala Abɩraham. Ɩsɩmɛl doho nɩaa nɛ ŋ-la.

ref: Ishmael had twelve sons, in this order: Nebaioth, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text: Ɩsɩmɛl hambolibiisiŋ yɩraa a chɩŋ ʋ bii-hɩaŋ lɛ a kaa tuu nɛ: Nɛbayɔf, Kɛda, Adɩbɛɛl, Mibisam,

ref: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
text: Mɩsɩma, Duma, Masa,

ref: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text: Hadad, Tɛma, Jetuu, Nafɩs arɩ Kɛdɛma.

ref: Each of Ishmael's sons was a tribal chief, and a village was named after each of them.
text: Ɩsɩmɛl hambolibiisiŋ yɩraa nɛ ŋɩɩ, ba kʋ ŋaa doho doho fii arɩ balɩa nɛ a hɔŋ hɔŋ arɩ ba nɩhɩasɩŋ.

ref: Ishmael had settled in the land east of his brothers, and his sons settled everywhere from Havilah to Shur, east of Egypt on the way to Asshur. Ishmael was
text: Ʋ doho nɩaa hɔŋ Havɩla lɛ a saa ŋɩɩ mʋ yi Suu a kpagɛ Ijipiti tinteeŋ bʋa dɩ ŋ nɩɩ mʋ Asiiria tinteeŋ. Ba fa tɛrɛ nɛ woruŋ ba naambiiriŋ teeŋ. Ba lʋl Jekɔb arɩ Ɛsɔ

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Aɩzɩk haala fa bɩ lʋl, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ sʋl Wɩa pʋ. Wɩa laa ʋ sʋla, ʋ haala kɛŋ luoruŋ.

ref: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ hambiisi-la yɩyɩ dɔŋɔ ʋ luoruŋ tʋɔŋ. Ʋ pɩɛsɛ La TƖƖNA, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ wɩɩŋ deeŋ ŋɩŋaa mɩ lɛ?”

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: La TƖƖNA bʋla pʋ, “Biiriŋ balɩa nɛ hɛ ŋ tʋɔŋ, ba jaŋ sii ŋaa tinteyee balɩa, ba bɩ jaŋ suguli dɔŋɔ lɛ, kʋbie nɛ jaŋ dolie kɩɩ kʋhɩaŋ. Kʋhɩaŋ jaŋ tɩma pɩ kʋbie.”

ref: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
text: Bile sɩ laa sɩpaaŋ lɩɩ ŋaa bii-fɩaŋ nɛ. Ʋ yaraa kala ŋaa pʋnaa a nagɛ gɛrɩŋ sɩ kɛŋ pʋnaa, ŋɩɩ nɛ ba hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Ɛsɔ.

ref: The second baby grabbed on to his brother's heel, so they named him Jacob. Isaac was
text: Ʋ ŋɩɩ harɩŋ lɛ, lɩa-la tɩɩna kʋ lɩɩ a kɛŋ Ɛsɔ naatilimiŋ lɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ba hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Jekɔb. Aɩzɩk fa ŋaa jɩsɩŋ mahɩŋ batori nɛ, ka Rɩbɛka-na lʋl daalɩarɩŋ deemba.

ref: As Jacob and Esau grew older, Esau liked the outdoors and became a good hunter, while Jacob lived the quiet life of a shepherd.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ biiri-la waa, Ɛsɔ ŋaa nambagɩl-wajɩa a hɛ gɩrɩŋ tʋɔŋ, ka Jekɔb ma ŋaa nɩchɩmchɩm a hɛ dɩa lɛ.

ref: Esau would take the meat of wild animals to his father Isaac, so Isaac loved him more, but Jacob was his mother's favorite son.
text: Aɩzɩk fa cho Ɛsɔ a tɩŋ ʋ sɩ cho baga nammɩa wɩaa, ka Rɩbɛka ma cho Jekɔb.

ref: One day, when Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came home hungry
text: Tapʋlɩɩ kʋbala Ɛsɔ lɩɩ gɩrɩŋ kɔ, losuŋ kɛnʋ woruŋ, dɩ Jekɔb ka ŋaa kʋdiilee.

ref: and said, “I'm starving to death! Here and now give me some of that red stew!” That's how Esau got the name “Edom.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ bʋla pɩ Jekɔb, “Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, mɩɩ sʋʋ losuŋ nɛ, ŋaa lɩma a pɩmɛ kʋdiile-la dɩ mɩ dii.” Ʋ bʋbʋɔŋ nɛ kʋfɩaŋ.

ref: Jacob replied, “Sell me your rights as the first-born son.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb sɩ, “Laa sɩpaaŋ joŋ ŋ hɩasɩŋ lenni mɩ hambiiriŋ, ka dɩ mɩ-na pɩŋ kʋdiile-la.”

ref: “I'm about to die,” Esau answered. “What good will those rights do me?”
text: Ɛsɔ sɩ, “Bee-na, mɩɩ chɛ dɩ mɩ sʋʋ nɛ arɩ losuŋ, bɛɛ kʋŋ wʋzɔŋ nɛ hɩasɩŋ deeŋ jaŋ ŋaa pɩmɛ?”

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋl, “Ŋmɩɛsa pɩmɛ arɩ ŋ joŋ ŋ hɩasɩŋ lenni mɩ hambiiriŋ nɛ.” Ɛsɔ ŋmɩɛsɛ a joŋ ʋ hɩasɩŋ lenni Jekɔb hambiiriŋ.

ref: Jacob then gave Esau some bread and some of the bean stew, and when Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and left, showing how little he thought of his rights as the first-born.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb ŋaa kʋdiile-la arɩ boroboro pʋ. Ʋ dii aŋ nyʋa, aŋ sii viiri. Ɛsɔ sɩ joŋ ʋ hɩasɩŋ lenni hambiiriŋ ŋɩɩ, ʋ muuri ʋ tɩtɩa nɛ.

ref: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
text: Losuŋ bɩra jʋʋ tintee-la, arɩ Abɩraham mɩɩsɩŋ lɛ losuŋ fa sɩ jʋʋ ŋɩɩ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk sii mʋ Gɛra tinteeŋ, Fɩlɩsɩtɩa tɩŋŋaa kuoro Abɩmɛlɛk teeŋ.

ref: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa dagɛ Aɩzɩk a bʋla pʋ, “Sɩ́ Ijipiti tinteeŋ mʋ, hɔŋ lee-la mɩ sɩ jaŋ dagɩŋ.

ref: You will live there as a foreigner, but I will be with you and bless you. I will keep my promise to your father Abraham by giving this land to you and your descendants.
text: Hɔŋ tinteeŋ deeŋ lɛ mʋa, mɩ jaŋ chɩŋ ŋ harɩŋ a pɛ ŋ lɛ. Ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa nɛ mɩ jaŋ joŋ leriŋ deeŋ pa arɩ mɩ sɩ bil niiŋ pɩ ŋ nyɩmma Abɩraham ŋɩɩ.

ref: I will give you as many descendants as there are stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of this land. They will be a blessing to every nation on earth,
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ a nagɛ chɛnwʋlaa sɩ hɛ wɩa-nyuŋ a joŋ leriŋ deeŋ kala pɩba. Ba lɛ nɛ mɩ jaŋ to pɛ dʋnɩa nɩaa kala lɛ,

ref: Isaac moved to Gerar
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk hɔŋ Gɛra tinteeŋ lɛ.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Taŋ-la nɩaa sɩ pɩɛsʋ ʋ haala wɩaa, fawʋllʋŋ kɛnʋ. Ʋ bʋl dɩ ʋ dɩhaala nɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa ʋ bɩɩna ba jaŋ kpʋʋ a tɩŋ Rɩbɛka sɩ zɔmɔ wɩaa.

ref: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
text: Aɩzɩk sɩ hɔŋ nɩmɛ a dieni, tapʋlɩɩ kʋbala Fɩlɩsɩtɩa tɩŋŋaa kuoro Abɩmɛlɛk to tokoro a na Aɩzɩk arɩ Rɩbɛka dɩ baa tɩrɩgʋ.

ref: Abimelech called him in and said, “Rebekah must be your wife! Why did you say she is your sister?” “Because I thought someone would kill me,” Isaac answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk yɩrɛ Aɩzɩk a bʋla pʋ, “Ŋ haala nɛ tolo-la, bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ bʋl dɩ ŋ dɩhaala nɛ?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk bʋla pʋ, “Mɩ fa bɩɩna arɩ ma jaŋ kpʋmɛ dɩ mɩ nɛ sɩ mɩ haala nɛ.”

ref: “Don't you know what you've done?” Abimelech exclaimed. “If someone had slept with her, you would have made our whole nation guilty!”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk pɩɛsʋ, “Bɛɛ nɛ ŋ ŋaa la lɛ ŋ-la? Baalaa deemba dɔŋɔ fa jaŋ wuo kɛŋ ŋ haal-la pɩŋ dɩ ʋ ŋaa disiniŋ a pɩla.”

ref: Then Abimelech warned his people that anyone who even touched Isaac or Rebekah would be put to death.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk bʋla pɩ ʋ nɩaa kala, “Nɩɩ-la kala sɩ jaŋ walɩmɛ baala deeŋ koo ʋ haala, mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ba kpʋʋ.”

ref: Isaac planted grain and had a good harvest that same year. The Lord blessed him,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk bil baga tintee-la lɛ a duu kɩaa. Jɩna-la tɩtɩa ʋ na kɩaa woruŋ, bɛɛ wɩaa La TƖƖNA pɛ ʋ lɛ nɛ.

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk dʋa kɩaa, kɩalɩŋ pʋŋ ŋɩɩ halɩɩ ʋ kʋ kɛŋ kɩaa woruŋ.

ref: In fact, the Philistines were jealous of the large number of sheep, goats, and slaves that Isaac owned,
text: A tɩŋ ʋ fa sɩ kɛŋ piesee, nɛsɩŋ arɩ tɩntɩnnaa yʋgɛ ŋɩɩ, Fɩlɩsɩtɩa tɩŋŋaa yaraa bibieri ʋ nyuŋ.

ref: and they stopped up the wells that Abraham's servants had dug before his death.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Fɩlɩsɩtɩa tɩŋŋaa paa hagɩla tɔ vile-la kala Aɩzɩk nyɩmma Abɩraham fa sɩ ŋaa ʋ tɩntɩnnaa lu.

ref: Finally, Abimelech said, “Isaac, I want you to leave our country. You have become too powerful to stay here.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk bʋla pɩ Aɩzɩk, “Sii la tinteeŋ lɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa ŋ dolie kɩɩla nɛ.”

ref: Isaac left and settled in Gerar Valley,
text: Aɩzɩk sii lee-la a mʋ hɛ ʋ lɛbɩŋ Gɛra fuonaaŋ lɛ a hɔŋ nɩmɛ.

ref: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk bɩra chaarɛ vile-la kala ʋ nyɩmma Abɩraham fa sɩ luo Fɩlɩsɩtɩa tɩŋŋaa paa hagɩla tɔ ʋ nyɩmma sʋʋŋ harɩŋ lɛ, aŋ bɩra hɛ yɩra-la tɩtɩa ʋ nyɩmma fa sɩ hɛ pɩ vile-la.

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk tɩntɩnnaa lu viliŋ fuonaa-la lɛ a na lɩɩzɔmʋŋ nɩmɛ.

ref: But the shepherds of Gerar Valley quarreled with Isaac's shepherds and claimed the water belonged to them. So this well was named “Quarrel,” because they had quarreled with Isaac.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Gɛra tɩŋŋaa kʋdaaraa kɛŋ Aɩzɩk kʋdaaraa yuo yuosuŋ a bʋl dɩ ba nɛ tɩɩ vil-la. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk hɛ vil-la yɩrɩŋ Ɛsɛk. Ɛsɛk bʋbʋɔŋ nɛ: ba kaa yuo yuosuŋ nɛ.

ref: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba bɩra lu dɔŋɔ, ba bɩra yuo yuosuŋ ʋ ma wɩaa, ʋ bɩra hɛ ʋ ma yɩrɩŋ Sɩtɩna. Sɩtɩna bʋbʋɔŋ nɛ: dʋndɔnnʋŋ.

ref: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ sii nɩmɛ a bɩra mʋ lu viliŋ dɔŋɔ. Ba bɩ ʋ-na wɩaa kaa yuo dɔŋɔ. Ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Rehobɔf aŋ bʋl, “La TƖƖNA pɩla leriŋ nɛ dɩ la hɔŋ arɩ fɩɛlɩŋ a dʋa.”

ref: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
text: Aɩzɩk sii nɩmɛ a bɩra mʋ Bɛɛsɩba tinteeŋ.

ref: where the Lord appeared to him that night and told him, “Don't be afraid! I am the God who was worshiped by your father Abraham, my servant. I will be with you and bless you, and because of Abraham I will give you many descendants.”
text: Tɩtaŋ-la tɩtɩa nɛ Wɩa kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa kʋ dagʋ aŋ bʋl, “Mɩ nɛ ŋaa ŋ nyɩmma Abɩraham Wɩa. Sɩ́ leŋe dɩ fawʋllʋŋ kɛnɩŋ. Mɩ hɛ ŋ harɩŋ, mɩ jaŋ pɛ ŋ lɛ a leŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ a tɩŋ mɩ tɩntɩnna Abɩraham wɩaa.”

ref: Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. Then he set up camp, and his servants started digging a well.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ nɩmɛ aa yɩrɛ La TƖƖNA yɩrɩŋ aŋ hɛ ʋ lɛbɩŋ. Ʋ tɩntɩnnaa ma lu viliŋ nɩmɛ.

ref: Meanwhile, Abimelech had left Gerar and was taking his advisor Ahuzzath and his army commander Phicol to see Isaac.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk arɩ Ahuzaf, nɩɩ-la sɩ bʋl wɩaa chɩchagɩlʋ, arɩ ʋ laalɩyuori-hɩaŋ Fiko lɩɩ Gɛra tinteeŋ a mʋ dɩ ba na Aɩzɩk.

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk pɩɛsɛba, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ maa kɔ dɩ ma namɛ lɛɛlɛ? Ma fa bɩ mɩ wɩaa cho a kirimi ma jaŋ lɛ.”

ref: They answered, “We now know for certain that the Lord is with you, and we have decided there needs to be a peace treaty between you and us. So let's make a solemn agreement
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba bʋl, “La na arɩ La TƖƖNA chɩŋ ŋ harɩŋ nɛ woruŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ laa chɛ dɩ la bil niiŋ la dɩŋ pɛgɛ, a ŋmɩɛsa pɩ dɔŋɔ

ref: not to harm each other. Remember, we have never hurt you, and when we sent you away, we let you go in peace. The Lord has truly blessed you.”
text: arɩ ŋ bɩ jaŋ ŋaala wʋbɔŋ, arɩ la sɩ bɩŋ wʋbɔŋ ŋaa ŋɩɩ aŋ kiriŋ sɔm, ama lɛɛlɛ La TƖƖNA pɛ ŋ lɛ nɛ.”

ref: Isaac gave a big feast for them, and everyone ate and drank.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk ŋaa kʋdiilee ba gbiele pɩba, ba dii aŋ nyʋa.

ref: Early the next morning Isaac and the others made a solemn agreement, then he let them go in peace.
text: Taŋ sɩ pʋla chʋchʋɔŋ, ba ŋmɩɛsa pɩ dɔŋɔ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk hɛba wombiiŋ lɛ, ba leŋu ʋ hɔŋ arɩ yarɩfɩɛlaa.

ref: Later that same day Isaac's servants came and said, “We've struck water!”
text: Tapʋlɩɩ-la nɛ Aɩzɩk tɩntɩnnaa kʋ bʋl vil-la ba sɩ luo wɩaa pʋ, “La yi lɩɩŋ lɛ nɛ.”

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Ʋ hɛ vil-la yɩrɩŋ Sɩba. Vil-la sɩ hɛ taŋ-la lɛ ma yɩrɩŋ nɛ Bɛɛsɩba. Arɩ jɩnɩŋ taŋ-la yɩrɩŋ nɛ ha ŋɩɩ.

ref: But these two women brought a lot of grief to Esau's parents Isaac and Rebekah.
text: Haalaa deemba bɩ leŋe Aɩzɩk arɩ Rɩbɛka tʋɔŋ pɩŋ dɩ-bala.

ref: Isaac was old and almost blind, when he called in his first-born son Esau, who asked him, “Father, what can I do for you?”
text: Aɩzɩk sɩ hɩɛsa, ʋ sɩɩ-poloo yʋɔrɛ, ʋ bɩra bɩ na. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ yɩrɛ ʋ nanʋa-hɩaŋ Ɛsɔ dɩ ʋ kɔ. Ɛsɔ kɔ a bʋla pɩ ʋ nyɩmma, “Namɛ.”

ref: Isaac replied, “I am old and might die at any time.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk bʋl, “Mɩ lɛɛlɛ hɩɛsa nɛ. Mɩ saa bɩ jɩŋ tapʋlɩɩ-la mɩ sɩ jaŋ sʋʋ.

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Joŋ ŋ toŋ arɩ huoŋ a mʋ gɩrɩŋ a kpʋ nammɩa a kaa kɔ

ref: Rebekah had been listening, and as soon as Esau left to go hunting,
text: Aɩzɩk sɩ bʋl wɩɩŋ deeŋ pɩpɩ ʋ bie Ɛsɔ dɩ Rɩbɛka ka jegilu. Ɛsɔ lɩɩ mʋ nambagɩlɩŋ dɩ ʋ kpʋ nammɩa kaa kɔ.

ref: she said to Jacob, “I heard your father tell Esau
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rɩbɛka bʋla pɩ ʋ bie Jekɔb a bʋl, “Mɩ nɩa arɩ ŋ nyɩmma bʋla pɩpɩ ŋ malɩma Ɛsɔ

ref: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.
text: dɩ ʋ mʋ kpʋ bagɩla a kaa kʋ ŋaa kʋdiile-la ʋ sɩ cho pʋ dɩ ʋ dii aŋ sʋl Wɩa pʋ, aŋ-na sʋʋ.

ref: Now, my son, listen carefully to what I want you to do.
text: Mɩ bii, jegile nɩɩ mɩ teeŋ woruŋ a ŋaa wɩɩ-la mɩ sɩɩ chɛ dɩ mɩ bʋla pɩŋ.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Jʋʋ bʋ-dɩa a kɛsɛ bʋ-bii-zɔŋŋɔɔ balɩa a kaa kɔ dɩ mɩ kpʋ a ŋaa kʋdiile-la ŋɩɩ ŋ nyɩmma sɩ cho,

ref: Then you can take it to him, so he can eat it and give you his blessing before he dies.”
text: dɩ ŋ kaa jʋʋ pʋ, ʋ jaŋ dii aŋ sʋl Wɩa pɩŋ aŋ-na sʋʋ.”

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋla pɩ ʋ naaŋ Rɩbɛka a bʋl, “Mɩ naambie Ɛsɔ kɛŋ pʋnaa nɛ, ka mɩ bɩ pʋnaa kɛnɛ.

ref: If my father touches me and realizes I am trying to trick him, he will put a curse on me instead of giving me a blessing.”
text: Dɩ mɩ nyɩmma nɛ digimi, ʋ jaŋ jɩŋ arɩ mɩ kɔ dɩ mɩ nyɩsʋ nɛ. Ʋ sɩ jaŋ sʋl Wɩa pɩmɛ, ʋ jaŋ mɩɩra ŋmɩɛsa hɛ mɩ lɛ.”

ref: Rebekah insisted, “Let his curse fall on me! Just do what I say and bring me the meat.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ naaŋ bʋla pʋ, “Mɩ bii leŋ dɩ ŋmɩaŋ-la diimi. Ŋaa wɩɩ-la mɩ sɩ bʋla pɩŋ. Mʋ kɛsɛ bʋ-bii-zɔmɔ-la balɩa kaa kʋ pɩmɛ.”

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ kɛsɛ kaa kʋ pʋ. Ʋ kpʋ a ŋaa kʋdiile-la ʋ nyɩmma sɩ cho.

ref: Then she took Esau's best clothes and put them on Jacob.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rɩbɛka paa Ɛsɔ, ʋ nanʋa-hɩaŋ gɛrɩ-zɔŋŋɔɔ sɩ hɛ dɩa lɛ a laalɛ ʋ ŋaana Jekɔb

ref: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
text: a paa bʋ-tenniŋ a vɔɔ ʋ nɩsaa arɩ ʋ baŋŋaa sɩ ŋaa mʋla mʋla lɛ.

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rɩbɛka joŋ kʋdii sɩma-la arɩ boroboro ʋ sɩ ŋaa a pɩ ʋ nanʋala Jekɔb.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Jekɔb kɛŋ kʋdiile-la mʋ ʋ nyɩmma teeŋ a bʋl, “Mɩ nyɩmma namɛ.” Ʋ nyɩmma pɩɛsʋ, “Kʋbɛɛ nɛ?”

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋla pɩ ʋ nyɩmma, “Mɩ-na Ɛsɔ nɛ, ŋ nanʋa-hɩaŋ, mɩ ŋaa wɩɩ-la ŋ sɩ sɩ mɩ ŋaa nɛ. Sii hɔŋ a dii kʋdiile-la ŋ sɩ cho aŋ sʋl Wɩa pɩmɛ.”

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk pɩɛsɛ ʋ nanʋala, “Ɛɛ nɛ ŋ ŋaa a naʋ lɩma ŋ-la?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋl, “La TƖƖNA, ŋ Wɩa nɛ pɛ mɩ lɛ.”

ref: “My son,” Isaac said, “come closer, where I can touch you and find out if you really are Esau.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk bʋla pɩ Jekɔb, “Kʋ yimi dɩ mɩ digiŋ na ŋ nɛ ŋaa Ɛsɔ koo Ɛsɔ dee.”

ref: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb mʋ yi ʋ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk ʋ digu aŋ bʋl, “Yikoro deeŋ nagɛ Jekɔb yikoro nɛ, aŋ ka nɩsaa nagɛ Ɛsɔ nɩsaa.”

ref: Isaac asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “Yes, I am,” Jacob answered.
text: Aŋ bɩra pɩɛsʋ, “Ŋ nɛ sɛnɛ ŋaa mɩ nanʋala Ɛsɔ?” Ʋ bʋl, “Mɩ nɛ.”

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk bʋl, “Mɩ bii, kɛŋ nammɩa-la dɔnsʋŋ kaa kʋ yimi dɩ mɩ dii, aŋ sʋl Wɩa pɩŋ.” Jekɔb joŋ nammɩa-la pʋ ʋ chaŋ, a joŋ tɩɩnɛnɛɛ sɩnɩŋ ma pʋ, ʋ nyʋa.

ref: Then Isaac said, “Son, come over here and kiss me.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk bʋla pʋ, “Kʋ yimi mɩ bii a guorimi.”

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Ʋ mʋ yiu a guoru. Aɩzɩk sɩ nɩɩ ʋ gɛnnɩŋ sʋɔra, ʋ sʋl Wɩa pʋ a bʋl, “Mɩ nanʋala sʋɔra sɩma a nagɛ baga-la La TƖƖNA sɩ leŋe ʋ wasɛ nɛ.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Wɩa jaŋ leŋ dɩ wɩa-nyuŋ nyal-zɔmʋŋ nyal ŋ baga lɛ dɩ ʋ sɩŋ, a leŋ dɩ ŋ na kʋdiilee yʋgɛ arɩ tɩɩnɛnɛɛ sɩnɩŋ.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Wɩa jaŋ leŋ dɩ dʋnɩa sɛmɛ na-kala nɩaa kpikpirimi ŋ sɩpaaŋ aŋ tɩmaa pɩŋ a leŋ dɩ ŋ bibeŋ ŋ naambiiriŋ, dɩ ba kpikpirimi ŋ sɩpaaŋ. Dɩ nʋʋ ma nɛ ŋmɩɛsa hɛ ŋ lɛ, dɩ ŋmɩaŋ-la dii ʋ tɩɩna. Dɩ nʋʋ ma nɛ sʋl wɩɩ-zɔmʋŋ pɩŋ, dɩ Wɩa pɛ tɩɩna ma lɛ.”

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Aɩzɩk yie sʋl Wɩa pɩ Jekɔb dɛrɛ, ʋ ŋaa dɩ ʋ lɩɩ dɩ ʋ naambie Ɛsɔ lɩɩ nambagɩlɩŋ kʋ jʋʋ.

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Ʋ ma bɩra ŋaa kʋdiile-la ʋ nyɩmma sɩ cho a kaa jʋʋ dɩ ʋ pɩ ʋ nyɩmma, aŋ bʋl, “Mɩ nyɩmma, sii hɔŋ a dii ŋ kɩalɩŋ aŋ sʋl Wɩa pɩmɛ.”

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Ʋ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk pɩɛsʋ, “Ŋ nɛ kʋbɛɛ?” Ɛsɔ bʋl, “Mɩ nɛ ŋ nanʋala, ŋ nanʋa-hɩaŋ Ɛsɔ.”

ref: Isaac started trembling and said, “Then who brought me some wild meat right before you came in? I ate it and gave him a blessing that cannot be taken back.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk faasa chel woruŋ aŋ pɩɛsɛ, “Kʋbɛɛ nɛ fa laa sɩpaaŋ kɛŋ baga nammɩa kʋ pɩmɛ ŋɩɩ? Mɩ dii kʋdiile-la nɛ, aŋ sʋl Wɩa pʋ, ka ŋ-na kʋ jʋʋ ŋɩɩ. Tɛŋɛ, Wɩa jaŋ pɛ nɩɩ-la lɛ.”

ref: Esau cried out in great distress, “Father, give me a blessing too!”
text: Ɛsɔ sɩ nɩɩ wɩɩ-la ʋ nyɩmma sɩ bʋla, ʋ chiesi a yel woruŋ, aŋ bʋla pɩ ʋ nyɩmma, “Sʋl Wɩa pɩmɛ ma, mɩ nyɩmma.”

ref: Isaac answered, “Your brother tricked me and stole your blessing.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋl, “Ŋ ŋaana kʋ nyɩsɛmɛ a laa ŋ Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ kala nɛ.”

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Ɛsɔ bʋl, “Ʋ yɩrɩŋ sɛnɛ magɛ dɩ ʋ dii Jekɔb. Bʋlɩa nɛ ŋ-la ʋ sɩ gʋrɩmɛ, ʋ laa sɩpaaŋ laa mɩ hɩɛsɩŋ aŋ bɩra laa mɩ Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ ma ŋ teeŋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ bɩra pɩɛsɛ, “Wɩɩsʋlɩɩ kala bɩra tuo, ŋ sɩ jaŋ sʋla pɩmɛ maa?”

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk bʋla pɩ Ɛsɔ, “Mɩ leŋe ʋ nɛ ŋaa kʋhɩaŋ ŋ lɛ, dɩ ʋ naambiiriŋ kala ŋaa ʋ tɩntɩnnaa, dɩ ʋ kɛŋ kʋdiilee arɩ tɩɩnɛnɛɛ sɩnɩŋ kala doluŋ. Mɩ bii, bɛɛ kʋŋ nɛ mɩ saa jaŋ bɩra wuo ŋaa pɩŋ?”

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ pɩɛsɛ ʋ nyɩmma, “Wɩɩ-sʋl-bala dʋndʋŋ nɛ ŋ kɛŋ? Mɩ nyɩmma, sʋl Wɩa pɩmɛ ma.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ faasa yel.

ref: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
text: Ʋ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk bʋla pʋ, “Lee-la ŋ sɩ jaŋ hɔŋ, ʋ tinteeŋ bɩ jaŋ zɔŋ, wɩa-nyuŋ fɩɛlɩŋ ma bɩ jaŋ yiŋ.

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: Ŋ jaŋ yuyuo laalɩŋ bʋa na-kala lɛ aŋ tɩma pɩpɩ ŋ naambie. Dɩ ʋ nɛ kʋ magɛ ŋɩɩ, ŋ jaŋ lɔl aŋ laa ŋ tɩtɩa ŋ naambie teeŋ.” Jekɔb fá mʋ Labaŋ teeŋ

ref: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
text: Ɛsɔ kɛŋ tʋɔŋ arɩ Jekɔb a tɩŋ Wɩɩsʋlɩɩ-la ʋ nyɩmma sɩ sʋla pʋ wɩaa a bʋl ʋ tʋɔŋ lɛ, “Ʋ ka mʋa ka dɩ mɩ nyɩmma yoho yi, dɩ ʋ nɛ dɛrɛ, mɩ jaŋ kpʋ mɩ ŋaana Jekɔb.”

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Rɩbɛka sɩ nɩɩ Ɛsɔ ʋ bii-hɩaŋ sɩ bʋl wɩaa deemba, ʋ tɩma yɩrɛ ʋ nanʋa-bie Jekɔb a bʋla pʋ, “Ɛsɔ kɛŋ ʋ tʋɔŋ dɩ ʋ kpʋŋ nɛ dɩ ʋ baanɩŋ fɩɛlɛ.

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: Ŋɩɩ wɩaa mɩ bii, nɩɩ mɩ teeŋ, sii a fá mʋ mɩ dɩbaala Labaŋ sɩ hɛ Haraŋ tinteeŋ teeŋ

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: a hɔŋ nɩmɛ dɩ ŋ naambie Ɛsɔ baanɩŋ fɩɛlɛ.

ref: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
text: Dɩ ŋ naambie baanɩŋ nɛ fɩɛla dɩ ʋ yʋ wɩɩ-la ŋ sɩ ŋaa ʋ lɛ, mɩ jaŋ tɩma yɩrɩŋ dɩ ŋ lɩɩ lee-la kɔ. Bɛɛ nɛ jaŋ tɩɩ dɩ ma kala balɩa lɛl mɩ nɩsɩŋ lɛ tapʋl-bala?”

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rɩbɛka bʋla pɩ Aɩzɩk, “Mɩ mɩɩsɩŋ fɩɩ Hiti tɩŋŋaa toloo deemba Ɛsɔ sɩ jaa wʋŋaalaa lɛ nɛ. Dɩ Jekɔb nɛ jaa Hiti toloo doho sɩ hɛ la tinteeŋ deeŋ lɛ, dɩ ba nagɛ haalaa deemba, mɩ mɩɩsɩŋ bɩ bʋbʋɔ kɛnɛ.”

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk yɩrɛ Jekɔb a sʋl Wɩa pʋ aŋ bʋla pʋ, “Pʋpɔ dɩ ŋ sɩ́ Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa tolo jaa.

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: Sii mʋ Mesopotemia tinteeŋ, ŋ naaŋ nyɩmma Bɛtʋɛl dɩa a jaa ŋ naaŋ dɩbaala Labaŋ toloo dɔŋɔ.

ref: I pray that God All-Powerful will bless you with many descendants and let you become a great nation.
text: Wɩa doluŋ kala Tɩɩna jaŋ pɛ ŋ lɛ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ a yʋgɛ.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: Wɩa jaŋ pɛ ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa lɛ arɩ ʋ sɩ pɛ Abɩraham lɛ ŋɩɩ. Tintee-la ʋ sɩ joŋo pɩ Abɩraham, ma lɛɛlɛ hɔŋ a ŋaa nʋhʋɔraa, ʋ jaŋ ŋaa ma kʋŋ.”

ref: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Aɩzɩk leŋ Jekɔb mʋ Mesopotemia tinteeŋ, Labaŋ teeŋ. Labaŋ nyɩmma Bɛtʋɛl ŋaa Siiria tɩɩna nɛ. Labaŋ dɩhaala nɛ Rɩbɛka. Rɩbɛka nɛ lʋl Jekɔb arɩ Ɛsɔ.

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Ɛsɔ sɩ kʋ jɩŋ dɩ Aɩzɩk sʋl Wɩa pɩ Jekɔb aŋ leŋ ʋ mʋ Mesopotemia dɩ ʋ jaa haala nɩmɛ, a bɩra jɩŋ arɩ ʋ sʋl Wɩa pʋ a bʋl, dɩ ʋ pɔ dɩ ʋ sɩ́ Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa tolo jaa,

ref: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.
text: a bɩra kʋ jɩŋ arɩ Jekɔb nɩɩ ʋ naaŋ arɩ ʋ nyɩmma niiŋ nɛ a mʋ Mesopotemia,

ref: Esau already had several wives, but he now realized how much his father hated the Canaanite women.
text: ʋ jɩma arɩ ʋ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk bɩ Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa toloo cho.

ref: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
text: Ʋ mʋ jaa Abɩraham nanʋala Ɩsɩmɛl tolo Mahalaf a pɛ haala-la lɛ ʋ sɩ kɛnɛ. Mahalaf ŋaa Nɛbayɔf dɩhaala nɛ. Jekɔb duoso Bɛtɛl lɛ

ref: Jacob left the town of Beersheba and started out for Haran.
text: Jekɔb lɩɩ Bɛɛsɩba a mʋmʋ Haraŋ tinteeŋ.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: Ʋ mʋ yi lee-kʋbala dɩ wɩa jʋa, ʋ chʋa lee-la. Ʋ joŋ tabɩɩŋ a bil a kɛŋ ʋ nyuŋ yelle pɩŋ doŋ nɩmɛ.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: Ʋ duoso na lusuŋ-chol duduoŋ kʋbala dɩ ʋ chɩŋ tinteeŋ aŋ jɩla tɩgɛ wɩa-nyuŋ. Wɩa tɩndaaraa to ʋ lɛ a tutuu aŋ jɩjɩl.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: Ka ʋ na La TƖƖNA dɩ ʋ chɩna a chuguli lusuŋ-chol-la nyuŋ a bʋl, “Mɩ nɛ ŋaa ma TƖƖNA, ma nyɩmma Abɩraham arɩ Aɩzɩk Wɩa. Mɩ jaŋ joŋ lee-la ŋ sɩ pɩna a pɩ ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: Ŋ doho nɩaa jaŋ pʋŋa nagɛ hagɩlɩ-biee a jɛrɛ su lee-kala a hɛ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ, wɩɩjʋʋlʋŋ arɩ taŋ-sɛsɩŋ kala. Ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa lɛ nɛ mɩ jaŋ to a pɛ dʋnɩa nɩaa kala lɛ.

ref: Wherever you go, I will watch over you, then later I will bring you back to this land. I won't leave you—I will do all I have promised.
text: Bee-na, mɩ chɩŋ ŋ harɩŋ nɛ aa beniŋ lee-la kala ŋ sɩɩ mʋ. Mɩ jaŋ kɛnɩŋ mɩɩra kɔ tinteeŋ deeŋ. Mɩ bɩ jaŋ leŋiŋ, see mɩ sɩ ŋaa wɩɩ-la mɩ sɩ sɩ mɩ jaŋ ŋaa pɩŋ.”

ref: Jacob woke up suddenly and thought, “The Lord is in this place, and I didn't even know it.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb hirige sii doŋ lɛ a bʋl ʋ tʋɔŋ lɛ, “Tɛŋɛ, La TƖƖNA hɛ daha aŋ ka mɩ fa bɩ jɩŋ.”

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Fawʋllʋŋ kɛnʋ, ʋ bʋl, “Ɛɛ nɛ leriŋ deeŋ kɛŋ fawʋllʋŋ ŋɩɩ? Leriŋ deeŋ bɩ lee-kala naga dɩ Wɩa dɩa dee, Wɩa-jaŋ wombiiŋ nɛ ŋ-la.”

ref: When Jacob got up early the next morning, he took the rock that he had used for a pillow and stood it up as a place of worship. Then he poured olive oil on the rock to dedicate it to God,
text: Ʋ taŋ sɩ pʋla chʋchʋɔ-fɩɛla, Jekɔb sii joŋ tabɩɩ-la ʋ sɩ yelle a pɛ ʋ ŋaa jebii-magɩl. Ʋ joŋ nʋʋŋ bɩsa wurisi.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ lee-la yɩrɩŋ Bɛtɛl, ama ba faa yɩrɛ lee-la Lus nɛ.

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Jekɔb ta niiŋ a bʋl, “Dɩ Wɩa nɛ jaŋ chɩŋ mɩ harɩŋ a pɔmɛ wombiiŋ deeŋ mɩ sɩ joŋo lɛ, a pɩmɛ kʋdiilee dɩ mɩ dii arɩ gɛnnɩŋ mɩ laalɛ,

ref: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
text: a leŋ dɩ mɩ mɩɩra kɔ mɩ nyɩmma dɩa arɩ yarɩfɩɛlaa, ŋɩɩ-na La TƖƖNA jaŋ ŋaa mɩ Wɩa.

ref: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
text: Tabɩɩŋ deeŋ mɩ sɩ joŋo pɛ ʋ ŋaa jebii-magɩl jaŋ ŋaa Wɩa dɩa. Kɩalɩŋ kala ʋ sɩ joŋo pɩmɛ, mɩ jaŋ kaa kpaa leree fii a joŋ kʋbala pʋ.”

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb vɛŋ wombiiŋ ŋɩɩ a mʋ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ dɩɩrɩŋ nɩaa teeŋ.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: Jekɔb sɩ bene, ʋ na viliŋ nɩmɛ, ka pie-laalɩŋ pɩŋ vil-la sɛmɛ bʋbʋɔ-bugoo batori, bɛɛ wɩaa vil-la lɩɩŋ nɛ piese-la nyʋnyʋa, ka tabɩɩ-jalɩŋ tɔ vil-la niiŋ.

ref: Shepherds would roll the rock away when all their sheep had gathered there. Then after the sheep had been watered, the shepherds would roll the rock back over the mouth of the well.
text: Dɩ pie-laal-la nɛ kʋ hɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ, pie-daaraa yie kɛŋ tabɩɩ-la suri nɛ a ŋaa lɩɩŋ pɩ piese-la ba nyʋa, dɩ ba nɛ nyʋa dɛrɛ ba bɩra kɛŋ tabɩɩ-la tɔ vil-la niiŋ.

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb pɩɛsɛ pie-daara-la, “Mɩ naambiiriŋ, nɩɩ nɛ ma lɩɩ?” Ba bʋl, “La lɩɩ Haraŋ tinteeŋ nɛ.”

ref: Then he asked, “Do you know Nahor's grandson Laban?” “Yes we do,” they replied.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ pɩɛsɛba, “Ma jɩŋ Nahɔ nihiŋ Labaŋ nɛɛ?” Ba bʋl, “La jɩmʋ nɛ.”

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bɩra pɩɛsɛba, “Ʋ yaraa fɩɛlaa?” Ba bʋl, “Ʋ yaraa fɩɛla nɛ. Na ʋ tolo Rakɛl nɛ kɛŋ ʋ piesee kʋkɔ ŋɩɩ.”

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Jekɔb bʋl, “Má bee na, wɩa ha yʋga nɛ arɩ ma sɩ jaŋ kiri piesee mʋ tɔ. Má leŋ dɩ ba nyʋa lɩɩŋ ka dɩ ma kire mʋ dɩ ba dii yaŋ.”

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba bʋl, “La bɩ jaŋ wuo ŋaa ŋɩɩ, see dɩ piesee kala kʋ hɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ dɩ la kɛŋ tabɩɩ-la suri vil-la niiŋ dɩ ba nyʋa lɩɩŋ.”

ref: While Jacob was still talking with the men, his cousin Rachel came up with her father's sheep.
text: Jekɔb arɩ pie-daara-la fa ha sɩɩ bʋl wɩaa ŋɩɩ, dɩ Rakɛl nɛ kiri ʋ nyɩmma piesee a kɔ ŋɩɩ. Rakɛl nɛ faa daa ʋ nyɩmma piesee.

ref: When Jacob saw her and his uncle's sheep, he rolled the rock away and watered the sheep.
text: Jekɔb sɩ na ʋ naaŋ dɩbaala Labaŋ tolo Rakɛl dɩ ʋ kɛŋ piesee kɔ, ʋ mʋ suri tabɩɩ-la vil-la niiŋ aŋ ŋaa lɩɩŋ pɩ ʋ nɩɛra piesee, ba nyʋa.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb guori Rakɛl aŋ yel, ʋ tʋɔŋ sɩ tɔrɔ.

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Jekɔb bʋla pɩ Rakɛl arɩ ʋ nɛ ŋaa ʋ nyɩmma naambie, Rɩbɛka nɛ ŋaa ʋ naaŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rakɛl fá mʋ bʋla pɩ ʋ nyɩmma Labaŋ.

ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Labaŋ sɩ nɩɩ wʋzɔmʋŋ deeŋ a tɩgɛ ʋ dɩhaala bie Jekɔb lɛ, ʋ fá mʋ chemu a guoru aŋ kɛnʋ kaa kɔ ʋ dɩa. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋl wɩalɩŋ kala sɩ ŋaa a pʋ.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Labaŋ bʋla pʋ, “Ŋ ŋaa mɩ chalɩŋ doho tɩtɩa nɛ.” Jekɔb hɛ ʋ teeŋ chɛŋ-piliŋ. Jekɔb jaa Liya arɩ Rakɛl

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ bʋla pʋ, “Ŋ sɩ ŋaa mɩ naambii ŋɩɩ, ŋɩɩ bɩ daga dɩ ŋ tɩma pɩmɛ pɛɩ. Baŋmɛɛ nɛ ŋ jaŋ laa?”

ref: Laban had two daughters. Leah was older than Rachel, but her eyes didn't sparkle, while Rachel was beautiful and had a good figure.
text: Liya sɩaa fa zɔmɔ, ama Rakɛl-na yaraa kala nɛ fa chaasa zɔŋ.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Jekɔb fa cho Rakɛl nɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋla pɩ Labaŋ, “Mɩ jaŋ tɩma pɩŋ jɩsɩŋ balɩpɛ aŋ laa ŋ toloo kʋbie Rakɛl.”

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ bʋl, “Dɩ mɩ nɛ joŋu pɩŋ nɛ kpɩa mɩ sɩ joŋu pɩ baal dɔŋ. Hɔŋ mɩ teeŋ daha.”

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb tɩŋ jɩsɩŋ balɩpɛ dɩ ʋ laa Rakɛl. A tɩŋ Jekɔb fa sɩ cho tolo-la ŋɩɩ, jɩsɩŋ balɩpɛ-la nagɛ tapʋlaa baŋmɛnɛ nɛ ʋ teeŋ.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋla pɩ Labaŋ, “Jɩsɩ-la yie nɛ. Joŋ mɩ haala pɩmɛ dɩ la pɛ bɔsɔ.”

ref: So Laban gave a big feast and invited all their neighbors.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ yɩrɛ nɩalɩŋ kala sɩ hɛ lee-la a hɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ a ŋaa kɩaa ba dii aŋ gbieli hajaarɩ-gbiele.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Dɩdaanɩŋ sɩ yie, Labaŋ kɛŋ ʋ tolo Liya mʋ pɩ Jekɔb ba pɛ bɔsɔ.

ref: Laban also gave Zilpah to Leah as her servant woman.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ joŋ ʋ dɩa tɩntɩnna Zilipa a daŋ ʋ tolo Liya ʋ ŋaa ʋ ketiiŋ.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Taŋ sɩ pʋla chʋchʋɔŋ Jekɔb sie dɩ Liya nɛ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ pɩɛsɛ Labaŋ, “Bɛɛ wɩɩ nɛ ŋ ŋaa mɩ lɛ ŋ-la? Rakɛl wɩaa dee mɩ tɩma pɩŋ, bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ nyɩsɛmɛ?”

ref: Laban replied, “In our country the older daughter must get married first.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ bʋl, “Ʋ ŋaa la kɩsɩŋ nɛ daha dɩ ŋ toli-bie laa sɩpaaŋ jaa bala ka dɩ kʋhɩaŋ hɔŋ.

ref: After you spend this week with Leah, you may also marry Rachel. But you will have to work for me another seven years.”
text: Leŋ dɩ hajaarɩ-gbiele deeŋ dɛrɛ yɔbɔ deeŋ lɛ, ka dɩ la joŋ kʋbie-la ma pɩŋ dɩ ŋ nɛ sɛɩ a bɩra tɩŋ jɩsɩŋ balɩpɛ pɩmɛ.”

ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Jekɔb arɩ Rakɛl ma bɩra pɛ bɔsɔ. Ʋ cho Rakɛl a kɩɩ Liya. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bɩra tɩŋ jɩsɩŋ balɩpɛ a pɩ Labaŋ. Jekɔb lʋl biiriŋ

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: La TƖƖNA sɩ na dɩ Jekɔb bɩ Liya cho, ʋ kɛŋ ʋ lʋla suri aŋ kɛŋ Rakɛl lʋla tɔ.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Liya kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Rubiŋ, bɛɛ wɩaa ʋ sɩ, “La TƖƖNA na mɩ hɛɛŋ nɛ. Lɛɛlɛ mɩ bala jaŋ chomi.”

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Ʋ bɩra kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie a bʋl, “A tɩŋ Wɩa sɩ na arɩ mɩ bala bɩ mɩ cho, ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ ʋ bɩra pɩmɛ bie deeŋ ma.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Simioŋ.

ref: When Leah's third son was born, she said, “Now my husband will hold me close.” So this son was named Levi.
text: Ʋ bɩra kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie aŋ bʋl, “Bʋa deeŋ-na lɛ, mɩ bala jaŋ kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa mɛrɛ mɩ lɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa mɩ lʋl hambolibiisiŋ batori nɛ pʋ.” Ʋ hɛ ʋ ma yɩrɩŋ Levi.

ref: She had one more son and named him Judah, because she said, “I'll praise the Lord!”
text: Ʋ bɩra kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie. Bʋa deeŋ lɛ ʋ sɩ, “Mɩ jaŋ dɛnnɛ mɩ Tɩɩna.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Juda, aŋ leŋ biiriŋ lʋlʋŋ.

ref: Rachel was very jealous of Leah for having children, and she said to Jacob, “I'll die if you don't give me some children!”
text: Rakɛl sɩ na arɩ ʋ bɩ bii lʋla pɩ Jekɔb, ʋ yaraa bibieri ʋ malɩma nyuŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋla pɩ Jekɔb, “Pɩmɛ bie, dɩ ŋɩɩ dee mɩ jaŋ sʋʋ.”

ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb baanɩŋ sii Rakɛl nyuŋ ʋ bʋl, “Mɩ nɛ ŋaa Wɩa a kɛŋ ŋ lʋlá tɔ dɩ ŋ sɩ́ biiriŋ lʋl?”

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rakɛl bʋl, “Na mɩ ketiiŋ Biliha nɛ ŋ-la, leŋ dɩ ma pɛ bɔsɔ dɩ ʋ lʋl biiriŋ, dɩ mɩ ma to ʋ lɛ a kɛŋ doho.”

ref: and they had a son.
text: Biliha kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie pɩ Jekɔb.

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rakɛl bʋl, “Wɩa laa mɩ sʋla nɛ, ʋ jegile nɩɩ mɩ bunso nɛ a pɩmɛ hambolibie.” Ŋɩɩ wɩaa, ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Daŋ.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Rakɛl ketiiŋ Biliha bɩra kɛŋ luoruŋ lɩamɩŋ lɛ, a lʋl hambolibie pɩ Jekɔb.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rakɛl bʋl, “Mɩ dɩ mɩ malɩma Liya lobo nɛ woruŋ, ama mɩ lobo diiu nɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ bile yɩrɩŋ Nafɩtalɩ.

ref: When Leah realized she could not have any more children, she let Jacob marry her servant Zilpah,
text: Liya sɩ na dɩ ʋ bɩra bɩ lʋl, ʋ joŋ ʋ ketiiŋ Zilipa a pɩ Jekɔb ʋ ŋaa ʋ haala.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Liya ketiiŋ Zilipa lʋl hambolibie pɩ Jekɔb.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Liya bʋl, “Mɩ nyuŋ sɩma nɛ,” ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Gad.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Zilipa bɩra lʋl hambolibii-lɩamɩŋ pɩ Jekɔb.

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Liya bʋl, “Na mɩ tʋɔŋ sɩ tɔrɛ ŋɩɩ, haalaa jaŋ yɩrɛmɛ tʋɔtɔrɛ tɩɩna.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ bile yɩrɩŋ Asa.

ref: During the time of the wheat harvest, Reuben found some love flowers and took them to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah for some of them,
text: Bʋa-la lɛ ba sɩɩ kʋŋ mɛmpɛ, Rubiŋ mʋ baga a na tɩɩ-pugu-bie kʋbala ba sɩ kaa ŋaa lʋlá daalusuŋ, a kere kaa kʋ pɩ ʋ naaŋ Liya. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rakɛl bʋl, “Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, keri ŋ bie sɩ kɛŋ lʋlá daalusu-la kʋ pɩŋ mʋa pɩmɛ.”

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Liya bʋla pʋ, “Ŋ bɩɩna wʋbie nɛ ŋ ŋaa ŋɩɩ? Dɩ ŋ laa mɩ bala, aŋ bɩraa chɛ dɩ ŋ laa mɩ bie lʋlá daaŋ maa?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rakɛl bʋl, “Dɩ ŋ nɛ sɛyɛ a pɩmɛ ŋ bie lʋla daa-la, mɩ jaŋ leŋ ŋ dɩ Jekɔb pɛ bɔsɔ tɩtaŋɩŋ deeŋ.”

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Jekɔb sɩ lɩɩ baga kɔ dɩdaanɩŋ, Liya lɩɩ chemu a bʋl, “Mɩ laaŋ nɛ ŋ haala teeŋ jɩnɩŋ arɩ mɩ bie lʋlá daaŋ. Mɩ dɩŋ nɛ jaŋ pɩŋ tɩtaŋɩŋ deeŋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb kɛnʋ pɩŋ tɩtaŋ-la.

ref: and God answered Leah's prayers by giving her a fifth son.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa laa Liya sʋla ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl ʋ hambolibii-nɔŋ a pɩ Jekɔb.

ref: Leah shouted, “God has rewarded me for letting Jacob marry my servant,” and she named the boy Issachar.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Liya bʋl, “Wɩa timmi nɛ a tɩŋ mɩ sɩ joŋ mɩ ketiiŋ pɩ mɩ bala.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Ɩsaka.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Liya bʋl, “Wɩa joŋ kʋyuŋi nɛ a dii zile pɩmɛ. Bʋa deeŋ lɛ, mɩ bala jaŋ zizilmi, bɛɛ wɩaa mɩ lʋl hambolibiisiŋ balɩdʋ nɛ pʋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ bile ma yɩrɩŋ Zebuluŋ.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Ʋ ŋɩɩ harɩŋ lɛ ʋ bɩra kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hantolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Daana.

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie aŋ bʋl, “Wɩa tɔ mɩ hɩɩsɩŋ nɛ.”

ref: “I'll name the boy Joseph, and I'll pray that the Lord will give me another son.” Jacob and Laban
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ bile yɩrɩŋ Josɛf aŋ bʋl, “La TƖƖNA jaŋ pɛ dɩ mɩ bɩra lʋl hambolibie.” Jekɔb pʋsʋŋ pʋŋa

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Rakɛl sɩ lʋl Josɛf ʋ harɩŋ lɛ, Jekɔb bʋla pɩ Labaŋ, “Tamɛ dɩ mɩ mɩɩra mʋ mɩ jaŋ.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Joŋ mɩ haala-la mɩ sɩ tɩma laa ŋ teeŋ arɩ mɩ biiriŋ pɩmɛ dɩ mɩ viiri. Ŋ jɩŋ ŋɩɩ mɩ sɩ tɩma magɛ a pɩŋ nɛ.”

ref: Jacob answered: You've seen how hard I've worked for you, and you know how your flocks and herds have grown under my care.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋla pʋ, “Ŋ jɩŋ ŋɩɩ mɩ sɩ tɩma pɩŋ nɛ, ŋ pʋsʋŋ pʋŋ mɩ nɩsɩŋ lɛ nɛ.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: Pʋsʋŋ mʋa nɛ ŋ kɛŋ ka mɩ-na kɔ ŋ teeŋ, ama ba pʋŋa nɛ woruŋ. Wɩa pɛ ŋ lɛ nɛ ŋ dʋa, bʋa-la kala mɩ sɩ hɛ ŋ teeŋ. Ama lɛɛlɛ tamɛ dɩ mɩ mʋ a chɛ mɩ tɩtɩa kɩaa.”

ref: “How much do you want me to pay you?” Laban asked. Then Jacob told him: I don't want you to pay me anything. Just do one thing, and I'll take care of your sheep and goats.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ pɩɛsɛ, “Bɛɛ kʋŋ nɛ ŋ chɛ dɩ mɩ pɩŋ?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋl, “Mɩ bɩ time ka chɛ, ama dɩ ŋ nɛ jaŋ sɛɩ a ŋaa wɩɩŋ deeŋ pɩmɛ, mɩ ha jaŋ bibeŋ ŋ pʋsʋŋ.

ref: Let me go through your flocks and herds and take the sheep and goats that are either spotted or speckled and the black lambs. That's all you need to give me.
text: Leŋ dɩ mɩ gɔllɛ ŋ pʋsʋŋ kala tʋɔŋ jɩnɩŋ a tʋɔsɛ pie-ŋmʋŋmʋnaa, pie-bel-bisiŋ, arɩ bʋ-ŋmʋŋmʋnaa. Kɩalɩŋ kala mɩ sɩɩ chɛ nɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: Ŋ-la lɛ nɛ ŋ maachie jaŋ to jɩŋ arɩ mɩ kɛŋ wʋtɩtɩɩ, dɩ ŋ nɛ maachie na bʋʋŋ koo piesu dɩ ʋ bɩ lagɩsa koo pie-bie sɩ bɩ kʋbine ŋaa mɩ pʋsʋŋ tʋɔŋ, jɩŋ arɩ mɩ ŋaa gaarʋ nɛ.”

ref: “I agree to that,” was Laban's response.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ bʋl, “Mɩ sɛyɛ nɛ. La jaŋ ŋaa ŋɩɩ ŋ sɩ daga.”

ref: Before the end of the day, Laban had separated his spotted and speckled animals and the black lambs from the others and had put his sons in charge of them.
text: Tapʋlɩɩ-la tɩtɩa Labaŋ jʋʋ tʋɔsɛ gbuŋgbogusuŋ arɩ bʋnɩɩnɩ-la kala sɩ tagɩsɛ kʋpʋllʋŋ arɩ pie-bii-bisiŋ kala pɩ ʋ hambolibiisiŋ dɩ baa daa.

ref: Then Laban made Jacob keep the rest of the sheep and goats at a distance of three days' journey.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ leŋ ʋ hambolibiisiŋ kɛŋ pʋsʋ-la mʋ hɔŋ dɩ-bolii. Ba dɩ Jekɔb pɛgɛ magɛ tapʋlaa batori vɛnɩŋ, ka Jekɔb dɩdaa Labaŋ pʋŋ-kaanaa.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb keri tɩɩ-naakel duduobiisiŋ a lɩɩ tɩɩ-doho doho lɛ a hige ta aŋ ka ba lagɩsɛ kʋpʋllʋŋ, ʋ leŋ dɩ-pʋla-la nyɩsɛ,

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: ʋ joŋo hɛ pʋsʋ-la dɩ-nyʋalʋŋ lɛ, dɩ pʋsʋ-la nɛ kɔ dɩ ba nyʋa lɩɩŋ a jɩjɩl dɔŋɔ, a chaasɛ daasɩ-la,

ref: in front of the branches, and their young were spotted and speckled.
text: dɩ ba nɩɩ chɛ dɩ ba lʋl, ba yie lʋl biiri-la dɩ ba lagɩsa lagɩsa.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb pɔrɛ pie-bisi-la aŋ leŋ pie-kaanaa chaasɛ pʋsʋ-la sɩ bire arɩ kɩalɩŋ sɩ tagɩsa a ŋaa Labaŋ kɩaa. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ ŋaa a kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa pʋsʋŋ. Ʋ bɩ ba joŋo guoli Labaŋ pʋsʋŋ lɛ.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Dɩ pʋŋ-duoru-la jɩlɩŋ nɛ kʋ yi na-kala, Jekɔb yie joŋ daasɩ-la hɛ pʋsʋ-la dɩ-nyʋalʋŋ lɛ dɩ ba jɩla kpagɛ daasɩ-la.

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Ama dɩ pʋsʋ-la nɛ bɩ pʋŋ-duori ŋaa, ʋ bɩ daasɩ-la joŋo bil ba sɩpaaŋ. Kʋduori-la ŋaa Jekɔb kɩaa, ka pʋŋ-pʋgʋnʋ-la ŋaa Labaŋ kɩaa.

ref: Jacob soon became rich and successful. He owned many sheep, goats, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of slaves.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb kɛŋ kɩaa woruŋ. Ʋ kɛŋ nɛsɩŋ arɩ piesee, bʋntaŋŋaba arɩ kakusuŋ, tɩntɩnnaa, baalaa arɩ haalaa.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb nɩɩ Labaŋ biiriŋ ka bʋl, “Jekɔb laa la nyɩmma dʋa kala a ŋaa kɩaa tɩɩna.”

ref: Jacob also noticed that Laban was not as friendly as he had been before.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb na arɩ Labaŋ wʋŋaalaa bɩra bɩ zɔŋ ʋ teeŋ arɩ ba fa sɩ zɔmɔ ŋɩɩ.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA bʋla pɩ Jekɔb, “Mɩɩra mʋ ŋ nyɩmma arɩ ŋ naambiiriŋ tinteeŋ, mɩ jaŋ hɛ ŋ teeŋ.”

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb tɩma yɩrɛ ʋ haala Rakɛl arɩ Liya dɩ ba kɔ pɔkɔŋ nyuŋ, lee-la ʋ pʋsʋŋ sɩ hɛ.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Ʋ bʋla pɩba, “Mɩ na arɩ ma nyɩmma wʋŋaalaa bɩra bɩ faafaa-la naga mɩ teeŋ, ama mɩ nyɩmma Wɩa nɛ chɩŋ mɩ harɩŋ.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Ma kala jɩma arɩ mɩ kɛŋ mɩ doluŋ kala nɛ a tɩma pɩ ma nyɩmma.
