ref: and that he keeps cheating me by changing my wages time after time. But God has protected me.
text: Ama ma nyɩmma ha gʋrɩmɛ nɛ woruŋ a kɛŋ mɩ time bɩrɩmɛ naansɩɩŋ fii. Arɩ ŋɩɩ kala Wɩa ha bɩ leŋe ʋ ŋaa wʋbɔŋ mɩ lɛ.

ref: When your father said the speckled sheep would be my wages, all of them were speckled. And when he said the spotted ones would be mine, all of them were spotted.
text: Dɩ Labaŋ nɛ sɩ pʋsʋ-la kala sɩ ŋaa ŋmʋna-ŋmʋna jaŋ ŋaa mɩ time, pʋsʋ-la kala yie lʋl bii-ŋmʋŋmʋnaa nɛ, dɩ ʋ nɛ bɩra sɩ pʋsʋ-la sɩ lagɩsa nɛ jaŋ ŋaa mɩ time, ba kala yie lʋl biiri-la sɩ lagɩsa lagɩsa nɛ.

ref: That's how God has taken sheep and goats from your father and given them to me.
text: Wɩa nɛ laa ma nyɩmma pʋsʋŋ a pɩmɛ.

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: Ʋ kʋ yi bʋa kʋbala, pʋsʋ-la sɩɩ chɛ ba jɩl, mɩ duosi a na arɩ gbuŋgbogusu-la sɩɩ chɛ dɩ ba jɩl bʋ-haala-la kala lagɩsa, aŋ pɛ ŋaa ŋmʋna-ŋmʋna.

ref: Then God's angel called me by name. I answered,
text: Mɩ duoso-la tʋɔŋ, Wɩa tɩndaara yɩrɛmɛ, ‘Jekɔb.’ Ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ sɛɩ, ‘Namɛ nɛ ŋ-la.’

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa tɩndaarʋ-la bʋl, ‘Bee-na dɩ gbuŋgbogusu-la kala sɩɩ jɩl bʋʋna-la ŋaa ŋmʋna-ŋmʋna nɛ aŋ pɛ lagɩsa, bɛɛ wɩaa mɩ na wɩalɩŋ kala Labaŋ sɩ ŋaa ŋ lɛ nɛ.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Mɩ nɛ ŋaa Wɩa-la sɩ kɛŋ mɩ tɩtɩa kʋ dagɩŋ Bɛtɛl lɛ. Nɩmɛ nɛ ŋ joŋ tabɩɩŋ pɛ ʋ nagɛ jebiiŋ ŋ joŋ nʋʋŋ bɩsa wurisi. Nɩmɛ nɛ ŋ ta niiŋ a pɩmɛ. Lɛɛlɛ sii tinteeŋ deeŋ lɛ lɩma lɩma a mɩɩra mʋ ŋ jaŋ.’ ”

ref: He treats us like foreigners and has even cheated us out of the bride price that should have been ours.
text: Ʋ bɩ bene dɩ la ŋaa nʋhʋɔraa nɛ ʋ jonla yallɛɛ? Ʋ bɩ naga arɩ ʋ yallɛla dʋndʋŋ nɛ, ama ʋ dii kɩalɩŋ ʋ sɩ laa la bʋbʋɔŋ lɛ kala nɛ.

ref: So do whatever God tells you to do. Even the property God took from our father and gave to you really belongs to us and our children.
text: Wʋtɩtɩɩ, dʋa-la kala Wɩa sɩ laa la nyɩmma teeŋ ŋaa la dɩ la biiriŋ dʋa nɛ. Ŋɩɩ wɩaa, wɩɩ-la kala Wɩa sɩ dagɩŋ, ŋaa ŋɩɩ.”

ref: Then Jacob, his wives, and his children got on camels and left
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb joŋ ʋ biiriŋ arɩ ʋ haalaa a hɛ bʋntaŋŋaba nyuŋ

ref: northern Syria for the home of his father Isaac in Canaan. Jacob took along all his flocks, herds, and other property.
text: a kiri ʋ pʋsʋŋ kala hɛ ʋ sɩpaaŋ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala ʋ sɩ dʋa Mesopotemia tinteeŋ dɩ ʋ mʋ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ, ʋ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk leriŋ.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Labaŋ sɩ mʋa dɩ ʋ kɛ ʋ piesee pʋnaa, Rakɛl jʋʋ gaa paa ʋ nyɩmma tɔŋŋɔɔ.

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Jekɔb ma nyɩsɛ Labaŋ, Siiria tɩɩna-la aŋ bɩ bʋla pʋ arɩ ʋ fá viviiri nɛ.

ref: When Jacob crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead, he took with him everything he owned. Laban Catches Up with Jacob
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ kɛŋ ʋ dʋa kala fá a chol fuo-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Yufireti a chaasɛ Gɩlɩad tintee-la peelee sɩ yʋga a mʋ. Labaŋ kiri Jekɔb harɩŋ

ref: Three days later Laban found out that Jacob had gone.
text: Jekɔb sɩ lɩɩ fá, chɛ-tori chɛɛŋ ba bʋla pɩ Labaŋ arɩ Jekɔb fá nɛ.

ref: So he took some of his relatives along and chased after Jacob for seven days, before catching up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
text: Labaŋ kɛŋ ʋ naambiiriŋ ba kiri Jekɔb harɩŋ tapʋlaa balɩpɛ a mʋ puu Gɩlɩad tintee-la peelee sɩ yʋga leriŋ.

ref: But God appeared to Laban in a dream that night and warned, “Don't say a word to Jacob. Don't make a threat or a promise.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa kɔ Labaŋ, Siiria tɩɩna-la teeŋ tɩtaŋɩŋ duosoo tʋɔŋ a bʋla pʋ, “Pɔ dɩ ŋ sɩ́ wɩɩkala bʋl Jekɔb teeŋ, wʋzɔŋ koo wʋbɔŋ.”

ref: Jacob had set up camp in the hill country of Gilead, when Laban and his relatives came and set up camp in another part of the hill country. Laban went to Jacob
text: Jekɔb hɛ ʋ lɛbɩŋ Gɩlɩad tintee-la peelee sɩ yʋga lɛ, aŋ ka Labaŋ arɩ ʋ naambiiriŋ kʋ puuba a ma hɛ ba lɛbɩŋ lee-la tɩtɩa.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ pɩɛsɛ Jekɔb, “Bɛɛ nɛ ŋ ŋaa ŋ-la? Ŋ nyɩsɛmɛ nɛ a paa mɩ toloo a nagɛ yosuŋ ba sɩ yuo laalɩŋ kɛsɛ.

ref: Why did you sneak away without telling me? I would have given you a going-away party with singing and with music on tambourines and harps.
text: Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ lʋara fá aŋ bɩ bʋla pɩmɛ? Dɩ ŋ nɛ fa bʋla, mɩ fa jaŋ leŋ dɩ ba du chenniŋ a chɩgɩsɛ chɩnchɩgɩsɩŋ a yɩɩ yɩɩlaa a kɛnɩŋ lɩɩ hɛ wombiiŋ lɛ arɩ tʋɔtɔrʋŋ.

ref: You didn't even give me a chance to kiss my own grandchildren and daughters goodbye. That was really foolish.
text: Bɛɛ kʋŋ wʋyaayaa nɛ ŋ ŋaa ŋɩɩ ŋ sɩ bɩ leŋe mɩ guori mɩ nihee arɩ mɩ toloo a taba?

ref: I could easily hurt you, but the God your father worshiped has warned me not to make any threats or promises.
text: Mɩ kɛŋ doluŋ mɩ fa sɩ jaŋ wuo ŋaa wʋbɔŋ kala ŋ lɛ, ama dɩa tɩtaŋɩŋ Wɩa-la sɩ ŋaa ŋ nyɩmma Wɩa bʋla pɩmɛ arɩ mɩ pʋpɔ mɩ sɩ́ wɩɩkala bʋl ŋ teeŋ, wʋzɔŋ koo wʋbɔŋ.

ref: I can understand why you were eager to return to your father, but why did you have to steal my idols?
text: Lɛɛlɛ ŋ cho dɩ ŋ viiri nɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa ŋ nyɩmma dɩa hɩɛnɩŋ kɛnɩŋ nɛ. Bɛɛ nɛ saa tɩɩ ŋ pɛ gaa paa mɩ tɔŋŋɔɔ?”

ref: Jacob answered, “I left secretly because I was afraid you would take your daughters from me by force.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋla pɩ Labaŋ, “Fawʋllʋŋ nɛ fa kɛmmɛ. Mɩ bɩɩna arɩ ŋ jaŋ kɛŋ ŋ toloo laa mɩ teeŋ arɩ doluŋ.

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Dɩ ŋ nɛ na ŋ tɔmɔ-la la kʋŋkala teeŋ, sɩ́ leŋe dɩ tɩɩna kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ. La naambiiriŋ deemba sɩpaaŋ, beŋ dɩ ŋ nɛ na ŋ kʋŋkala la teeŋ, ŋ jaŋ wuo laa ŋ kʋa.” Ama Jekɔb fa bɩ jɩŋ arɩ Rakɛl nɛ gaa paa tɔmɔ-la.

ref: Laban searched the tents of Jacob, Leah, and the two servant women, but did not find the idols. Then he went to Rachel's tent.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ jʋʋ Jekɔb lɛbɩŋ, a jʋʋ Liya ma kʋa, ʋ jʋʋ ketie-la balɩamɩŋ lɛŋŋɛɛ a bɩ kʋŋkala na. Ʋ sɩ lɩɩ Liya lɛbɩŋ kʋ lɩɩ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ jʋʋ Rakɛl ma lɛbɩŋ.

ref: She had already hidden them in the cushion she used as a saddle and was sitting on it. Laban searched everywhere and did not find them.
text: Ama Rakɛl joŋ tɔmɔ-la a hɛ bʋntaŋŋa kpasa bʋbʋɔŋ a jɩla hɔŋ ba nyuŋ, ka Labaŋ beŋ lɛŋŋɛ-la tʋɔŋ a bɩ kʋŋkala na.

ref: Rachel said, “Father, please don't be angry with me for not getting up; I'm having my period.” Laban kept on searching, but still did not find the idols.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rakɛl bʋla pɩ ʋ nyɩmma, “Mɩ tɩɩna, sɩ́ leŋe dɩ ŋ baanɩŋ sii mɩ nyuŋ a tɩŋ mɩ sɩ bɩ jaŋ wuo sii chɩŋ ŋ sɩpaaŋ, mɩɩ na mɩ hakɩaa nɛ.” Labaŋ sɩ gbille, ʋ bɩ kʋŋkala na.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Jekɔb na baanɩŋ Labaŋ nyuŋ a pɩɛsʋ, “Bɛɛ nɛ mɩ ŋaa cheyiŋ? Mɩ wʋcheeliŋ nɛ bɛɛ a maga dɩ ŋ kire to mɩ harɩŋ?

ref: After searching through everything I have, did you find anything of yours? If so, put it here, where your relatives and mine can see it. Then we can decide what to do.
text: Ŋ sɩ beŋ mɩ kɩaa kala tʋɔŋ, ŋ na ŋ kʋŋ ba tʋɔŋ? Kʋ-la kala ŋ sɩ na ʋ ŋaa ŋ kʋŋ, kaa kʋ bil la kala sɩpaaŋ dɩ ba jɩma pɩla.

ref: If a wild animal killed one of your sheep or goats, I paid for it myself. In fact, you demanded the full price, whether the animal was killed during the day or at night.
text: Dɩ baga kʋŋ nɛ kɛŋ pʋsʋ-la kʋŋ, ʋ yie ŋaa mɩ wɩɩ nɛ, mɩ tɩtɩa nɛ yie tiŋ. Dɩ kʋŋ ma nɛ ba gaa wɩɩhɛyɛ koo tɩtaŋɩŋ, ŋ yie leŋ mɩ tiŋ nɛ.

ref: I sweated every day, and I couldn't sleep at night because of the cold.
text: Hɛɛ-la mɩ sɩ na nɛ ŋ-la: Wɩɩhɛyɛ, wɩa yie kpʋmɛ nɛ woruŋ, tɩtaŋɩŋ ma waarɩŋ ma yie kpʋmɛ nɛ woruŋ, mɩ bɩ doŋ ka pɩŋ.

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Dɩ mɩ nyɩmma Abɩraham Wɩa arɩ Aɩzɩk sɩɩ fá Wɩa-la fa bɩ mɩ harɩŋ chɩna, ŋ fa jaŋ lemmi ta arɩ na-hɩllɩŋ. Ama Wɩa na hɛɛ-la mɩ sɩ na nɛ ŋ nɩsɩŋ lɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ Wɩa laa pɛ mɩ lɛ a dɩa bʋl wɩaa pɩŋ tɩtaŋɩŋ ŋɩɩ.”

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ bʋl, “Haalaa deemba ŋaa mɩ toloo nɛ, biiri-la ŋaa mɩ biiriŋ, pʋsʋ-la ma ŋaa mɩ pʋsʋŋ nɛ, kɩaa deemba kala ŋ sɩ na ŋaa mɩ kɩaa nɛ. Ama wʋbɛɛ nɛ mɩ jaŋ ŋaa pɛ mɩ toloo deemba arɩ ba biiriŋ deemba ba sɩ lʋla lɛ jɩnɩŋ?

ref: So I am ready to make an agreement with you, and we will pile up some large rocks here to remind us of the agreement.”
text: Lɛɛlɛ, leŋ dɩ la bil niiŋ a pɩ dɔŋɔ dɩ ʋ ŋaa wɩɩ la pɛgɛ.”

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb joŋ tabɩɩŋ a pɛ ʋ nagɛ jebiiŋ.

ref: he told his men to get some more rocks and pile them up next to it. Then Jacob and Laban ate a meal together beside the rocks.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋla pɩ ʋ dɩa nɩaa, “Má paa tabɩaa hɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba paa a hɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ ba chʋɔrɛ, ba hɔŋ nɩmɛ a dii kʋdiilee.

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ hɛ lee-la yɩrɩŋ Jega-Sahaduta, Jekɔb ma kɛŋ lee-la aa yɩrɛ Galeed.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “This pile of rocks will remind us of our agreement.” That's why the place was named Galeed.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Labaŋ bʋl, “Tabɩa-la sɩ chʋɔrɛ leriŋ deeŋ ma bɩra jaŋ ŋaa la dɩŋ pɛgɛ magɩl. Ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ mɩ kɛŋ lee-la yɩrɛ Galeed.”

ref: Laban also said, “This pile of rocks means that the Lord will watch us both while we are apart from each other.” So the place was also named Mizpah.
text: Ʋ bɩra kɛŋ lee-la yɩrɛ Mɩzɩpa, aŋ bʋl, “La TƖƖNA jaŋ benla dɩ la nɛ pɔrɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ ma.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Dɩ ŋ nɛ dɔgɩsɛ mɩ toloo koo dɩ ŋ nɛ bɩra jaa haalaa dɔnsʋŋ a pɛ ba lɛ, nʋʋkala tuo daha a na. Ama jɩŋ arɩ Wɩa-naa beŋ la kala nɛ.”

ref: Both this pile of rocks and this large rock have been set up between us as a reminder. I must never go past them to attack you, and you must never come past them to attack me.
text: Tabɩaa deemba sɩ chʋɔra arɩ tabɩɩ-la sɩ pɛ nɛ ŋaa la magɩlɩŋ arɩ mɩ bɩ jaŋ kieli tabɩaa deemba arɩ jebiiŋ deeŋ a kʋ ŋaaŋ wʋbɔŋ, ŋ ma bɩ jaŋ kieli jebii-la a kɔ mɩ teeŋ a ŋaamɛ wʋbɔŋ.

ref: My father Nahor, your grandfather Abraham, and their ancestors all worshiped the same God, and he will make sure that we each keep the agreement. Then Jacob made a promise in the name of the fearsome God his father Isaac had worshiped.
text: Abɩraham Wɩa, Nahɔ Wɩa, arɩ ba nyɩmma Wɩa nɛ jaŋ jɩŋ la wɩɩŋ deeŋ pɩla.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb ŋmɩɛsɛ Wɩa-la ʋ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk sɩɩ fá.

ref: Jacob killed an animal and offered it as a sacrifice there on the mountain, and he invited his men to eat with him. After the meal they spent the night on the mountain.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb kpʋ pʋna a sʋl Wɩa peele-la tʋɔŋ a ŋaa kʋdiilee a yɩrɛ ʋ dɩa nɩaa ba kʋ dii. Ba sɩ dii kʋdiile-la dɛrɛ, ba chʋa peele-la tʋɔŋ.

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: Chʋchʋɔ-fɩɛla Labaŋ sii guori ʋ nihee arɩ ʋ toloo a sʋl Wɩa pɩba, aŋ lemba aŋ mʋ dɩa.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekɔb sɩ sii mʋmʋ, Wɩa tɩndaaraa chemu wombiiŋ lɛ.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb sɩ naba, ʋ bʋl, “Daha nɛ Wɩa hɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ lee-la yɩrɩŋ Mahanem.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb tɩŋ tɩndaaraa ba laa sɩpaaŋ mʋ bʋla pɩ ʋ naambie Ɛsɔ sɩ hɛ Sɛɛ lɛ, Ɛdɔm tinteeŋ.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Jekɔb bʋl, “Dɩ ma nɛ mʋa, má bʋl ŋ-la a pɩ mɩ malɩma Ɛsɔ: ‘Ŋ ŋaana Jekɔb nɛ tɩa tɩndaarɩŋ deeŋ. Mɩ hɔŋ Labaŋ teeŋ a dieni nɛ a kʋʋ yi bʋa deeŋ lɛ.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Mɩ kɛŋ nɛsɩŋ, kakusuŋ, piesee arɩ bʋʋnaa, tɩntɩnnaa, baalaa arɩ haalaa, mɩɩ pɩŋ tɩndaarɩŋ deeŋ dɩ ŋ tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ nɛ mɩ nyuŋ.’ ”

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Tɩndaara-la sɩ mɩɩra kɔ Jekɔb teeŋ, ba bʋla pʋ, “La mʋ ŋ naambie Ɛsɔ teeŋ nɛ. Ʋʋ kɔ dɩ ʋ chemiŋ nɛ arɩ nɩaa zɔɔ-banɛsɛ.”

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ fawʋllʋŋ faasa kɛŋ Jekɔb ʋ tʋɔŋ chei. Ʋ kɛŋ nɩalɩŋ sɩ hɛ ʋ teeŋ kaa kpaa leree balɩa, a kɛŋ ʋ pʋsʋ-la arɩ bʋntaŋŋaba kala ma kpaa ŋɩɩ tɩtɩa.

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: Ʋ bɩɩnɛ ʋ tʋɔŋ lɛ, “Dɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ kɔ a yuo leriŋ dɔŋɔ, dɔŋɔ jaŋ fá.”

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb sʋl Wɩa a bʋl, “Mɩ nyɩmma Abɩraham Wɩa, mɩ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk Wɩa, ŋ nɛ bʋla pɩmɛ, dɩ mɩ mɩɩra mʋ mɩ tinteeŋ mɩ nɩaa teeŋ, dɩ ŋ jaŋ pɛ mɩ lɛ dɩ mɩ nɩsɩŋ jʋʋ woruŋ.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ dɩ ŋ laamɛ ta mɩ naambie Ɛsɔ nɩsɩŋ lɛ. Mɩɩ fáʋ nɛ arɩ ʋ jaŋ kʋ yuo mɩ dɩ mɩ haalaa arɩ mɩ biiriŋ.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Ama ŋ nɛ bʋla arɩ ŋ jaŋ pɛ mɩ lɛ a leŋ dɩ mɩ doho nɩaa pʋŋ a nagɛ hagɩlɩ-bumbugulo sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ niiŋ nʋʋ sɩ bɩ jaŋ wuo dɩɩsɛ.”

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Jekɔb chʋa nɩmɛ tɩtaŋ-la. Kɩalɩŋ ʋ sɩ kɛnɛ ʋ tʋɔsɛ dɔnsʋŋ dɩ ʋ pɩ ʋ naambie Ɛsɔ:

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Má bʋla pʋ arɩ ʋ tɩntɩnna Jekɔb-na ha hɛ harɩŋ nɛ.” Ʋ sɩ bʋl ŋɩɩ, ʋ bɩɩna ʋ tʋɔŋ lɛ, “Mɩ kɛŋ kɩaa deemba kala laa sɩpaaŋ kaa mʋ dɩ mɩ sʋlʋ nɛ, ka dɩ ʋ laamɛ.”

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb kɛŋ lee-la yɩrɛ Pɛnɩɛl, aŋ bʋl “Mɩ na Wɩa nɛ arɩ mɩ sɩɩ-biiŋ aŋ bɩ sʋba.”

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: Ʋ joŋ kɩaa deemba a hɛ ʋ tɩntɩnnaa nɩsɩŋ lɛ dɩ ba kire vɛŋ dabaŋ dabaŋ, aŋ bʋla pɩba, “Má laa sɩpaaŋ a kiri kɩalɩŋ vɩvɛŋ, aŋ ka wuoluŋ hɛ ba pɛgɛ.”

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Ʋ bʋla pɩ tɩntɩnnʋ-la sɩ laa sɩpaaŋ a bʋl, “Dɩ mɩ naambie Ɛsɔ nɛ chemiŋ a pɩɛsɩŋ a bʋl, ‘Ma nɛ kʋbɛɛba, nɩɩ nɛ maa mʋ? Kʋbɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ pʋsʋŋ deemba ma sɩ kire vɩvɛŋ?’

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Má bʋl, ‘Ŋ tɩntɩnna Jekɔb nɛ tɩɩba. Ʋ sɩ dɩ la kaa mʋ dii zile pɩ ʋ nɩhɩaŋ Ɛsɔ nɛ. Ʋ nɛ hɛ la harɩŋ kʋkɔ.’ ”

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Ŋɩɩ tɩtɩa nɛ ʋ bʋla pɩ nɩlɩamɩŋ, nɩ-torimuŋ arɩ nɩkaanaa kala sɩɩ to pʋsʋ-la harɩŋ a bʋl, “Ŋ-la nɛ ma kala maga dɩ ma bʋla pɩ Ɛsɔ dɩ ma nɛ chemu.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba kɛŋ Jekɔb chɔgɩta-la ʋ sɩ kaa mʋ dɩ ʋ pɩ Ɛsɔ laa sɩpaaŋ, ka ʋ chʋa lee-la. Jekɔb arɩ Wɩa lobo Pɛnɩɛl lɛ

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ aŋ ka ʋ-na Jekɔb dʋndʋŋa fuo-la cholo. Baal kʋbala kʋ kɛnʋ ba loo ŋɩɩ halɩɩ taŋ pʋl.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Baal-la sɩ na dɩ ʋ bɩ Jekɔb wuo, ʋ yi ʋ kɛrɛ lɛ, kɛrɩ-sɩɩŋ lɩɩ.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la bʋl, “Jommi ta mɩ viiri, bɛɛ wɩaa taŋ pʋla nɛ.” Ama Jekɔb bʋl, “Mɩ bɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ mʋ, see dɩ ŋ sʋl Wɩa pɩmɛ.”

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la pɩɛsɛ, “Ŋ yɩrɩŋ nɛ bɛɛ?” Jekɔb bʋl, “Mɩ yɩrɩŋ nɛ Jekɔb.”

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la bʋl, “Ŋ yɩrɩŋ bɩra bɩ jaŋ ŋaa Jekɔb. Ŋ yɩrɩŋ nɛ Iziral, bɛɛ wɩaa ŋ kɛŋ Wɩa arɩ nuhuobiinee loo nɛ a lobo dii.”

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋl “Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, dagɛ ŋ yɩrɩŋ pɩmɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la bʋl, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ chɩchɛ dɩ ŋ jɩŋ mɩ yɩrɩŋ?” Ʋ bʋl ŋɩɩ aŋ sʋl Wɩa pʋ nɩmɛ.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Wɩa pɔsɔ jɩl nyuŋ dɩ Jekɔb lɩɩ Pɛnɩɛl lɛ a jujusi a tɩŋ ʋ kɛrɛ sɩ lɩa wɩaa.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Ŋ-la wɩaa nɛ tɩɩ Iziral tɩŋŋaa bɩ nammɩa-la sɩ tɔ kʋŋkala kɛrɩ-sɩɩŋ ka chaŋ arɩ jɩnɩŋ kala, bɛɛ wɩaa Jekɔb lee-la nɛ baal-la yi lɩɩsɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekɔb sɩ sii mʋmʋ, Wɩa tɩndaaraa chemu wombiiŋ lɛ.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb sɩ naba, ʋ bʋl, “Daha nɛ Wɩa hɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ lee-la yɩrɩŋ Mahanem.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb tɩŋ tɩndaaraa ba laa sɩpaaŋ mʋ bʋla pɩ ʋ naambie Ɛsɔ sɩ hɛ Sɛɛ lɛ, Ɛdɔm tinteeŋ.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Jekɔb bʋl, “Dɩ ma nɛ mʋa, má bʋl ŋ-la a pɩ mɩ malɩma Ɛsɔ: ‘Ŋ ŋaana Jekɔb nɛ tɩa tɩndaarɩŋ deeŋ. Mɩ hɔŋ Labaŋ teeŋ a dieni nɛ a kʋʋ yi bʋa deeŋ lɛ.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Mɩ kɛŋ nɛsɩŋ, kakusuŋ, piesee arɩ bʋʋnaa, tɩntɩnnaa, baalaa arɩ haalaa, mɩɩ pɩŋ tɩndaarɩŋ deeŋ dɩ ŋ tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ nɛ mɩ nyuŋ.’ ”

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Tɩndaara-la sɩ mɩɩra kɔ Jekɔb teeŋ, ba bʋla pʋ, “La mʋ ŋ naambie Ɛsɔ teeŋ nɛ. Ʋʋ kɔ dɩ ʋ chemiŋ nɛ arɩ nɩaa zɔɔ-banɛsɛ.”

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ fawʋllʋŋ faasa kɛŋ Jekɔb ʋ tʋɔŋ chei. Ʋ kɛŋ nɩalɩŋ sɩ hɛ ʋ teeŋ kaa kpaa leree balɩa, a kɛŋ ʋ pʋsʋ-la arɩ bʋntaŋŋaba kala ma kpaa ŋɩɩ tɩtɩa.

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: Ʋ bɩɩnɛ ʋ tʋɔŋ lɛ, “Dɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ kɔ a yuo leriŋ dɔŋɔ, dɔŋɔ jaŋ fá.”

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb sʋl Wɩa a bʋl, “Mɩ nyɩmma Abɩraham Wɩa, mɩ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk Wɩa, ŋ nɛ bʋla pɩmɛ, dɩ mɩ mɩɩra mʋ mɩ tinteeŋ mɩ nɩaa teeŋ, dɩ ŋ jaŋ pɛ mɩ lɛ dɩ mɩ nɩsɩŋ jʋʋ woruŋ.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ dɩ ŋ laamɛ ta mɩ naambie Ɛsɔ nɩsɩŋ lɛ. Mɩɩ fáʋ nɛ arɩ ʋ jaŋ kʋ yuo mɩ dɩ mɩ haalaa arɩ mɩ biiriŋ.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Ama ŋ nɛ bʋla arɩ ŋ jaŋ pɛ mɩ lɛ a leŋ dɩ mɩ doho nɩaa pʋŋ a nagɛ hagɩlɩ-bumbugulo sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ niiŋ nʋʋ sɩ bɩ jaŋ wuo dɩɩsɛ.”

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Jekɔb chʋa nɩmɛ tɩtaŋ-la. Kɩalɩŋ ʋ sɩ kɛnɛ ʋ tʋɔsɛ dɔnsʋŋ dɩ ʋ pɩ ʋ naambie Ɛsɔ:

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Má bʋla pʋ arɩ ʋ tɩntɩnna Jekɔb-na ha hɛ harɩŋ nɛ.” Ʋ sɩ bʋl ŋɩɩ, ʋ bɩɩna ʋ tʋɔŋ lɛ, “Mɩ kɛŋ kɩaa deemba kala laa sɩpaaŋ kaa mʋ dɩ mɩ sʋlʋ nɛ, ka dɩ ʋ laamɛ.”

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb kɛŋ lee-la yɩrɛ Pɛnɩɛl, aŋ bʋl “Mɩ na Wɩa nɛ arɩ mɩ sɩɩ-biiŋ aŋ bɩ sʋba.”

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”

text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba kɛŋ Jekɔb chɔgɩta-la ʋ sɩ kaa mʋ dɩ ʋ pɩ Ɛsɔ laa sɩpaaŋ, ka ʋ chʋa lee-la. Jekɔb arɩ Wɩa lobo Pɛnɩɛl lɛ

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ aŋ ka ʋ-na Jekɔb dʋndʋŋa fuo-la cholo. Baal kʋbala kʋ kɛnʋ ba loo ŋɩɩ halɩɩ taŋ pʋl.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Baal-la sɩ na dɩ ʋ bɩ Jekɔb wuo, ʋ yi ʋ kɛrɛ lɛ, kɛrɩ-sɩɩŋ lɩɩ.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la bʋl, “Jommi ta mɩ viiri, bɛɛ wɩaa taŋ pʋla nɛ.” Ama Jekɔb bʋl, “Mɩ bɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ mʋ, see dɩ ŋ sʋl Wɩa pɩmɛ.”

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la pɩɛsɛ, “Ŋ yɩrɩŋ nɛ bɛɛ?” Jekɔb bʋl, “Mɩ yɩrɩŋ nɛ Jekɔb.”

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la bʋl, “Ŋ yɩrɩŋ bɩra bɩ jaŋ ŋaa Jekɔb. Ŋ yɩrɩŋ nɛ Iziral, bɛɛ wɩaa ŋ kɛŋ Wɩa arɩ nuhuobiinee loo nɛ a lobo dii.”

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋl “Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, dagɛ ŋ yɩrɩŋ pɩmɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la bʋl, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ chɩchɛ dɩ ŋ jɩŋ mɩ yɩrɩŋ?” Ʋ bʋl ŋɩɩ aŋ sʋl Wɩa pʋ nɩmɛ.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Wɩa pɔsɔ jɩl nyuŋ dɩ Jekɔb lɩɩ Pɛnɩɛl lɛ a jujusi a tɩŋ ʋ kɛrɛ sɩ lɩa wɩaa.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Ŋ-la wɩaa nɛ tɩɩ Iziral tɩŋŋaa bɩ nammɩa-la sɩ tɔ kʋŋkala kɛrɩ-sɩɩŋ ka chaŋ arɩ jɩnɩŋ kala, bɛɛ wɩaa Jekɔb lee-la nɛ baal-la yi lɩɩsɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: The two servant women, Zilpah and Bilhah, together with their children went first, followed by Leah and her children, then by Rachel and Joseph.
text: Ʋ leŋ ketie-la arɩ ba biiriŋ laa sɩpaaŋ, Liya arɩ ʋ biiriŋ tɩtɩgɛ ba lɛ, ka Rakɛl arɩ Josɛf tuto harɩŋ.

ref: Jacob himself walked in front of them all, bowing to the ground seven times as he came near his brother.
text: Jekɔb tɩtɩa nɛ laa sɩpaaŋ mʋ. Ʋ sɩɩ mʋ, ʋ kpirimi naansɩɩŋ bʋlɩpɛ aŋ-na yi ʋ naambie.

ref: But Esau ran toward Jacob and hugged and kissed him. Then the two brothers started crying.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ fá a mʋ chemu a guoru. Ba guori dɔŋɔ a lɩlaarɛ dɔŋɔ aŋ pɛ yiyel.

ref: When Esau noticed the women and children he asked, “Whose children are these?” Jacob answered, “These are the children the Lord has been kind enough to give to me, your servant.”
text: Ɛsɔ beŋ sɩpaaŋ a na haalaa arɩ ba biiriŋ a pɩɛsɛ Jekɔb, “Kʋbɛɛba ma nɩɩ to ŋ harɩŋ ŋɩɩ?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋl, “Biiri-la Wɩa sɩ joŋo dii zile mɩ-na ŋ tɩntɩnna lɛ nɛ.”

ref: Then the two servant women and their children came and bowed down to Esau.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ketie-la arɩ ba biiriŋ ma kʋ yi a tuu kpirimi.

ref: Next, Leah and her children came and bowed down; finally, Joseph and Rachel also came and bowed down.
text: Liya arɩ ʋ biiriŋ ma kʋ yi a tuu kpirimi. Ʋ harɩŋ Josɛf arɩ Rakɛl ma kɔ a ma tuu kpirimi.

ref: Esau asked Jacob, “Why did you send those herds I met along the road?” “Master,” Jacob answered, “I sent them so you would be friendly to me.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ pɩɛsɛ, “Ka nɩalɩŋ arɩ kʋdaŋ-la mɩ sɩ laa sɩpaaŋ cheŋ, ʋ bʋbʋɔŋ nɛ bɛɛ?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋl, “Mɩ nɩhɩaŋ, mɩɩ chɛ dɩ ŋ tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ mɩ nyuŋ nɛ.”

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ bʋl, “Mɩ sɩ kɛŋ kɩalɩŋ, ba maga nɛ mɩ naambii, ŋ ma kɛŋ ŋ kɩaa.”

ref: “No!” Jacob said. “Please accept them as a sign of your friendship for me. When you welcomed me and I saw your face, it was like seeing the face of God.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋl, “Aɩ, mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ dɩ ŋ tʋɔŋ nɛ tɔrɛ mɩ nyuŋ, laa mɩ chɔgɩta deeŋ, bɛɛ wɩaa mɩ sɩ naŋ, ʋ naga Wɩa nɛ mɩ na, a tɩŋ ŋ sɩ laamɛ arɩ tʋɔtɔrʋŋ.

ref: Please accept these as gifts I brought to you. God has been good to me, and I have everything I need.” Jacob kept insisting until Esau agreed.
text: Mɩɩ sʋlʋŋ, laa chɔgɩta deeŋ mɩ sɩ kaa kɔ dɩ mɩ pɩŋ, bɛɛ wɩaa Wɩa dii zile nɛ woruŋ mɩ lɛ a pɩmɛ mɩ kʋchɛɛlɛɛ kala.” A tɩŋ Jekɔb sɩ pɛsɛ ŋɩɩ, ʋ laa kɩalɩŋ.

ref: “Let's get ready to travel,” Esau said. “I'll go along with you.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ bʋl, “Má sii dɩ la mʋ, mɩ jaŋ to ma harɩŋ mʋ.”

ref: But Jacob answered, “Master, you know traveling is hard on children, and I have to look after the sheep and goats that are nursing their young. If my animals travel too much in one day, they will all die.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋl, “Mɩ nɩhɩaŋ, ŋ jɩma hambiisi-la muuro, piese-la arɩ nɛsɩ-la ma kɛŋ bii-mulluŋ a magɛ dɩ mɩ beŋ ba nyuŋ woruŋ. Dɩ la nɛ sɩ dɩ la faasa kiriba vɛŋ chɛ-bala, ba kala jaŋ sʋʋ.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: Mɩ nɩhɩaŋ, laa mɩ-na ŋ tɩntɩnna sɩpaaŋ, ka dɩ mɩ kɛŋ pʋsʋ-la arɩ hambiisi-la fɩɛla vɛŋ mʋa mʋa kʋkɔ ŋ teeŋ Sɛɛ lɛ, Ɛdɔm tinteeŋ.”

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ bʋl, “Mɩ jaŋ leŋ mɩ nɩalɩŋ dɔnsʋŋ ŋ teeŋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb pɩɛsɛ, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ chɛ ŋ ŋaa ŋɩɩ? Mɩ nɩhɩaŋ, nɩalɩŋ-na bɩ wɩɩ tɔ, ŋ-na leŋ dɩ ŋ tʋɔŋ tɔrɛ mɩ nyuŋ.”

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Ka Jekɔb mʋ Sʋkɔf a saa dɩa nɩmɛ aŋ hɛ nɛ-dɩɩsɩŋ ma a hɛ ʋ pʋsʋŋ, ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ ba yɩyɩrɛ lee-la Sʋkɔf ŋɩɩ.

ref: After leaving northern Syria, Jacob arrived safely at Shechem in Canaan and set up camp outside the city.
text: Ŋɩɩ harɩŋ lɛ Jekɔb sii lɩɩ Mesopotemia tinteeŋ a kɔ tabal-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Sɛkɛm a hɛ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ lɛ a hɔŋ lee-la a kpagɛ taŋ-la.

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ yɔɔ tinteeŋ Hamɔ biiriŋ teeŋ warɩpʋla zɔlɔ a hɛ ʋ lɛbɩŋ nɩmɛ. Hamɔ ŋaa Sɛkɛm nyɩmma nɛ.

ref: then he set up his tents and built an altar there to honor the God of Israel.
text: Ʋ saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ nɩmɛ a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Ɛl-Ɛlɔhɩ Iziral.

ref: Dinah, the daughter of Jacob and Leah, went to visit some of the women who lived nearby.
text: Tapʋlɩɩ kʋbala Daana sɩ ŋaa Jekɔb arɩ Liya tolo lɩɩ dɩ ʋ gɔllɔ chʋɔlɛ Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa haalaa.

ref: She was seen by Hamor's son Shechem, the leader of the Hivites, and he grabbed her and raped her.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Hamɔ nanʋala Sɛkɛm naʋ a gurigu. Ʋ fa ŋaa Hivi tɩɩna nɛ a ŋaa taŋ-la kuoro.

ref: But Shechem was attracted to Dinah, so he told her how much he loved her.
text: Sɛkɛm joŋ ʋ dɩma kala daŋ ʋ lɛ a bʋl wʋsɩŋŋaa tolo-la teeŋ.

ref: Shechem even asked his father to arrange for him to marry her.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Sɛkɛm bʋla pɩ ʋ nyɩmma Hamɔ, “Jaa tolo deeŋ pɩmɛ.”

ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Jekɔb sɩ nɩa arɩ ba gurigi ʋ tolo Daana, bʋa-la lɛ dɩ ʋ hambolibiisiŋ hɛ kʋdaalɩŋ lɛ, ʋ tɔrɛ ʋ niiŋ, ba lɩɩ kʋdaalɩŋ kɔ.

ref: just as Jacob's sons were coming in from work. When they learned that their sister had been raped, they became furiously angry, because nothing is more disgraceful than rape, and it must not be tolerated.
text: Bʋa deeŋ lɛ dɩ Jekɔb hambolibiisiŋ lɩɩ baga kʋkɔ a nɩɩ wɩɩ-la. Ba tʋɔŋ chei, ba na baanɩŋ woruŋ Sɛkɛm sɩ ŋaa wɩɩ-la. Ʋ hɛ Iziral tinteeŋ kala hɩɩsɩŋ nɛ a tɩŋ ʋ sɩ kɛŋ tolo-la pɩŋ. Ʋ bɩ maga dɩ ʋ ŋaa ŋɩɩ.

ref: Hamor said to Jacob and his sons: My son Shechem really loves Dinah. Please let him marry her.
text: Ama Hamɔ bʋla pɩba, “Mɩ nanʋala Sɛkɛm dɩma daŋ ma tolo nyuŋ nɛ. Mɩɩ sʋlma, má joŋo pʋ dɩ ʋ jaa.

ref: Why don't you start letting your families marry into our families and ours marry into yours?
text: Má leŋ dɩ la jɩjaa dɔŋɔba toloo, dɩ maa jaa la toloo, dɩ la ma jɩjaa ma toloo.

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.
text: Ma jaŋ wuo hɔŋ la tinteeŋ lɛ lee na-kala ma sɩɩ chɛ. Wombiiŋ hɛ nɩmɛ. Ma jaŋ wuo yɩyallɛ kɩaa a kɛŋ dʋa.”

ref: Ask anything, no matter how expensive. I'll do anything, just let me marry Dinah.”
text: Ma-na joŋ tolo-la pɩmɛ, a dagɛ jaarɩŋ sɩ magɛ ŋɩɩ arɩ kʋŋkala ma sɩɩ chɛ, mɩ jaŋ pɩma.”

ref: Jacob's sons wanted to get even with Shechem and his father because of what had happened to their sister.
text: A tɩŋ Sɛkɛm sɩ gurigi ba dɩhaala Daana wɩaa, Jekɔb hambolibiisiŋ nyɩsɛ Sɛkɛm arɩ ʋ nyɩmma Hamɔ.

ref: So they tricked them by saying: You're not circumcised! It would be a disgrace for us to let you marry Dinah now.
text: Ba bʋla pɩba, “Ʋ ŋaa la kɩsɩŋ nɛ, la sɩ jaŋ joŋ la dɩhaala pɩ nɩɩ-la sɩ bɩ ʋ peniŋ kere. Ʋ jaŋ ŋaa hɩɩsɛ a pɩla.

ref: But we will let you marry her, if you and the other men in your tribe agree to be circumcised.
text: Wʋbala dʋndʋŋ nɛ la jaŋ cho dɩ ma ŋaa ka dɩ la-na joŋ la dɩhaala a pɩma, dɩ ma nɛ jaŋ keri ma baalaa kala penee a nagɛla.

ref: Then your families can marry into ours, and ours can marry into yours, and we can live together like one nation.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ma jaŋ wuo jɩjaa la toloo, dɩ la ma jɩjaa ma toloo. La dɩ ma jaŋ hɔŋ a ŋaa kʋbala.

ref: But if you don't agree to be circumcised, we'll take Dinah and leave this place.
text: Ama dɩ ma nɛ bɩ jaŋ sɛɩ a keri ma penee, la jaŋ laa la tolo a mʋ.”

ref: Hamor and Shechem liked what was said.
text: Ba wʋbʋlɩɩ-la sɩma pɩ Hamɔ arɩ ʋ nanʋala Sɛkɛm.

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.
text: Bapʋɔsɩ-bie deeŋ nɛ ba fa zizil woruŋ a kɩɩ nʋʋkala ʋ nyɩmma dɩa nɩaa tʋɔŋ. Ʋ-na bɩ diene aŋ ŋaa wɩalɩŋ kala ba sɩ bʋla dɩ ba ŋaa a tɩŋ ʋ sɩ cho Jekɔb tolo wɩaa.

ref: The two men met with the other leaders of their city and told them:
text: Hamɔ arɩ ʋ nanʋala Sɛkɛm mʋ ba tabal-la nɩaa dɩ-hilimiŋ a bʋla pɩ ba taŋ-la nɩaa,

ref: These people really are friendly. Why not let them move freely about until they find the property they want? There's enough land here for them and for us. Then our families can marry into theirs, and theirs can marry into ours.
text: “Nɩaa deemba ŋaa nɩzɔmɔ nɛ la tʋɔŋ. Má leŋ dɩ ba hɔŋ la tinteeŋ lɛ aa yallɛ kɩaa, tintee-la jalɩa nɛ. La jaŋ wuo jaa ba toloo dɩ ba ma jɩjaa la toloo.

ref: We have to do only one thing before they will agree to stay here and become one nation with us. Our men will have to be circumcised just like theirs.
text: Ama wʋbala dʋndʋŋ nɩalɩŋ sɩɩ chɛ dɩ la ŋaa a ŋaa kʋbala nɛ, dɩ la baal na-kala sɛɩ a keri ʋ peniŋ arɩ ba sɩ keri ba penee ŋɩɩ.

ref: Just think! We'll get their property, as well as their flocks and herds. All we have to do is to agree, and they will live here with us.
text: Dɩ la nɛ sɛyɛ dɩ la ŋaa kʋbala, ba pʋsʋŋ arɩ ba dʋa kala bɩ jaŋ ŋaa la kɩaa? Má leŋ dɩ la sɛɩ ba kʋa, dɩ ba hɔŋ la teeŋ.”

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,
text: Ba sɩ keri ba penee tapʋlaa batori chɛɛŋ, dɩ ba penee ha bɩ fɩɛla. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb hambolibiisiŋ balɩa, Simioŋ arɩ Levi, Daana dɩbaalaa, ba sii paa tokobiee a jʋʋ taŋ-la tʋɔŋ nʋʋkala sɩ bɩ yɩɛla a kpʋ baal na-kala sɩ hɛ nɩmɛ.

ref: including Hamor and Shechem. Then they took Dinah and left.
text: Ba kpʋ Hamɔ arɩ ʋ bie Sɛkɛm aŋ kɛŋ Daana lɩɩ Sɛkɛm dɩa lɛ a kaa mʋ ba dɩa.

ref: Jacob's other sons came and took everything they wanted. All this was done because of the horrible thing that had happened to their sister.
text: Jekɔb biiriŋ to sʋʋnʋ-la tʋɔŋ a paa tabal-la kɩaa kala a tɩŋ ba sɩ gurigi ba dɩhaala wɩaa.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Ba kɛsɛ ba piesee, nɛsɩŋ arɩ kakusuŋ arɩ kʋŋkala ba sɩ kɛnɛ ba dɩɩsɩŋ arɩ ba bagɩsɩŋ lɛ.

ref: After taking everything of value from the houses, they dragged away the wives and children of their victims.
text: Ba paa ba dʋa kala, a kɛsɛ ba haalaa arɩ ba biiriŋ a paa ba kʋŋkala sɩ hɛ ba dɩɩsɩŋ lɛ a kaa viiri.

ref: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “Look what you've done! Now I'm in real trouble with the Canaanites and Perizzites who live around here. There aren't many of us, and if they attack, they'll kill everyone in my household.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋla pɩ Simioŋ arɩ Levi, “Má lɔgɛ wɩaa nɛ a hɛ mɩ lɛ ŋɩɩ. Ma joŋ dʋndɔnnʋ hɛ la dɩ Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa arɩ Pɛrɛs tɩŋŋaa pɛgɛ nɛ. La bɩ yʋgɛ, dɩ nɩaa deemba nɛ pɛ dɔŋɔ lɛ dɩ ba yuomi, ba jaŋ kpʋ mɩ dɩ mɩ dɩa nɩaa kala.”

ref: They answered, “Was it right to let our own sister be treated that way?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba pɩɛsɛ, “Ʋ maga dɩ ʋ joŋ la dɩhaala arɩ ʋ ŋaa hachɔrʋŋ nɛɛ?”

ref: God told Jacob, “Return to Bethel, where I appeared to you when you were running from your brother Esau. Make your home there and build an altar for me.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋla pɩ Jekɔb, “Sii mʋ Bɛtɛl a hɔŋ nɩmɛ a saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a pɩ Wɩa-la sɩ kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa kʋ dagɩŋ bʋa-la lɛ ŋ sɩ fáa lɛl ŋ naambie Ɛsɔ nɩsɩŋ lɛ.”

ref: Jacob said to his family and to everyone else who was traveling with him: Get rid of your foreign gods! Then make yourselves acceptable to worship God and put on clean clothes.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb bʋla pɩ ʋ dɩa nɩaa arɩ nɩalɩŋ kala sɩ hɛ ʋ teeŋ, “Má paa ma tɔŋŋɔɔ kala ta aŋ pɩɩsɛ ma disiniŋ ta aŋ wuri ma gɛnnɩŋ aŋ laalɛ gɛrɩ-falaa

ref: Afterwards, we'll go to Bethel. I will build an altar there for God, who answered my prayers when I was in trouble and who has always been at my side.
text: aŋ ka dɩ la mʋ Bɛtɛl. Nɩmɛ nɛ mɩ jaŋ saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a pɩ Wɩa, Wɩa-la sɩ laa mɩ sʋla bʋa-la lɛ wɩaa sɩ jʋʋmɛ. Ʋ chɩŋ mɩ harɩŋ nɛ lee na-kala mɩ sɩ mʋa.”

ref: So everyone gave Jacob their idols and their earrings, and he buried them under the oak tree near Shechem.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba paa ba tɔmɔ-la arɩ ba dɩgɩlɩŋ-kpɛnsɩŋ kala a pɩ Jekɔb, ʋ joŋo hugi piine bʋbʋɔŋ a kpagɛ Sɛkɛm.

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Ba sɩ sie a mʋmʋ, Wɩa leŋ fawʋllʋŋ jʋʋ tasɩ-la kala fa sɩ golli lee-la chu nɩaa, ba kʋŋkala bɩ ba harɩŋ kire.

ref: Finally, they reached Bethel, also known as Luz.
text: Jekɔb arɩ ʋ nɩaa kala kɔ Lus a hɛ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ.

ref: Jacob built an altar there and called it “God of Bethel,” because that was the place where God had appeared to him when he was running from Esau.
text: Nɩmɛ nɛ ʋ saa Wɩɩsʋllɩ koruŋ a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Ɛl Bɛtɛl, bɛɛ wɩaa lee-la nɛ Wɩa kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa dagʋ bʋa-la lɛ ʋ fa sɩ fáa lɛl ʋ naambie nɩsɩŋ lɛ.

ref: While they were there, Rebekah's personal servant Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree and called it “Weeping Oak.” God Blesses Jacob at Bethel
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Debora, nɩɩ-la sɩɩ beŋ Rɩbɛka, sʋʋ. Ba joŋu hugi piine-la sɩ hɛ Bɛtɛl lɛ bʋbʋɔŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba hɛ lee-la yɩrɩŋ Alɔŋ Bakuf.

ref: After Jacob came back to the land of Canaan, God appeared to him again. This time he gave Jacob a new name and blessed him by saying: I am God All-Powerful, and from now on your name will be Israel instead of Jacob. You will have many children. Your descendants will become nations, and some of the men in your family will even be kings.
text: Wɩa bɩra bʋla pʋ, “Mɩ nɛ ŋaa Wɩa doluŋ kala Tɩɩna, kɛŋ doho a pʋŋa yʋgɛ, ŋ jaŋ ŋaa tintee, dɩ tintee daŋ lɩɩ ŋ lɛ, dɩ kuoroo ma lɩɩ ŋ doho nɩaa tʋɔŋ.

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: Tintee-la mɩ sɩ joŋo pɩ Abɩraham arɩ Aɩzɩk, mɩ jaŋ joŋ ʋ tɩtɩa a pɩ ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa.”

ref: Jacob set up a large rock, so that he would remember what had happened there. Then he poured wine and olive oil on the rock to show that it was dedicated to God,
text: Jekɔb joŋ tabɩɩŋ pɛ ʋ nagɛ jebiiŋ lee-la ʋ dɩ Wɩa sɩ bʋl wɩaa a joŋ kʋnyʋalaa Wɩɩsʋlʋŋ kɩaa arɩ nʋʋŋ bɩsa wurisi.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Benjamin Is Born
text: Jekɔb hɛ lee-la ʋ dɩ Wɩa sɩ bʋl wɩaa yɩrɩŋ Bɛtɛl. Rakɛl arɩ Aɩzɩk sʋba

ref: Jacob and his family had left Bethel and were still a long way from Ephrath, when the time came for Rachel's baby to be born.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba lɩɩ Bɛtɛl lɛ a mʋmʋ a ka mʋa, ka dɩ ba yi Ɛfɩraf, Rakɛl chɩchɛ dɩ ʋ lʋl. Ʋ luoruŋ faasa wʋwɩɩ ʋ chaasa na hɛɛŋ.

ref: She was having a rough time, but the woman who was helping her said, “Don't worry! It's a boy.”
text: Bʋa-la lɛ ʋ sɩɩ na hɛɛŋ ŋɩɩ, hachagɩra bʋla pʋ, “Sɩ́ leŋe dɩ fawʋllʋŋ kɛnɩŋ, ŋ jaŋ bɩra lʋl hambolibie.”

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ lʋl bile a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Bɛŋ-Oni aŋ wiesi ʋ wiesi katɩɩ aŋ sʋʋ. Ka ʋ nyɩmma ma hɛ bile yɩrɩŋ Bɛnjamɩŋ.

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: Rakɛl sɩ sʋba, ba joŋu hugi Ɛfɩraf wombiiŋ .

ref: Jacob set up a tombstone over her grave, and it is still there.
text: Jekɔb saa jebiiŋ Rakɛl vaamɩŋ nyuŋ. Ʋ ŋaa magɩl a hɛ nɩmɛ arɩ jɩnɩŋ kala.

ref: Jacob, also known as Israel, traveled to the south of Eder Tower, where he set up camp.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb ba sɩ kaa yɩyɩrɛ Iziral bɩra sii a kieli Mɩgɩdal Ɛda a mʋ hɛ ʋ lɛbɩŋ.

ref: During their time there, Jacob's oldest son Reuben slept with Bilhah, who was one of Jacob's other wives. And Jacob found out about it. Jacob's Twelve Sons
text: Bʋa-la lɛ Iziral sɩ hɔŋ tintee-la lɛ, Rubiŋ jʋʋ kɛŋ ʋ nyɩmma haal-la ʋ sɩ bile aŋ bɩ jaa baa yɩrɛ Biliha a pɩŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Iziral nɩɩ wɩɩ-la. Jekɔb fa kɛŋ hambolibiisiŋ fii arɩ balɩa nɛ.

ref: Jacob went to his father Isaac at Hebron, also called Mamre or Kiriath-Arba, where Isaac's father Abraham had lived as a foreigner.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb mɩɩra kɔ dɩa ʋ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk teeŋ Mamɩrɛ lɛ, a kpagɛ Kɩrɩaf Aba . Lee-la nɛ Abɩraham arɩ Aɩzɩk fa hɔŋ.

ref: Esau, also known as Edom, had many descendants.
text: Ɛsɔ doho nɩaa nɛ ŋ-la:

ref: He married three Canaanite women: The first was Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite; the second was Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
text: Ɛsɔ jaa Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa toloo nɛ. Dɔŋɔ nɛ Ada, Hiti tɩŋŋaa Ɛlɔŋ tolo. Dɔŋɔ nɛ Oholibama, Ana tolo. Ʋ nɛ ha ŋaa Hiti tɩŋŋaa Zibioŋ nihiŋ.

ref: the third was Basemath, who was Ishmael's daughter and Nebaioth's sister.
text: Dɔŋɔ nɛ Basɩmaf, Ɩsɩmɛl tolo. Basɩmaf ŋaa Nɛbayɔf dɩhaala nɛ.

ref: Esau and his three wives had five sons while in Canaan. Adah's son was Eliphaz; Basemath's son was Reuel; Oholibamah's three sons were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Ɛsɔ arɩ Oholibama lʋl Jeyus, Jalam arɩ Kora. Ɛsɔ lʋl hambolibiisiŋ deemba Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ lɛ nɛ.

ref: Esau took his children and wives, his relatives and servants, his animals and possessions he had acquired while in Canaan, and moved far from Jacob.
text: Ɛsɔ sii kɛŋ ʋ haalaa, ʋ hambolibiisiŋ, ʋ toloo arɩ ʋ dɩa nɩaa kala a pɛ kɛsɛ ʋ pʋsʋŋ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala ʋ sɩ dʋa Kɛnaŋ lɛ, a lɩɩ ʋ naambie Jekɔb sɩ hɔŋ tintee-la lɛ a mʋ hɔŋ dɩ-bolii.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: Ɛsɔ arɩ Jekɔb dʋa fa yʋga nɛ woruŋ. Ba pʋsʋŋ pʋrɩgɛ tintee-la lɛ ba sɩ hɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ tɩɩ ba bɩ dɩ-bala hɔnɔ.

ref: That's why Esau made his home in the hill country of Seir.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ mʋ hɔŋ Sɛɛ tintee-la lɛ peelee sɩ yʋga.

ref: Esau and Adah's oldest son was Eliphaz, and the clans that descended from him were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
text: Nɩaa deemba nɛ dii kuoruŋ Ɛsɔ doho nɩaa tʋɔŋ: Ɛsɔ bii-hɩaŋ Ɛlɩfas hambolibiisiŋ nɛ Tɛmaŋ, Omaa, Zefo, Kenas,

ref: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. These and Esau's other descendants lived in the land of Edom.
text: Kora, Gatam arɩ Amalɛk. Kuoroo deemba ŋaa Ɛlɩfas doho nɩaa nɛ a lɩɩ Ɛdɔm. Ba ŋaa Ada nihee nɛ.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Basemath's son Reuel were Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
text: Ɛsɔ nanʋala Rɛwɛl ma hambolibiisiŋ sɩ dii kuoruŋ nɛ ŋ-la: Nahaf, Zɛra, Sama arɩ Mɩza. Rɛwɛl doho nɛ ŋɩɩ a lɩɩ Ɛdɔm tinteeŋ. Ɛsɔ haala Basɩmaf ma nihee nɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Ɛsɔ haala Oholibama Ana tolo biiriŋ sɩ dii kuoruŋ nɛ ŋ-la: Jeyus, Jalam arɩ Kora.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Ɛsɔ hambolibiisiŋ deemba nɛ dii kuoruŋ.

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Sɛɛ, Hori tɩɩna-la fa sɩ hɔŋ tintee-la lɛ hambolibiisiŋ nɛ ŋ-la: Lotaŋ, Sobal, Zibioŋ, Ana,

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lotaŋ ma hambolibiisiŋ nɛ Hori arɩ Homam. Lotaŋ dɩhaala nɛ Tɩmɩna.

ref: Shobal's sons were Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text: Sobal ma hambolibiisiŋ nɛ: Alɩvaŋ, Manahaf, Ɛbal, Sefo arɩ Onam.

ref: Zibeon's sons were Aiah and Anah—the same Anah who found an oasis in the desert while taking the donkeys of his father out to pasture.
text: Zibioŋ hambolibiisiŋ nɛ: Aya arɩ Ana. Ana deeŋ nɛ laa sɩpaaŋ na lɩɩ-bul-sɩɩŋ sɩ lɩma ŋɩɩ gɩrɩŋ lɛ, ʋ sɩ kɛŋ ʋ nyɩmma Zibioŋ kakusuŋ lɩɩ dɩ ba dii.

ref: Anah's children were Dishon and Oholibamah.
text: Ana nanʋala nɛ Dɩsɔŋ, ʋ tolo ma nɛ Oholibama.

ref: Dishon's sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text: Dɩsɔŋ ma hambolibiisiŋ nɛ: Hemidaŋ, Ɛsɩbaŋ, Ɩtɩraŋ arɩ Keraŋ.

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Ɛza hambolibiisiŋ nɛ: Bilihaŋ, Zaavaŋ arɩ Akaŋ.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Dɩsaŋ hambolibiisiŋ nɛ: Yus arɩ Araŋ.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Hori tɩŋŋaa kuoroo nɛ: Lotaŋ, Sobal, Zibioŋ, Ana,

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: Dɩsɔŋ, Ɛza arɩ Dɩsaŋ. Kuoroo deemba fa kɛŋ ba leree nɛ Sɛɛ tinteeŋ lɛ.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau took their names from their families and the places where they lived. They are Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
text: Kuoroo deemba nɛ lɩɩ Ɛsɔ doho tʋɔŋ. Ba doho nɩaa arɩ lee-la ba sɩ hɔnɔ hɔnɔ yɩraa nɛ: Tɩmɩna, Alɩva arɩ Jɛtɛf.

ref: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
text: Oholibama, Ɛla, Pɩnɔŋ,

ref: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
text: Kenas, Tɛmaŋ, Mibizaa,

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.
text: Magɩdɩɛl arɩ Ɩram. Ɛdɔm tinteeŋ kuoroo arɩ ba dɩ-hɔnɔɔ nɛ ŋɩɩ. Ɛsɔ nɛ ŋaa Ɛdɔm tɩŋŋaa kala nyɩmma.

ref: Jacob lived in the land of Canaan, where his father Isaac had lived,
text: Jekɔb hɔŋ Kɛnaŋ tinteeŋ lɛ nɛ, nɩmɛ nɛ ʋ nyɩmma ma fa hɔŋ.

ref: “They're not here anymore,” the man replied. “I overheard them say they were going to Dothan.” Joseph left and found his brothers in Dothan.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ baal-la bʋla pʋ, “Ba sʋrɩsɛ daha nɛ. Mɩ nɩa dɩ ba sɩ baa chɛ dɩ ba mʋ Dotaŋ nɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf kiri ba harɩŋ a mʋ naba Dotaŋ lɛ.

ref: Jacob loved Joseph more than he did any of his other sons, because Joseph was born when Jacob was very old. Jacob had even given Joseph a fancy coat
text: Iziral fa cho Josɛf nɛ a kɩɩ ʋ biiri-la kala, bɛɛ wɩaa ʋ fa hɩɛsa nɛ aŋ lʋlʋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ yɛrɛ gɛrɩ-zɔŋ kʋbala a pʋ.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: Josɛf naambiiriŋ sɩ na arɩ ba nyɩmma chou a kɩɩba, ba bɛrʋ a bɩ wʋzɔŋ kala bʋlaa pʋ.

ref: One day, Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed, and they hated him even more.
text: Tapʋlɩɩ kʋbala Josɛf pɩŋ doŋ a duosi a sii bʋla pɩ ʋ naambiiriŋ, ba wasa bɛrʋ woruŋ.

ref: Joseph said, “Let me tell you about my dream.
text: Ʋ bʋla pɩba, “Má jegile mɩ duoso-la mɩ sɩ duoso.

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: Mɩ dɩ ma nɛ kʋŋ mɩaa baga lɛ a vɔɔ hɩhɛ dɔŋɔ, ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ kʋa guu sii chɩŋ tegi tegi, ka ma kɩaa kʋ chɩna golli mɩ kʋa a kpirimi ʋ sɩpaaŋ.”

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ naambiiri-la pɩɛsʋ, “Ŋ saa bɩɩna ŋ nɛ jaŋ ŋaa la tʋɔŋ kuoro, koo ŋ nɛ jaŋ bibenla?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba wasa bɛrʋ woruŋ a tɩŋ duoso-la wɩaa ʋ sɩ bʋla pɩba.

ref: Joseph later had another dream, and he told his brothers, “Listen to what else I dreamed. The sun, the moon, and eleven stars bowed down to me.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf bɩra duosi, a bɩra bʋla pɩ ʋ naambiiriŋ. Ʋ sɩ, “Má jegile nɩɩ, mɩ bɩra duoso nɛ, lɛɛlɛ mɩ duoso a na arɩ wɩɩpɔsʋŋ arɩ chɛnɛ arɩ chɛnwʋlaa fii arɩ kʋbala, dɩ ba kala kpirimi mɩ sɩpaaŋ.”

ref: When he told his father about this dream, his father became angry and said, “What's that supposed to mean? Are your mother and I and your brothers all going to come and bow down to you?”
text: Ʋ sɩ bʋla pɩ ʋ nyɩmma, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ nyɩmma kpɩaʋ lɛ a bʋl, “Bɛɛ kʋŋ duoso nɛ ŋ duosi ŋɩɩ?” Mɩ dɩ ŋ naaŋ arɩ ŋ naambiiriŋ jaŋ sɛnɛ kʋ kpirimi tinteeŋ ŋ sɩpaaŋ?

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: Josɛf naambiiriŋ yaraa fa bibieri ʋ nyuŋ woruŋ, ka ʋ nyɩmma-na joŋ wɩɩ-la hɛ ʋ nyuŋ lɛ. Josɛf naambiiriŋ joŋu yallɛ

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf naambiiriŋ kɛŋ ba nyɩmma pʋsʋŋ kaa mʋ kʋdaalɩŋ a kpagɛ Sɛkɛm.

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Iziral bʋla pɩ Josɛf, “Ŋ naambiiriŋ mʋ kʋdaalɩŋ nɛ a kpagɛ Sɛkɛm, kɔ dɩ mɩ tɩmɩŋ ba teeŋ.” Josɛf sɛɩ.

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋla pʋ, “Mʋ na ŋ naambiiriŋ arɩ pʋsʋ-la, dɩ wɩɩ nɛ jʋʋba, ŋ mɩɩra kʋ bʋla pɩmɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ nyɩmma tɩmʋ ʋ lɩɩ Hɛbʋrɔŋ fuonaa-la lɛ a mʋ. Josɛf sɩ mʋ yi Sɛkɛm,

ref: and wandering through the fields, when a man asked, “What are you looking for?”
text: ŋɩɩ nɛ baal kʋbala naʋ dɩ ʋʋ gɔllɛ ŋmaŋkaa-la lɛ. Ʋ pɩɛsʋ, “Bɛɛ kʋŋ nɛ ŋ chɩchɛ?”

ref: Joseph answered, “I'm looking for my brothers who are watching the sheep. Can you tell me where they are?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋl, “Mɩɩ chɛ mɩ naambiiriŋ nɛ. Ŋ jaŋ wuo dagɛmɛ lee-la ba sɩ hɛ aa daa ba pʋsʋŋ?”

ref: But before he got there, they saw him coming and made plans to kill him.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ naambiiriŋ naʋ dɩ-bolii a vʋʋrɛ dɩ ba kpʋʋ, ka ʋ ha bɩ ba yie.

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Ba bʋla pɩ dɔŋɔ, “Na duduo-suru-la nɩɩ kɔ.”

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Ama Rubiŋ sɩ nɩɩ wɩɩ-la ba sɩ vʋʋra, ʋ chɩchɛ dɩ ʋ laa Josɛf ta ba nɩsɩŋ lɛ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋl, “Má sɩ́ leŋe la kpʋʋ.”

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Má sɩ́ leŋe dɩ chal kala jaasɛ. Má joŋu yuo hɛ viliŋ deeŋ tʋɔŋ gɩrɩŋ deeŋ lɛ, ama má sɩ́ leŋe la kpʋʋ. Rubiŋ faa bʋl wɩaa deemba kala aa chɛ ʋ laa Josɛf ta nɛ a kɛnʋ mɩɩra mʋ pɩ ʋ nyɩmma.

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Josɛf sɩ kʋ yi ʋ naambiiriŋ, ba gurigu a wuri ʋ gɛrɩ-zɔŋ-la ʋ fa sɩ laala.

ref: As Joseph's brothers sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were loaded with all kinds of spices that they were taking to Egypt.
text: Ba sɩ hɔnɔ dɩ ba dii kɩaa, ba na dɩ Ɩsɩmɛl doho nɩaa kɛŋ ba bʋntaŋŋaba kɩɛla to dɔŋɔ a lɩɩ Gɩlɩad tinteeŋ dɩɩrɩŋ a kʋkɔ. Ba bʋntaŋŋaba fa chʋŋ tegisiŋ nɛ, nʋʋ-sʋɔrʋsʋŋ, tɩɩ-kel-zɔŋŋɔɔ arɩ tɩɩ-nɛŋ-zɔŋŋɔɔ ba sɩ yie joŋo hɛ kʋdiilee lɛ ba sɩŋ a kaa mʋmʋ Ijipiti tinteeŋ.

ref: So Judah said, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his body?
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Juda bʋla pɩ ʋ naambiiriŋ “Bɛɛ nyʋʋrɩɩ nɛ la jaŋ na dɩ la nɛ kpʋ la naambie aŋ bʋl la bɩ ʋ sʋʋŋ lɛ jɩŋ?

ref: Let's sell him to the Ishmaelites and not harm him. After all, he is our brother.” And the others agreed.
text: Má leŋ la yallʋ pɩ Ɩsɩmɛl tɩŋŋaa aŋ sɩ́ wʋbɔŋ kala ŋaa ʋ lɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa ʋ ŋaa la naambii nɛ a ŋaa la tɩtɩa chalɩŋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba sɛɩ ʋ nii-la.

ref: When the Midianite merchants came by, Joseph's brothers took him out of the well, and for
text: Ɩsɩmɛl doho nɩaa bayalɩa-la sɩ kʋʋ bal, ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf naambiiriŋ kɛnʋ lɩɩsɛ vil-la tʋɔŋ a yallʋ warɩpʋla mara a pɩba ba kɛnʋ mʋ Ijipiti tinteeŋ.

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Rubiŋ sɩ mɩɩra kɔ vil-la leriŋ dɩ Josɛf tuo vil-la tʋɔŋ. Ʋ tʋɔŋ chei, ʋ kɛŋ ʋ gɛnnɩŋ kɩɛsɛ.

ref: Then he went back to his brothers and said, “The boy is gone! What am I going to do?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ mɩɩra mʋ ʋ naambiiriŋ teeŋ a bʋla pɩba, “Bile tuo vil-la tʋɔŋ, bɛɛ nɛ mɩ jaŋ ŋaa?”

ref: Joseph's brothers killed a goat and dipped Joseph's fancy coat in its blood.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba kpʋ bʋʋna a kɛŋ Josɛf gɛrɩŋ yuu ʋ chalɩŋ.

ref: After this, they took the coat to their father and said, “We found this! Look at it carefully and see if it belongs to your son.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba mʋ dɩa a joŋ gɛrɩ-la arɩ chal-la a kaa mʋ dagɛ ba nyɩmma aŋ bʋl, “Gɛrɩŋ deeŋ nɛ la na, bee na ŋ nanʋal-la gɛrɩŋ dee?”

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ beŋ aŋ bʋl, “Oo, ʋ nɛ. Gɩrɩŋ kʋŋ nɛ kɛnʋ chaŋ, kʋŋ nɛ sɛnɛ kɛŋ Josɛf pʋɔsɛ.”

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb kɛŋ ʋ gɛrɩŋ kɩɛsɛ a tɩŋ ʋ tʋɔŋ sɩ cheye aŋ paa tʋɔ-chei gɛnnɩŋ laalɛ aŋ yel ʋ nanʋala wɩaa tapʋl-daŋ.

ref: All of Jacob's children came to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. “No,” he said, “I will go to my grave, mourning for my son.” So Jacob kept on grieving.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hambolibiisiŋ arɩ ʋ toloo kala pʋ kenyiri dɩ ʋ leŋ, ama ʋ vɩa aŋ yiyel aŋ bʋl, “Mɩ jaŋ yel ŋɩɩ a mʋ sʋʋ a mʋ mɩ bie teeŋ, aŋ ha yiyel ʋ wɩaa.”

ref: Meanwhile, the Midianites had sold Joseph in Egypt to a man named Potiphar, who was the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Ɩsɩmɛl doho nɩaa kɛŋ Josɛf mʋ yalla pɩ Pɔtɩfa, Ijipiti tinteeŋ lɛ. Pɔtɩfa fa ŋaa laalɩyuoro-la sɩɩ pɔ Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro nɩhɩaŋ nɛ.

ref: About that time Judah left his brothers in the hill country and went to live near his friend Hirah in the town of Adullam.
text: Bʋa-la lɛ, Juda leŋ ʋ naambiiriŋ aŋ mʋ hɛ Adulam tɩɩna kʋbala teeŋ baa yɩrɛ Hira.

ref: While there he met the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite man. Judah married her,
text: Nɩmɛ nɛ Juda na Kɛnaŋ tɩŋŋaa tolo kʋbala, ʋ nyɩmma yɩrɩŋ nɛ Sʋa. Ʋ jaaʋ ba pɛ bɔsɔ.

ref: and they had three sons. He named the first one Er;
text: Ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie pʋ. Ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Ɛɛ.

ref: she named the next one Onan.
text: Haal-la bɩra kɛŋ luoruŋ a lʋl hambolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Onaŋ.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ ha bɩra lʋl hambolibie a hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Sɛla. Juda fa hɛ Kɛzɩb tinteeŋ lɛ nɛ, ka ba lʋl Sɛla.

ref: Later, Judah chose Tamar as a wife for Er, his oldest son.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Juda jaa haala pɩ ʋ bii-hɩaŋ Ɛɛ. Ʋ yɩrɩŋ nɛ Tama.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.
text: Ama Ɛɛ fa ŋaa nɩbɔŋ nɛ La TƖƖNA teeŋ, ŋɩɩ nɛ La TƖƖNA kpʋʋ.

ref: So Judah told Onan, “It's your duty to marry Tamar and have a child for your brother.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Juda bʋla pɩ Onaŋ, “Joŋ ŋ malɩma haala jaa dɩ ma lʋl biiriŋ a pɩ ŋ malɩma.”

ref: Onan knew the child would not be his, and when he had sex with Tamar, he made sure that she would not get pregnant.
text: Ama Onaŋ sɩ jɩma arɩ ʋ dɩ Tama nɛ lʋl biiriŋ bɩ jaŋ ŋaa ʋ kɩaa, dɩ ʋ dɩ haal-la nɛ pɛ bɔsɔ na-kala, ʋ yie fɩɩ bafɩɩrɩŋ tɩta tinteeŋ nɛ dɩ ʋ sɩ́ biiriŋ lʋla pɩ ʋ malɩma.

ref: The Lord wasn't pleased with Onan and took his life too.
text: La TƖƖNA na wɩɩ-la ʋ sɩ ŋaa, dɩ ʋ ŋaa wʋbɔŋ nɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ kpʋ ʋ ma.

ref: Judah did not want the same thing to happen to his son Shelah, and he told Tamar, “Go home to your father and live there as a widow until my son Shelah is grown.” So Tamar went to live with her father.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Juda bʋla pɩ ʋ bii-haala Tama, “Mʋ hɔŋ ŋ nyɩmma dɩa lɛ a ŋaa yo-haal, ka dɩ mɩ bie Sɛla waa.” Juda fa bɩɩna arɩ Sɛla ma nɛ jaaʋ, ʋ jaŋ sʋʋ arɩ ʋ malɩmaba sɩ sʋʋ ŋɩɩ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Tama mʋ hɔŋ ʋ nyɩmma dɩa lɛ.

ref: Some years later Judah's wife died, and he mourned for her. He then went with his friend Hirah to the town of Timnah, where his sheep were being sheared.
text: Ʋ sɩ diene kʋ magɛ ŋɩɩ, Juda haala sʋʋ. Ʋ fa ŋaa Sʋa tolo nɛ. Juda sɩ yel yoho dɛrɛ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ mʋ taŋ-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Tɩmɩna, baala-la sɩɩ kɛ ʋ pie-pʋnaa teeŋ, ʋ dɩ ʋ nandɔŋɔ Hira, Adulam tɩɩna-la.

ref: Tamar found out that her father-in-law Judah was going to Timnah to shear his sheep.
text: Ba sɩ bʋla pɩ Tama arɩ ʋ bala nyɩmma ka mʋ Tɩmɩna dɩ ʋ kɛ ʋ pie-pʋnaa,

ref: She also realized that Shelah was now a grown man, but she had not been allowed to marry him. So she decided to dress in something other than her widow's clothes and to cover her face with a veil. After this, she sat outside the town of Enaim on the road to Timnah.
text: ʋ sii wuri ʋ yoha-gɛnnɩŋ a bil aŋ joŋ nyu-bɛlɛ tɔ ʋ tɩtɩa, dɩ Juda sɩ́ ʋ jɩŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ aŋ lɩɩ hɔŋ wombii-la sɩɩ jʋʋ Enem a mʋmʋ Tɩmɩna, bɛɛ wɩaa ʋ na dɩ Sɛla waa nɛ, ka ba bɩ ʋ joŋo pʋ dɩ ʋ ŋaa ʋ bala.

ref: When Judah came along, he did not recognize her because of the veil. He thought she was a prostitute
text: Juda sɩ na dɩ Tama tɔ nyu-bɛlɛ ŋɩɩ, ʋ bɩɩnɛ arɩ hachɔrʋŋ nɛ.

ref: and asked her to sleep with him. She asked, “What will you give me if I do?”
text: Ʋ bɩ jɩŋ arɩ ʋ bii-haala nɛ ŋɩɩ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ mʋ ʋ teeŋ a bʋla pʋ, “Leŋ dɩ mɩ kɛnɩŋ pɩŋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ tolo-la pɩɛsɛ, “Bɛɛ kʋŋ nɛ ŋ jaŋ pɩmɛ?”

ref: “One of my young goats,” he answered. “What will you give me to keep until you send the goat?” she asked.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ sɩ, “Mɩ jaŋ lɩɩsɛ bʋ-bie mɩ bʋʋna tʋɔŋ a pɩŋ.” Tama bɩra pɩɛsɛ, “Bɛɛ kʋŋ nɛ ŋ jaŋ joŋo bil bʋbʋɔŋ aŋ-na kɛŋ bʋʋŋ-la kɔ?”

ref: “What do you want?” he asked in return. “The ring on that cord around your neck,” was her reply. “I also want the special walking stick you have with you.” He gave them to her, they slept together, and she became pregnant.
text: Juda pɩɛsɛ, “Bɛɛ kʋŋ nɛ ŋ chɩchɛ mɩ joŋo bil bʋbʋɔŋ?” Ʋ sɩ, “Lɩɩsɛ ŋ nenii-pɩrɩ-la ŋ yɩrɩŋ sɩ hɛ ʋ lɛ arɩ ʋ ŋmɛnɩŋ arɩ ŋ dantɩga a pɩmɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ joŋ ba kala pʋ aŋ kɛnʋ pɩŋ ʋ kɛŋ luoruŋ.

ref: After returning home, Tamar took off the veil and dressed in her widow's clothes again.
text: Tama sii mʋ puri ʋ nyu-bɛlɛ-la bil aŋ joŋ ʋ yoha-gɛnnɩŋ laalɛ.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Juda mʋ dɩa a kɛŋ bʋʋŋ-la pɩ ʋ nandɔŋɔ Adulam tɩɩna-la dɩ ʋ kaa mʋ pɩ haal-la aŋ laa kɩalɩŋ ʋ sɩ joŋo bil bʋbʋɔŋ. Ama ʋ sɩ mʋa ʋ bɩ ʋ na.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ pɩɛsɛ baala-la sɩ hɛ lee-la, “Nɩɩ nɛ hachɔrʋŋ-la fa sɩ hɔŋ Enem wombiiŋ niiŋ hɛ?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba bʋl, “Hachɔrʋŋ kala tuo daha.”

ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
text: Ʋ mɩɩra mʋ Juda teeŋ a bʋl, “Mɩ bɩ ʋ na, ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ pɩɛsɛ baala-la ma sɩ hɛ lee-la. Ba ma sɩ hachɔrʋŋ kala tuo daha.”

ref: “If you couldn't find her, we'll just let her keep the things I gave her,” Judah answered. “And we'd better forget about the goat, or else we'll look like fools.”
text: Juda bʋl, “Leŋ dɩ ʋ kɛŋ kɩalɩŋ, dɩ ŋɩɩ dee la hɩɩsɩŋ jaŋ lɩɩ dɩ nɩaa mʋmɔnla. Mɩ folo tɩmɩŋ ŋ kɛŋ bʋʋŋ-la mʋ dɩ ŋ puu, ama ŋ bɩ ʋ na.”

ref: About three months later someone told Judah, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has behaved like a prostitute, and now she's pregnant!” “Drag her out of town and burn her to death!” Judah shouted.
text: Chɛnsɩŋ batori sɩ kʋ bal, ba bʋla pɩ Juda, “Ŋ bii-haala Tama ŋaa hachɔrʋŋ wɩaa nɛ a kɛŋ luoruŋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Juda bʋl, “Má kɛnʋ kaa kʋ lɩɩ nyɩgɛ dɩ ʋ sʋʋ.”

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
text: Ba sɩ kɛnʋ kaa kʋ lɩɩ, ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ tɩma yɩrɛ ʋ bala nyɩmma a bʋl, “Baal-la sɩ tɩɩ kɩaa deemba nɛ hɛ luoruŋ deeŋ pɩmɛ. Bee na, ŋ jaŋ wuo jɩŋ nɩɩ-la sɩ tɩɩ nenii-pɩrɩŋ deeŋ sɩ kɛŋ yɩrɩŋ arɩ ʋ ŋmɛnɩŋ arɩ dantɩga deeŋ?”

ref: “Those are mine!” Judah admitted. “She's a better person than I am, because I broke my promise to let her marry my son Shelah.” After this, Judah never slept with her again.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Juda beŋ a jɩmba aŋ bʋl, “Ʋ nɛ kɛŋ wʋtɩtɩɩ a kɩɩmɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa mɩ nɛ vɩa dɩ mɩ joŋu pɩ mɩ bie Sɛla dɩ ʋ jaa.” A lɩɩ ŋɩɩ harɩŋ ʋ dɩ Juda bɩra bɩ bɔsɔ pɛ.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Ʋ sɩ kʋʋ lʋl, dɔŋɔ kɛŋ ʋ nɩsɩŋ kʋ lɩɩ, ŋɩɩ nɛ hachagɩra joŋ ŋmɛŋ-fɩaŋ a vɔɔ ʋ nɩsɩ-la lɛ aŋ bʋl, “Deeŋ nɛ laa sɩpaaŋ kʋ lɩɩ.”

ref: At once his hand went back in, and the other child was born first. The woman then said, “What an opening you've made for yourself!” So they named the baby Perez.
text: Ama bile kɛŋ ʋ nɩsɩŋ mɩɩra jʋʋ, ka dɔŋɔ-la mɩɩra laa sɩpaaŋ kʋ lɩɩ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ hachagɩrʋ-la bʋl, “Ɛɛ nɛ ŋ ŋaa kʋ lɩɩ?” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ba hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Pɛrɛs. Ʋ bʋbʋɔŋ nɛ gɩrɩma lɩɩ.

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ naambile ma ba fa sɩ joŋ ŋmɛŋ-fɩaŋ vɔɔ nɩsɩŋ ma kʋ lɩɩ. Ba hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Zɛra.

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Ɩsɩmɛl doho nɩalɩŋ sɩ yɔɔ Josɛf ʋ naambiiriŋ teeŋ kɛnʋ mʋ Ijipiti tinteeŋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ Pɔtɩfa sɩ ŋaa laalɩyuoro-la sɩɩ pɔ Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro nɩhɩaŋ laaʋ yɔɔ ba nɩsɩŋ lɛ.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: Ʋ kʋhɩaŋ na arɩ Wɩa chɩŋ Josɛf harɩŋ nɛ a leŋ ʋ wɩɩkala wʋwasɛ woruŋ.

ref: Potiphar liked Joseph and made him his personal assistant, putting him in charge of his house and all of his property.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Pɔtɩfa cho Josɛf wɩaa a leŋ ʋ ŋaa ʋ tɩntɩnnʋ, ʋ joŋ ʋ dɩa arɩ kʋŋkala ʋ sɩ kɛnɛ hɛ ʋ nɩsɩŋ lɛ ʋ bibeŋ.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Ʋ joŋ ʋ kʋŋkala hɛ Josɛf nɩsɩŋ lɛ aŋ ta ʋ jɩa. Ʋ kʋdiilee dʋndʋŋa wɩaa nɛ chɩlʋ. Josɛf fa waa nɛ woruŋ, a ŋaa bapʋɔsɛ-zɔŋ.

ref: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
text: Ʋ ŋɩɩ harɩŋ lɛ Pɔtɩfa haala suomo hɩhɩɛnɛ Josɛf a bʋla pʋ, “Kɛmmɛ pɩŋ.”

ref: but he refused and said, “My master isn't worried about anything in his house, because he has placed me in charge of everything he owns.
text: Ama Josɛf vɩa aŋ bʋl, “Mɩ kʋhɩaŋ joŋ ʋ dɩa arɩ kʋŋkala a hɛ mɩ nɩsɩŋ lɛ nɛ mɩ bibeŋ.

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: Nʋʋkala bɩ mɩ kɩɩ dɩa deeŋ lɛ. Ʋ bɩ kʋŋkala joŋo faamɛ, ŋ dʋndʋŋa nɛ ʋ bɩ joŋo hɛ mɩ nɩsɩŋ lɛ a tɩŋ ŋ sɩ ŋaa ʋ haala wɩaa. Ɛɛ nɛ saa mɩ jaŋ wuo ŋaa wʋbɔmʋŋ deeŋ a chei Wɩa tʋɔŋ?”

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: Arɩ Josɛf sɩ vɩa dɩ ʋ nɩɩ haal-la sɩ bʋl ŋɩɩ, a vɩa dɩ ʋ kɛnʋ pɩŋ, a paala bɩ chɛ dɩ ʋ kpagʋ, haal-la ha pɛsɛ Josɛf lɛ tapʋlɩɩ kala.

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Tapʋlɩɩ kʋbala dɩa-la tɩntɩnnaa kʋŋkala tuo, ka Josɛf jʋʋ dɩa-la dɩ ʋ tɩŋ ʋ tɩntɩŋŋaa.

ref: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ haal-la kɛŋ Josɛf gɛrɩŋ lɛ aŋ bʋl, “Kɛmmɛ pɩŋ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf leŋ ʋ gɛrɩŋ haal-la nɩsɩŋ lɛ aŋ fá lɩɩ.

ref: she called in her servants and said, “Look! This Hebrew has come just to make fools of us. He tried to rape me, but I screamed for help.
text: ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ yɩrɛ ʋ dɩa tɩntɩnna-la a bʋla pɩba, “Má na mɩ bala Pɔtɩfa kɛŋ Hiburu tɩɩna deeŋ kɔ dɩ ʋ hɩɩsɛla nɛ. Ʋ kʋ jʋʋ dɩ ʋ gurigimi nɛ mɩ bunsi.

ref: And when he heard me scream, he ran out of the house, leaving his coat with me.”
text: Ʋ sɩ nɩa mɩ bunso ŋɩɩ, ʋ fá kagɛ ʋ gɛrɩŋ lɛ.”

ref: Potiphar's wife kept Joseph's coat until her husband came home.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ joŋ gɛrɩ-la bil ʋ teeŋ ʋ bala kɔ.

ref: But when I screamed for help, he left his coat and ran out of the house.”
text: Ama mɩ sɩ bunso, ʋ fá lɩɩ kagɛ ʋ gɛrɩŋ lɛ.”

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf kʋhɩaŋ kɛnʋ mʋ tɔ kuoroo nɩtɔɔrɩ dɩa lɛ ʋ hɛ nɩmɛ.

ref: the Lord helped him and was good to him. He even made the jailer like Joseph so much that
text: Ama La TƖƖNA ha chɩŋ Josɛf harɩŋ nɛ a fá ʋ nisusuŋ a leŋ nɩtɔɔrɩ dɩa-la kʋhɩaŋ cho ʋ wɩaa.

ref: he put him in charge of the other prisoners and of everything that was done in the jail.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ nɩɩ-la sɩɩ beŋ nɩtɔɔrɩ dɩa la joŋ nɩalɩŋ kala ba sɩ kaa tɔ dɩa arɩ wɩɩ na-kala ba sɩɩ ŋaa lee-la a hɛ Josɛf nɩsɩŋ lɛ dɩ ʋ bibemba.

ref: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
text: Nɩtɔɔrɩ dɩa-la kʋhɩaŋ ma fa ta jɩa nɛ a mʋ tɩgɛ kʋŋ na-kala ʋ sɩ joŋo hɛ Josɛf nɩsɩŋ lɛ, bɛɛ wɩaa La TƖƖNA fa chɩŋ Josɛf harɩŋ nɛ a leŋ wɩɩ na-kala ʋ sɩɩ ŋaa vɩvɛŋ woruŋ.

ref: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
text: Nɩtɔɔrɩ dɩa-la kʋhɩaŋ ŋaa Josɛf tɩma pɩpɩ tɩtɩnnɩ-hɩasɩŋ deemba balɩa. Ba sɩ hɛ nɩmɛ tapʋlaa baŋmɛnɛ,

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: tapʋlɩɩ kʋbala Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro nyʋnyʋalɩ-koru-la arɩ nɩɩ-la sɩɩ wasɛ kuoro boroboro-la kala sɩ hɛ nɩtɔɔrɩ dɩa-la lɛ duosi duosoo dabamba tɩta-bala. Duoso kala kɛŋ ʋ bʋbʋa.

ref: The next morning, when Joseph went to see the men, he could tell they were upset,
text: Chʋchʋɔŋ Josɛf sɩ kɔ ba teeŋ, ʋ na arɩ ba tʋɔŋ cheye.

ref: and he asked, “Why are you so worried today?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf pɩɛsɛba, “Bɛɛ nɛ kɛmma ma tʋɔŋ chei jɩnɩŋ?”

ref: “We each had a dream last night,” they answered, “and there is no one to tell us what they mean.” Joseph replied, “Doesn't God know the meaning of dreams? Now tell me what you dreamed.”
text: Ba bʋla pʋ, “La duosi duosoo nɛ aŋ ka nʋʋkala tuo dɩ ʋ dagɛ duoso-la bʋbʋɔŋ pɩla.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf bʋl, “Wɩa nɛ jɩŋ duoso bʋbʋɔŋ. Má dagɛ ma duoso-la pɩmɛ.”

ref: The king's personal servant told Joseph, “In my dream I saw a vine
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ kuoro nyʋnyʋalɩ koruru-hɩaŋ-la bʋl ʋ duoso pɩ Josɛf a bʋl, “Mɩ duosoo tʋɔŋ mɩ na girep tɩa dɩ ʋ chɩŋ mɩ sɩpaaŋ.

ref: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
text: Tɩa-la kɛŋ naakelee batori, naakele-la pʋʋra fɩɩlɛ a nɛŋ nɛnɛɛ a bɩɩ.

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: Dɩ Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro nyʋnyʋala hɛ mɩ nɩsɩŋ lɛ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ mɩ chagɛ tɩɩnɛnɛ-la lɩɩŋ hɛ nyʋnyʋala-la lɛ a joŋo hɛ kuoro nɩsɩŋ lɛ.”

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf bʋla pʋ, “Ŋ duoso-la bʋbʋɔŋ nɛ ŋ-la: Tɩɩ-naakele-la batori ŋaa tapʋlaa batori nɛ ŋɩɩ.

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Dɩ tapʋlaa batori-la nɛ yie, Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro jaŋ lɩɩsɩŋ nɩtɔɔrɩ dɩa lɛ a bɩra leŋ dɩ ŋ tɩtɩŋ ŋ tɩntɩŋŋaa arɩ ŋ fa sɩɩ tɩŋ ŋɩɩ. Ŋ jaŋ bɩra joŋ ʋ nyʋnyʋala a pʋpʋ arɩ ŋ fa sɩɩ ŋaa ŋɩɩ.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Ama dɩ wɩaa nɛ vɛŋ woruŋ pɩŋ sɩ́ mɩ wɩaa yʋ, bʋl mɩ wɩaa pɩ Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro dɩ ʋ lɩɩsɛmɛ nɩtɔɔrɩ dɩa deeŋ lɛ.

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: Doluŋ nɛ ba dii mɩ lɛ a lɩɩsɛmɛ Hiburu tɩŋŋaa tinteeŋ lɛ a kaa kɔ daha. Mɩ sɩ hɛ daha ma, mɩ bɩ wɩɩ cheye, ka ba kɛmmɛ tɔ dɩa deeŋ lɛ.”

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Kuoro boroboro wasɩrʋ-hɩaŋ-la sɩ nɩa dɩ Josɛf dagɛ duoso-la bʋbʋɔŋ, ʋ sɩma pʋ. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋla pɩ Josɛf, “Mɩ ma duoso dɩ mɩ chʋŋ sinsiŋ batori mɩ nyuŋ lɛ.

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
text: Sime-la sɩ hɛ nyuŋ su arɩ boroboro dosunuŋ, mɩ kaa mʋ dɩ mɩ pɩ Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro, ka diibiisiŋ didii boroboro-la mɩ sɩ chʋŋa.”

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Josɛf bʋl, “Ŋ duoso-la bʋbʋɔŋ nɛ ŋ-la: Sinsiŋ batori-la ŋaa tapʋlaa batori nɛ.

ref: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
text: Tapʋlaa batori-la nɛ yie, Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro jaŋ leŋ dɩ ba lɩɩsɩŋ a keri ŋ nyuŋ aŋ joŋuŋ sagɛ tɩa nyuŋ dɩ diibiisiŋ dii ŋ nammɩa.”

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Tapʋlaa batori-la yie dɩ Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro tapʋl-lʋlʋŋ ma nɛ ŋɩɩ. Ʋ ŋaa ba ŋaa kʋdiilee pɩ ʋ tɩntɩnnɩ-hɩasɩŋ kala ba dii aŋ gbigbieli. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ chaarɛ ʋ nyʋnyʋalɩ koruru-hɩaŋ-la arɩ boroboro wasɩrʋ-hɩaŋ-la nɩtɔɔrɩ-dɩa lɛ a kaa kʋ lɩɩ ʋ tɩntɩnnɩ-hɩasɩŋ kala sɩpaaŋ.

ref: He put the personal servant back in his old job
text: Ʋ leŋ ba bɩra joŋ ʋ nyʋnyʋalɩ koruru-hɩaŋ-la nyʋnyʋala pʋ ʋ bɩra tɩtɩŋ ʋ tɩntɩŋŋaa,

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: ama ʋ leŋ ba lieŋi ʋ boroboro wasɩrʋ-hɩaŋ-la, arɩ Josɛf fa sɩ bʋl ŋɩɩ pʋ.

ref: but the king's personal servant completely forgot about Joseph.
text: Ama kuoro nyʋnyʋalɩ kokuru-hɩaŋ-la yʋ dɩ ʋ bʋl Josɛf wɩaa a pɩ Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro.

ref: Two years later the king of Egypt dreamed he was standing beside the Nile River.
text: Jɩsɩŋ balɩa harɩŋ, Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro duosi. Ʋ duoso arɩ ʋ chɩŋ fuo-la ba sɩɩ yɩrɛ Naayɩl niiŋ,

ref: Suddenly, seven fat, healthy cows came up from the river and started eating grass along the bank.
text: dɩ nɛ-duoruŋ balɩpɛ lɩɩ fuo-la lɛ a didii yaŋ.

ref: Then seven ugly, skinny cows came up out of the river and
text: Nɛ-kɔnnʋŋ balɩpɛ ma lɩɩ fuo-la lɛ a kʋ lɩɩ chɩŋ a kpagɛ nɛ-duoru-la sɩ chɩŋ fuo-la niiŋ.

ref: ate the fat, healthy cows. When this happened, the king woke up.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ nɛ-kɔnnʋ-la paa nɛ-duoru-la balɩpɛ a liŋ, Ijipiti tinteeŋ kuoro hirige sii doŋ lɛ.

ref: The king went back to sleep and had another dream. This time seven full heads of grain were growing on a single stalk.
text: Ʋ bɩra pɩŋ doŋ a bɩra duosi a na kuorimii-daa-bala pagɩlɛ pagɩlɩ-duoruŋ balɩpɛ.
